pitch.title: Open House
pitch.logline: A ruthless real estate agent discovers her latest luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity that feeds on potential buyers, forcing her to market the deadly property to the worst people she knows to satisfy its hunger.
pitch.core_theme: The moral bankruptcy of corporate greed and the modern housing crisis.
pitch.synopsis:
Act I: Valerie is a cutthroat realtor on the verge of bankruptcy. She lands the exclusive listing for a stunning, modernist mansion. During the first showing, the house literally consumes a wealthy, obnoxious client. Instead of calling the police, Valerie realizes the house produces pristine, blood-free diamonds after every meal.

Act II: Valerie strikes a dark pact with the house. She begins hosting exclusive, invite-only open houses for corrupt politicians, rival cutthroat realtors, and toxic tech bros. The house gets fed, and Valerie gets rich. However, the house's appetite grows exponentially, and it begins demanding more innocent souls, trapping Valerie inside during a massive gala.

Act III: Realizing she has become the ultimate monster, Valerie decides to destroy the property. She locks herself in with her most ruthless corporate boss and triggers a gas leak. In a chaotic, bloody finale, the house tries to digest them both, but Valerie ignites the gas, blowing the mansion to kingdom come and ending the cycle of greed.

beats.description: Valerie is a cutthroat but broke realtor facing imminent bankruptcy and eviction.
She lands a miracle exclusive listing for a stunning modernist mansion but immediately senses a dark and eerie presence in the architecture.
Valerie hosts a private showing for an obnoxious wealthy client and ignores the warning signs to close the deal.
The house literally consumes the client and spits out a pristine diamond. Therefore Valerie chooses profit over morality and strikes a silent pact to keep feeding the entity.
Valerie curates a VIP list of corrupt politicians and rival realtors luring them to private showings under the guise of exclusivity.
The house devours the terrible people one by one. Therefore Valerie collects the diamonds and quickly amasses immense wealth and status.
Valerie enjoys her lavish lifestyle but the house begins acting erratic and violently hungry between scheduled showings.
The house rejects a vile billionaire and demands a pure innocent soul instead. Therefore Valerie realizes she is no longer the one in charge of the transaction.
Valerie tries to appease the house with lesser evils but it begins locking doors and physically threatening her.
Her ruthless corporate boss demands a massive open house gala at the property. Therefore Valerie is forced to bring a crowd of innocent staff and guests into the beast.
During the gala the house seals all exits and begins a mass feeding frenzy targeting the innocent caterers and guests.
Valerie watches the slaughter and realizes her own greed created this monster. Therefore she decides she must destroy her prized listing to save the survivors.
Valerie saves a few innocent guests and lures her wicked boss into the basement to distract the entity.
She begins opening the gas lines but the house realizes her plan and manipulates its walls and floors to attack her directly.
The house attempts to digest Valerie and her boss. Valerie sacrifices her diamond fortune to jam the crushing mechanism and sparks a lighter.
The mansion explodes in a fiery spectacle. A bruised Valerie walks away from the rubble broke but finally free of her corporate greed.

characters.name: Valerie Vance
characters.brief_summary: A cutthroat, financially drowning real estate agent who becomes a literal monster's middleman. She drives the plot by feeding bad clients to a sentient house in exchange for blood-free diamonds.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: Grew up in extreme poverty and was traumatically evicted from her childhood home while her parents begged the landlord for mercy.
psychological_core.the_lie: Wealth is the absolute only shield against victimization; if I am rich enough, no one can ever hurt, control, or humiliate me again.
psychological_core.fear: Being poor, powerless, and evicted, returning to the pathetic state of her childhood.
psychological_core.desire: To sell the modernist mansion, secure the massive commission, and achieve an unbreakable financial future.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To realize her self-worth and safety are not inherently tied to her bank account or social status.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop treating other human beings as disposable stepping stones for her own financial security.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Sharp, transactional, and heavily reliant on real estate buzzwords to sanitize grim realities. She NEVER says the words 'I cannot afford this.'
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Pivots the conversation to the aesthetic, architectural, or financial value of a nearby object.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A ruthless, people-using capitalist who meticulously rescues and fosters injured stray insects she finds in her properties.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: She masks her desperation 90 percent of the time with a flawless, icy professional veneer. Under extreme stress, her mask slips and she obsessively scrubs at invisible stains on her expensive cuffs.

characters.name: Richard Sterling
characters.brief_summary: Valerie's predatory corporate boss who views the housing crisis as an all-you-can-eat buffet. He attempts to steal the deadly listing to weaponize its diamond-producing hunger for his own firm.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: Inherited his father's agency but bankrupted it early on due to incompetence, spending years clawing back power through sheer ruthlessness to erase the shame.
psychological_core.the_lie: Empathy is a liability; the world is a zero-sum game where you are either the predator or the prey.
psychological_core.fear: Being exposed as a fraud who did not actually earn his success or power.
psychological_core.desire: To take over the exclusive mansion listing from Valerie and claim the massive prestige and wealth for himself.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To accept that his father's legacy does not define his worth as a man.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop exploiting his employees and clients to feed his bottomless, fragile ego.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in predatory, pseudo-philosophical metaphors about nature and business. He NEVER apologizes or admits he does not understand something.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Threatens the other person's job or social status with a calm, unnerving smile.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A hyper-masculine, ruthless corporate shark who is deeply and genuinely obsessed with restoring antique dollhouses.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: He projects unbothered dominance 90 percent of the time. When truly threatened, his sophisticated facade breaks and he physically invades personal space, whispering his threats through clenched teeth.

characters.name: Toby Higgins
characters.brief_summary: Valerie's earnest, deeply indebted assistant who genuinely believes in helping people find homes. He acts as the moral compass, eventually becoming the pure soul the house demands to eat.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: His family lost their home to a predatory loan when he was a teen, making him vow to help people find safe, honest housing.
psychological_core.the_lie: If I play by the rules and work hard within the system, the system will reward me and protect the innocent.
psychological_core.fear: That the world is inherently unfair and his relentless optimism is just foolish naivete.
psychological_core.desire: To earn his real estate license and close his first ethical, honest starter-home sale.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To accept that the system is broken and requires active disruption, not just passive, polite participation.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop enabling Valerie's toxic behavior out of a misplaced sense of loyalty and subservience.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Earnest, slightly overly formal, and asks too many clarifying questions. He NEVER uses cynical industry slang.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Offers to get coffee or physically organizes objects on a desk to avoid eye contact.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A naive, soft-spoken pushover who is an absolute savant at reading complex architectural blueprints and structural engineering anomalies.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: He hides his deep financial panic behind relentless, cheerful optimism 90 percent of the time. Under stress, he develops a severe stutter and compulsively organizes objects into perfect right angles.

characters.name: Gemma Frost
characters.brief_summary: A hyper-competitive rival realtor and Valerie's former best friend. She constantly forces Valerie to escalate her lies and body count to protect the exclusive listing from being stolen.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: Was Valerie's best friend and mentor until Valerie stole a massive commission from her, leaving her with deep trust issues and a thirst for vengeance.
psychological_core.the_lie: Success is the only valid form of revenge; whoever dies with the most toys wins the argument.
psychological_core.fear: Being forgotten, irrelevant, or forever seen as second-best to Valerie.
psychological_core.desire: To sabotage Valerie's exclusive listing and steal the mansion's wealthy clientele for herself.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To let go of her bitter obsession with Valerie and find her own independent path to happiness.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop tearing down other women in the industry just to inflate her own wounded ego.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Passive-aggressive, dripping with fake sweetness and backhanded remarks. She NEVER gives a genuine, strings-free compliment.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Checks her reflection in her phone screen or slowly reapplies lip gloss while the other person is talking.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A superficial, backstabbing mean-girl who volunteers every weekend at a gritty soup kitchen, keeping it a total secret from her colleagues.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: She masks her deep insecurities with designer clothes and a bubbly laugh 90 percent of the time. Under stress, she aggressively picks at her own cuticles until they bleed.

characters.name: Arthur Pendelton
characters.brief_summary: An obnoxious, newly-wealthy tech bro desperate to buy a personality through real estate. He serves as the initial victim, demonstrating the house's power and tempting Valerie into her dark pact.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: Grew up as a sickly, ignored middle child in a massive family, leading to a desperate, lifelong need to take up physical and financial space.
psychological_core.the_lie: If I buy the biggest, most expensive things, people will finally have to respect and notice me.
psychological_core.fear: Being insignificant, invisible, or swallowed up by the crowd.
psychological_core.desire: To purchase the modernist mansion to use as a hollow status symbol for his obnoxious, loud parties.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To realize that buying expensive things will not heal his profound, lingering loneliness.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop treating service workers and agents like personal property he has purchased.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Loud, booming, and interrupts constantly. Drops names of obscure luxury brands. He NEVER asks about someone else's day.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Laughs obnoxiously loud and offers to buy the person a ridiculously expensive drink.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A boorish, uncultured tech-bro billionaire who possesses a refined, world-class palate for playing classical piano.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: He projects overwhelming, loud confidence to hide his imposter syndrome 90 percent of the time. Under stress, his booming voice cracks and he obsessively checks his luxury watch without actually reading the time.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Establishes the Sin (greed) that will eventually invite the wrath of the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Cold detachment and moral bankruptcy.
beats.pacing_notes: Sharp, aggressive, and highly transactional to establish the protagonist's ruthless baseline.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie Vance, impeccably dressed and devoid of empathy, oversees the eviction of a struggling family from a modest home to secure a meager commission. She ignores the tears of the former owners, aggressively pivoting the conversation to market comps and property values when confronted. This establishes her absolute moral bankruptcy and her deeply ingrained false belief that wealth is the only shield against victimization.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Highlights the moral failing and false belief that the Monster will exploit.
beats.emotional_arc: Annoyance masking a deep-seated fear of vulnerability.
beats.pacing_notes: A brief, dialogue-heavy pause that grounds the thematic argument of the film.
beats.detailed_action: Back at the luxury brokerage, Valerie's overworked and highly empathetic assistant, Toby Aris, timidly suggests that human beings have inherent value beyond their financial utility. Valerie immediately shuts him down with a barrage of corporate buzzwords, asserting that morality is a luxury only the ultra-rich can afford. This exchange plants the central thematic question of whether human life is worth more than capital, highlighting the systemic rot of corporate greed.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist commits the Sin out of desperation, setting her on a collision course with the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Feral panic hidden beneath a polished, professional exterior.
beats.pacing_notes: Mounting tension as the protagonist's safe world threatens to collapse.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie is called into the glass-walled office of her sociopathic boss, Richard Sterling, who calmly informs her that her secret, mounting debts have become a liability and she is on the verge of termination. Desperate to maintain her armor of wealth, Valerie discovers a scandalous secret about a rival agent and ruthlessly blackmails them to steal the agency's crown jewel. She secures the exclusive listing for a bizarre, stunning modernist mansion, setting the stage for her descent into literal and figurative darkness.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster reveals itself within the House, disrupting the protagonist's reality.
beats.emotional_arc: Absolute shock and primal terror.
beats.pacing_notes: Sudden, violent escalation that shatters the grounded corporate reality.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie hosts a private showing at the modernist mansion for an obnoxiously wealthy, emotionally abusive client who relentlessly insults the property and threatens to ruin Valerie's career. Without warning, the sleek hardwood floorboards liquefy into a fleshy maw, swallowing the screaming client whole in a gruesome, silent gulp. Valerie is thrown into a state of absolute shock, realizing her luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist rationalizes the Sin, debating whether to flee the House or feed the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Moral hesitation overridden by desperate greed.
beats.pacing_notes: Suspenseful, ticking-clock hesitation as a life-altering choice is weighed.
beats.detailed_action: Trembling with panic, Valerie pulls out her phone and prepares to dial the police to report the monstrous event. However, before she can make the call, the house shudders and regurgitates a pristine, blood-free diamond onto the floor. Valerie stares at the gemstone, mentally weighing her crushing financial ruin against the horrific murder she just witnessed, wrestling with the ultimate moral compromise.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist fully commits to the Sin, actively choosing to co-exist with the Monster in the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Cold resolve and the crossing of a moral point of no return.
beats.pacing_notes: Deliberate, heavy, and final beat that locks the narrative into the second act.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie slowly lowers her phone, picks up the diamond, and wipes it clean, making a dark, unspoken pact with the entity. She chooses to keep quiet, actively stepping into the upside-down world of being a monster's caretaker to solve her financial woes. The sin of greed is fully embraced, locking her into the literal and figurative Monster in the House dynamic.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Introduces the antithesis of the Sin (empathy) through a character who represents salvation.
beats.emotional_arc: Guilt masked by transactional generosity and false confidence.
beats.pacing_notes: Warm but fundamentally uneasy, providing a breather before the carnage.
beats.detailed_action: Flush with cash from her first diamond, Valerie pays off her debts and interacts with Toby, who is thrilled by her sudden success and hopes she will finally fund his dream of a non-profit affordable housing trust. Valerie uses Toby's deep knowledge of the city's corrupt elites to unwittingly curate a hit list of terrible clients, masking her predatory intentions as ambitious networking. Toby acts as her moral anchor, constantly reminding the audience of the humanity Valerie is sacrificing to feed her own ambition.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Sin seems to pay off as the Monster consumes the wicked, fulfilling the promise of the premise.
beats.emotional_arc: Giddy, sociopathic triumph and intoxicating power.
beats.pacing_notes: Fast, rhythmic, darkly comedic montage of escalating horror and wealth.
beats.detailed_action: A darkly comedic montage unfolds as Valerie hosts a series of exclusive, invite-only open houses for the city's worst elites, including corrupt politicians like Councilwoman Rostova and toxic tech bros. The house devours these terrible clients one by one in creatively horrific ways, turning modernist architecture into deadly, shifting traps. Valerie collects a fortune in regurgitated diamonds, ascending to the very top of her brokerage while gleefully ignoring the mounting moral toll of her actions.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster grows too powerful to control; the Sin officially traps the protagonist inside the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Sudden shift from arrogant control to terrifying captivity.
beats.pacing_notes: An abrupt halt to the fun and games, spiking anxiety and raising the stakes.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie revels in her lavish new lifestyle, believing she has completely mastered the beast and achieved a false victory of ultimate wealth. However, during a routine cleaning, the house begins to rumble, crack, and bleed, unsatisfied with the meager portions of single clients. It violently seals the doors, holding Valerie hostage until she promises to organize a massive, invite-only gala to feed it a buffet of corporate scumbags, proving she is the pet, not the master.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster turns on everyone; the House drops its disguise and becomes a literal death trap.
beats.emotional_arc: Claustrophobic dread and the total loss of control.
beats.pacing_notes: Accelerating chaos as multiple threats converge on the protagonist simultaneously.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie packs the mansion with rival realtors, wealthy criminals, and her awful boss, Richard Sterling, desperately trying to orchestrate a controlled feeding to appease the entity. The sleazy rival realtor, Julian Vance, becomes suspicious of Valerie's success and begins snooping around the property, threatening to expose her dark secret to the world. The house loses its patience with the slow drip of victims, dropping its architectural facade to reveal shifting, fleshy walls and sealing all the exits to begin a mass consumption.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster threatens the innocent; the true, devastating cost of the Sin is revealed.
beats.emotional_arc: Crushing guilt and the death of the ego.
beats.pacing_notes: Frantic, breathless survival mode that culminates in a devastating realization.
beats.detailed_action: As the modernist mansion transforms into a deadly labyrinth of teeth and crushing walls, Valerie tries to navigate the chaos, arrogantly assuming the house will spare its provider. To her absolute horror, the entity ignores the corrupt elites and turns its insatiable hunger toward the innocent catering staff and her fiercely loyal assistant, Toby. Valerie experiences the whiff of death as she realizes her unmitigated greed has unleashed an unstoppable force that is about to slaughter the only decent person in her life.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist must choose between continuing the Sin or saving her soul by fighting the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Agonizing self-reflection and absolute despair.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, heavy, and intensely intimate amidst the surrounding carnage.
beats.detailed_action: The house corners Valerie and Toby on a collapsing staircase, opening a gaping, acidic maw directly beneath them. The entity telepathically demands that Valerie push her innocent assistant into the pit to save herself and continue their lucrative partnership. Staring into Toby's terrified eyes, Valerie is forced to confront her deepest psychological wound, finally realizing that her pursuit of wealth has turned her into the exact same predatory monster as her boss, Richard.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist repents of the Sin and decides to destroy the Monster and the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Righteous defiance and the spark of redemption.
beats.pacing_notes: Explosive burst of proactive energy as the protagonist finally takes a moral stand.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie violently rejects the house's demand, grabbing Toby by the collar and pulling him back from the edge of the maw. She loudly declares that she is breaking the lease, actively choosing human life over infinite wealth and severing her symbiotic pact with the monster. Armed with her intimate knowledge of the mansion's floor plan, she takes charge to lead Toby and the surviving innocent staff toward a structural weak point in the basement.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The ultimate battle against the Monster and the physical embodiment of the Sin (Richard) within the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Cathartic release of rage against the corrupt system.
beats.pacing_notes: High-octane, brutal, and explosive climax.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie successfully guides the innocent staff to an escape vent, but Richard Sterling intervenes, realizing the house's nature and attempting to sacrifice Valerie to secure the diamond-producing property for himself. A brutal, bloody fistfight erupts between Valerie and her sociopathic boss in the mansion's mechanical room while the house's walls literally close in to crush them both. Valerie overpowers Richard, uses a heavy wrench to smash the main gas line, sparks a lighter, and dives into a reinforced panic room just as the entity lunges for her.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Sin is permanently rejected; the Monster and House are destroyed, leaving a changed protagonist.
beats.emotional_arc: Peaceful liberation and spiritual rebirth.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, quiet, and resolute, contrasting sharply with the opening image.
beats.detailed_action: The ignited gas causes a massive explosion, blowing the monstrous mansion and Richard Sterling to kingdom come in a spectacular shower of debris. Valerie emerges from the smoldering crater, battered, bloody, but entirely alive and free from her debts. She spots a single, massive diamond resting in the ashes, picks it up for a fleeting moment, and then intentionally tosses it back into the rubble before walking away with Toby, finally ending the cycle of greed.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Establishes the 'sin' of the protagonist—her ruthless greed and lack of empathy—before introducing the physical confined space of the monster.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From detached authority to aggressive callousness. (><) Valerie wants to execute the eviction efficiently vs. the mother pleading for mercy.
beats.pacing_notes: Cold, methodical, and unyielding, emphasizing the brutal reality of her worldview before transitioning to the sterile corporate environment.
beats.detailed_action: Movers ruthlessly haul cardboard boxes and worn furniture onto the curb of a modest suburban home. Valerie Vance stands perfectly still on the manicured lawn, checking her luxury watch with icy detachment. When a tearful mother clutching a toddler approaches to plead for more time, Valerie doesn't flinch or offer a shred of sympathy. Instead, she aggressively steps past the devastated mother, pointing at the home's facade and loudly lecturing the eviction crew about market comps and necessary cosmetic renovations.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The moral argument is presented by the 'pure' character, highlighting the thematic sin (valuing capital over humanity) that will awaken the monster.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From anxious pleading to suffocating dominance. (><) Toby wants Valerie to recognize human value vs. Valerie's ruthless adherence to wealth as armor.
beats.pacing_notes: Brisk, walking-and-talking rhythm that abruptly halts into an uncomfortably intimate, tense confrontation.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie strides through the sterile, glass-and-steel luxury brokerage bullpen like a predator on the prowl. Toby Aris scurries behind her, nervously adjusting his glasses and aggressively organizing a stack of listing agreements to manage his anxiety. Toby stammers out a desperate plea about the evicted family's inherent value, arguing that people matter more than property. Valerie stops dead, invading Toby's personal space to deliver a suffocating barrage of corporate buzzwords, whispering sharply that morality is a luxury only the ultra-rich can afford.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Introduces the ordinary world's ultimate threat, establishing the desperation that will drive the protagonist into the monster's lair.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From composed arrogance to breathless, white-knuckled panic. (><) Valerie wants to maintain her wealthy facade vs. Richard exposing her financial ruin.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, deliberate, and cavernous, building a suffocating tension as the protagonist's armor is systematically dismantled.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie is summoned into a cavernous, glass-walled office where Richard Sterling stands at the window, calmly pouring a scotch. He doesn't even look at her as he begins listing her secret, mounting personal debts, systematically stripping away her wealthy armor. Valerie's composed veneer shatters; her breathing hitches, she obsessively rubs her blazer cuff, and her hands white-knuckle the back of a leather chair. Richard finally turns, dead-eyed, and delivers his ultimate threat: she is a financial liability on the verge of termination, starting a ticking clock on her total ruin.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The introduction of the 'Monster' and the 'House' simultaneously, disguised as a miraculous salvation.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From hopeless desperation to awe-struck dread. (><) Valerie wants to appraise the house vs. the house's subtle, predatory sentience.
beats.pacing_notes: Eerie and atmospheric, slowing down to a creeping dread as the supernatural elements subtly manifest.
beats.detailed_action: Following a cryptic lead, Valerie drives to an isolated, stunning modernist mansion that has miraculously fallen into her exclusive portfolio. As she unlocks the grand front doors, the temperature plummets and the architectural lines of the hallway seem to subtly shift, creating an optical illusion of a closing throat. She senses a dark, predatory presence vibrating in the floorboards, but she forcefully pushes her instincts aside, calculating the massive commission.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist ignores the classic horror red flags, actively choosing to enter the monster's lair due to her fatal flaw.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From hesitant anxiety to aggressive salesmanship. (><) Valerie wants to close the deal vs. Arthur's obnoxious behavior and the house's warning signs.
beats.pacing_notes: Tense and erratic, alternating between obnoxious comedic dialogue and sudden, silent environmental threats.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie brings Arthur Pendelton, a loud and obnoxious tech bro, for a private showing of the property. Arthur booms through the halls, constantly interrupting Valerie and obsessively checking his luxury watch while insulting the craftsmanship. Doors mysteriously slam shut behind them and the hardwood floors groan hungrily, leaving Valerie paralyzed for a moment, debating whether to flee the terrifying structure or push through her terror to secure the life-saving sale.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist commits the ultimate 'sin' of the genre, actively aligning with the monster for personal gain.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From sheer terror to cold, calculating greed. (><) Valerie wants the diamond vs. her own basic human morality.
beats.pacing_notes: Violent, shocking explosion of action followed by a dead-silent, chilling moment of decision.
beats.detailed_action: While Arthur leans against a load-bearing wall to mock the lighting, the wall suddenly liquefies into a fleshy, jagged maw and violently pulls him inside, digesting him in seconds. Valerie screams and scrambles for the exit, but stops dead when the house violently spits out a massive, pristine, blood-free diamond onto the floor. Scrubbing her cuffs with trembling hands, she stares at the gem, makes a silent, dark pact with the entity, and pockets the diamond instead of calling the police.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Introduces the moral counterweight to the protagonist's sin, someone who represents the innocence the monster will eventually crave.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From isolated guilt to manipulative partnership. (><) Valerie wants to use Toby's skills vs. Toby's desire for an ethical business practice.
beats.pacing_notes: Light, brisk, and deceptively cheerful, providing a breather from the horror.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie returns to the office flush with cash and enlists Toby to act as the exclusive concierge for her new 'invite-only' property viewings. Toby, hiding his own deep financial panic behind relentless optimism, eagerly agrees, believing this is his chance to learn ethical real estate from a newly successful mentor. He begins organizing the VIP guest list into perfect right angles on his desk, completely unaware that he is actually curating a menu for a supernatural predator.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Delivers the promise of the premise: a horror-comedy montage of terrible people meeting gruesome, architectural ends.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From nervous complicity to euphoric invincibility. (><) Valerie wants to feed the house smoothly vs. the victims' attempts to survive.
beats.pacing_notes: Upbeat, kinetic, and darkly comedic, moving at a rapid clip as the body count and bank account rise.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie curates a VIP list of corrupt politicians, predatory landlords, and toxic influencers, luring them to private showings under the guise of ultimate exclusivity. In a series of darkly comedic set pieces, the house creatively devours the terrible people—crushing a slumlord in a shrinking elevator and drowning a corrupt mayor in a sentient infinity pool. Valerie calmly collects a growing pile of pristine diamonds after each meal, quickly amassing immense wealth, buying designer clothes, and finally paying off her massive debts.

beats.genre_variation_notes: A false victory where the monster changes the rules, shifting from a controllable tool to an uncontrollable threat.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From arrogant control to sudden, sickening realization. (><) Valerie wants to maintain the transactional status quo vs. the house demanding pure innocence.
beats.pacing_notes: Confident and smooth, abruptly crashing into a jarring, violent halt that raises the stakes.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie confidently brings a vile, elderly billionaire into the foyer, expecting another easy diamond and a massive payday. Instead of consuming him, the house violently regurgitates the billionaire across the marble floor, slamming all the exterior doors shut and trapping Valerie inside. The walls pulsate, bleeding a clear, sap-like substance that forms the name 'TOBY' on the floorboards, making Valerie realize with absolute horror that she is no longer in charge of the transaction.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The monster turns on the protagonist, while external human antagonists simultaneously tighten the noose.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From desperate appeasement to cornered panic. (><) Valerie wants to satisfy the house with lesser evils vs. Richard demanding access to the property.
beats.pacing_notes: Frantic and suffocating, with the pressure mounting from both supernatural and corporate forces.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie frantically tries to appease the house by dragging in rival cutthroat realtors, including Gemma, but the house refuses to eat them, instead shifting its hallways to physically strike Valerie with flying debris. Back at the office, Richard Sterling notices the string of missing high-profile clients and corners Valerie, physically invading her space to whisper a terrifying ultimatum. He demands she host a massive, public open house gala at the modernist mansion that very night, threatening to seize the listing and fire her if she refuses.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The confined space is fully locked down, trapping the innocent victims inside the monster's belly.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From reluctant obedience to total loss of control. (><) Valerie wants to cancel the event vs. Richard forcing the gala to proceed.
beats.pacing_notes: Tense buildup of dread as the crowd gathers, accelerating into sheer chaos as the trap snaps shut.
beats.detailed_action: Against her will, Valerie stands at the entrance of the mansion as Richard forces his way inside, bringing a massive crowd of innocent caterers, staff, and wealthy guests. Toby arrives in a tuxedo, nervously stuttering as he tries to manage the coats, completely unaware of the danger pulsating in the walls around him. The moment the grandfather clock strikes eight, heavy steel security shutters violently slam down over every window and door, plunging the gala into a terrifying, inescapable lockdown.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The monster unleashes its full power, and the protagonist's sin results in the 'whiff of death' for the innocents.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From panicked denial to devastating guilt. (><) Valerie wants to stop the slaughter vs. the house's overwhelming, indiscriminate hunger.
beats.pacing_notes: Frantic, bloody, and overwhelming, moving at breakneck speed before landing on a moment of crushing despair.
beats.detailed_action: The house begins a mass feeding frenzy, turning staircases into slides that feed into grinding basement gears and dropping chandeliers to skewer innocent caterers. Valerie watches in absolute horror as the slaughter unfolds, clutching her bag of diamonds, realizing her insatiable corporate greed directly created this unstoppable massacre. When the floorboards open up and nearly swallow a screaming Toby, Valerie drops her diamonds, the weight of her moral bankruptcy finally crushing her icy facade.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist confronts her sin within the belly of the beast, finding the resolve to destroy the monster.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From paralyzing despair to resolute sacrifice. (><) Valerie wants to wallow in her failure vs. Toby's need for leadership and survival.
beats.pacing_notes: Dead-silent and deeply intimate, providing a stark emotional contrast to the previous carnage.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie drags Toby into a reinforced utility closet, locking the door as the sounds of the massacre echo outside. She collapses against the wall, obsessively scrubbing at her cuffs, and tearfully confesses the entire dark pact to Toby, admitting she became the exact type of monster that evicted her family. Toby, overcoming his stutter, gently stops her hands and tells her that the system is broken, but she has the power to break it permanently, sparking a massive epiphany in Valerie.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The A-story (surviving the monster) and B-story (Toby's moral guidance) merge to form the ultimate counter-attack.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From passive victims to proactive saboteurs. (><) Valerie and Toby want to destroy the house vs. the house's shifting, defensive architecture.
beats.pacing_notes: Determined and escalating, shifting from quiet planning to urgent, stealthy execution.
beats.detailed_action: Toby taps into his savant-like ability to read complex blueprints, pulling up the mansion's structural engineering anomalies on his phone to locate the central, highly pressurized gas main. Valerie realizes that to give Toby enough time to rupture the pipes, she needs to distract the entity's primary digestive core in the basement. They intertwine their skills, with Toby navigating the shifting ventilation shafts while Valerie arms herself with a fire axe, stepping out of the closet to hunt down her predatory boss, Richard.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The final confrontation where the protagonist uses her 'sin' (the diamonds) to destroy the monster and the human antagonist.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From terrifying confrontation to explosive liberation. (><) Valerie wants to detonate the house vs. Richard's greed and the house's crushing walls.
beats.pacing_notes: Maximum kinetic intensity, a chaotic and explosive climax that races against a ticking clock.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie lures Richard into the basement's massive, fleshy furnace room, where he attempts to murder her to claim the diamond-producing core for himself. The house turns on them both, animating the concrete walls to crush them into the central maw. In a final act of sacrifice, Valerie hurls her heavy bag of accumulated diamonds directly into the house's grinding gears, jamming the crushing mechanism just as Toby successfully floods the room with raw gas. Valerie sparks her engraved corporate lighter, tosses it into the fumes, and dives into a reinforced dumbwaiter as the mansion explodes in a spectacular, fiery demolition, incinerating Richard and the entity.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The opposite of the Opening Image; the protagonist is physically ruined but morally cleansed, having survived the monster.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From burdened and wealthy to broke and spiritually free. (><) Valerie wants to rest vs. the chaotic aftermath of the explosion.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, peaceful, and cathartic, lingering on the quiet aftermath of the destruction.
beats.detailed_action: A bruised, soot-covered Valerie sits on the curb across the street, watching the smoldering, destroyed rubble of the modernist mansion as emergency sirens wail in the distance. Toby sits beside her, handing her a cheap cup of coffee from a surviving thermos. Valerie looks down at her torn, ruined blazer cuffs, but makes absolutely no move to scrub them, finally broke, entirely unbothered, and completely free of her corporate greed.

stage5_treatment.title_logline_characters:
TITLE: Open House
LOGLINE: A ruthless real estate agent discovers her latest luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity that feeds on potential buyers, forcing her to market the deadly property to the worst people she knows to satisfy its hunger.
CHARACTERS: Valerie Vance, Richard Sterling, Toby Higgins, Gemma Frost, Arthur Pendelton.
stage5_treatment.act_1:
[SEQUENCE 1 START]
SEQUENCE 1: The Desperate Hustle

Movers ruthlessly haul cardboard boxes and worn furniture onto the curb of a modest suburban home. VALERIE VANCE (35) stands perfectly still on the manicured lawn, checking her luxury watch with icy detachment. She calculates the exact minute this property flips from a family dwelling to a marketable asset.

A tearful mother clutching a toddler approaches the immaculate agent to plead for just a few more days. Valerie does not flinch, nor does she offer a single shred of sympathy to the devastated woman. She aggressively steps past the mother, treating her like an invisible obstacle on the pavement.

Valerie points sharply at the home's weathered facade, loudly lecturing the eviction crew about neighborhood market comps and necessary cosmetic renovations. She demands the crew work faster, transforming her detached authority into aggressive callousness. The human cost of the eviction dissolves entirely into a sterile math equation.

Hours later, Valerie strides through the sterile, glass-and-steel luxury brokerage bullpen like a predator on the prowl. TOBY HIGGINS (25) scurries behind her, nervously adjusting his glasses. He aggressively organizes a stack of listing agreements into perfect right angles to manage his escalating anxiety.

Toby stammers out a desperate plea regarding the evicted family, arguing that people inherently matter more than property. He insists the brokerage has a responsibility to help those displaced by their aggressive acquisitions. His earnest optimism clashes violently against the cold, transactional environment of the bullpen.

Valerie stops dead in her tracks, pivoting to invade Toby's personal space. She delivers a suffocating barrage of corporate buzzwords to sanitize the grim reality of their business. Leaning in, she whispers sharply that morality is a luxury only the ultra-rich can afford, leaving Toby stunned.

Moments later, Valerie is summoned into a cavernous, glass-walled office at the end of the hall. RICHARD STERLING (50) stands facing the floor-to-ceiling window, calmly pouring a glass of expensive scotch. He does not even bother to look at her as she enters, establishing immediate, suffocating dominance.

Richard begins listing her secret, mounting personal debts aloud, systematically stripping away her wealthy armor. Valerie's composed veneer shatters instantly; her breathing hitches and she obsessively rubs her expensive blazer cuff. Her hands white-knuckle the back of a leather chair as the reality of her financial ruin is laid bare.

Richard finally turns, his eyes completely dead, and steps close enough for her to smell the liquor on his breath. He delivers his ultimate threat through clenched teeth: she is a financial liability on the verge of termination. The ticking clock on her total ruin begins, leaving her in a state of white-knuckled panic.
[SEQUENCE 1 END]

[SEQUENCE 2 START]
SEQUENCE 2: The First Meal

Following a cryptic, desperate lead, Valerie drives her luxury sedan to an isolated, heavily wooded lot. A stunning, brutalist modernist mansion sits silently among the trees, miraculously falling into her exclusive portfolio. She approaches the towering structure, her hopeless desperation driving her toward the grand front doors.

As she unlocks the heavy entryway, the temperature inside the foyer plummets unnaturally. The sharp architectural lines of the hallway seem to subtly shift, creating a dizzying optical illusion of a closing throat. A dark, predatory presence vibrates deep within the pristine hardwood floorboards, sending a chill up her spine.

Valerie senses the immediate danger, her survival instincts screaming at her to flee the property. However, she forcefully pushes her dread aside, her mind rapidly calculating the massive, life-saving commission. She chooses to ignore the supernatural warning signs, stepping fully into the belly of the beast.

The next afternoon, Valerie brings ARTHUR PENDELTON (40), a loud and newly-wealthy tech bro, for a private showing. Arthur booms through the echoing halls, constantly interrupting Valerie to brag about his obscure luxury purchases. He treats the magnificent space as nothing more than a hollow status symbol for his obnoxious parties.

Arthur aggressively insults the home's craftsmanship, his booming voice cracking slightly under the weight of his own imposter syndrome. He obsessively checks his heavy luxury watch without actually reading the time, desperate to project dominance. In response to his disrespect, heavy oak doors mysteriously slam shut behind them with bone-rattling force.

The hardwood floors groan hungrily beneath their feet, the sound mimicking a massive, starving stomach. Valerie stands paralyzed in the center of the room, debating whether to drag Arthur out of the terrifying structure. Ultimately, her greed wins; she pushes through her terror, launching into an aggressive sales pitch to secure the deal.
[SEQUENCE 2 END]
stage5_treatment.act_2a:
[SEQUENCE 3 START]
SEQUENCE 3: A Killer Marketing Strategy

Arthur continues his insufferable tirade, casually leaning his full weight against a pristine, load-bearing plaster wall to mock the recessed lighting. He taps the drywall rhythmically, his booming laugh echoing through the empty corridor. Without warning, the solid plaster beneath his expensive blazer softens, liquefying into a jagged, fleshy maw. The architectural lines warp violently inward, creating a terrifying vacuum that grips Arthur by the shoulders.

Arthur barely manages a wet, confused gasp before the wall violently inhales him. The plaster snaps back into perfect, rigid form, instantly silencing his screams and leaving the hallway dead silent. Valerie shrieks in pure, primal terror, her polished veneer shattering as she scrambles frantically toward the heavy oak front doors. She claws at the brass handles, her breath coming in ragged, panicked sobs as her survival instincts completely take over.

A sharp, metallic clatter echoes behind her, freezing Valerie in her tracks. She slowly turns to find a massive, blood-free diamond resting innocently on the pristine hardwood floor, exactly where Arthur vanished. Her panicked breathing slows as she stares at the impossible gem, her trembling hands obsessively scrubbing her blazer cuffs. The sheer terror in her eyes hardens into cold, calculating greed as she pockets the diamond, silently sealing a dark pact with the entity.

The next morning, Valerie struts into the glass-and-steel brokerage bullpen, radiating a dangerous, flush-with-cash confidence. She bypasses the senior agents and corners Toby at his desk, dropping a thick, velvet-bound ledger over his scattered paperwork. She pitches him an irresistible offer to act as the exclusive concierge for a highly classified, invite-only property viewing experience. She frames the venture as a masterclass in elite, ethical real estate, directly manipulating his earnest desire to do good.

Toby desperately attempts to mask his own mounting financial panic behind a wide, overly enthusiastic smile. He eagerly accepts the promotion, viewing this as his golden ticket to escape the ruthless bottom-feeding of the bullpen. He stammers out a pledge to handle the clients with the utmost care, completely blind to the sinister reality of Valerie's new enterprise. Valerie simply nods, her predatory smile widening as she secures her unwitting accomplice.

Immediately, Toby sets to work, aggressively organizing the new VIP guest list into perfect right angles on his desk. He color-codes the dossiers of corrupt politicians and predatory landlords, believing he is curating an exclusive, high-society gala. In reality, his meticulous organization serves a much darker purpose. Toby is unknowingly drafting a highly specific, gourmet menu for a starving supernatural predator.
[SEQUENCE 3 END]

[SEQUENCE 4 START]
SEQUENCE 4: The Appetite Grows

Over the next few weeks, Valerie transforms the haunted property into an exclusive trap for the city's most despicable elite. She curates a highly specific VIP list, luring a notorious, predatory SLUMLORD (55) into the brutalist mansion under the guise of an off-market steal. As the slumlord steps into the custom glass elevator to inspect the second floor, the steel cables suddenly shriek with unnatural tension. The elevator car violently shrinks, crushing the screaming man into a compact cube of twisted metal and shattered glass.

Days later, Valerie guides a deeply corrupt city MAYOR (60) out to the property's breathtaking, cliffside infinity pool. The mayor arrogantly dips his feet into the water, bragging about his latest embezzlement scheme. The water instantly thickens into a sentient, gelatinous mass, wrapping around his ankles and violently dragging him into the deep end. The pool surface quickly smooths over into perfect, glass-like stillness, leaving no trace of the struggling politician.

After every gruesome meal, Valerie calmly returns to the scene of the crime to collect a growing pile of pristine diamonds. She rapidly amasses immense, untraceable wealth, upgrading her wardrobe to bespoke designer suits and driving a brand-new luxury sports car. Striding into Richard Sterling's cavernous office, she slams a heavy briefcase of cash onto his desk, buying her absolute freedom. Her nervous complicity has completely transformed into a state of euphoric, untouchable invincibility.

Valerie’s arrogance reaches its peak as she escorts a vile, elderly BILLIONAIRE (80) into the mansion's grand foyer. She confidently recites her sanitized sales pitch, mentally calculating the size of the diamond this massive meal will produce. The billionaire sneers at the modernist architecture, tapping his gold-tipped cane aggressively against the hardwood floors. Valerie steps back, crossing her arms with a smug, expectant smile, waiting for the house to swallow its latest victim.

Instead of the usual smooth consumption, the floorboards violently buckle and heave, emitting a deafening, sickly groan. The house violently regurgitates the billionaire, launching his bruised and terrified body across the marble foyer. Instantly, every heavy exterior door slams shut with bone-rattling force, the deadbolts sliding into place with a synchronized, metallic snap. Valerie lunges for the exit, but the brass handles refuse to budge, trapping her inside the belly of the beast.

The pristine walls begin to pulsate with a sickening, organic rhythm, bleeding a clear, viscous sap from the recessed lighting fixtures. The glowing substance drips onto the hardwood floorboards, slowly pooling and shifting to form the word 'TOBY' in jagged, glowing letters. Valerie’s arrogant control instantly evaporates into absolute, sickening horror. She realizes with terrifying clarity that she is no longer the master of this transaction; the house is now making its own demands.
[SEQUENCE 4 END]
stage5_treatment.act_2b:
[SEQUENCE 5 START]
SEQUENCE 5: The Moral Compromise

Desperate to satisfy the starving entity without sacrificing her golden goose, Valerie lures her most ruthless rival, GEMMA (32), into the mansion's shadowy grand library. She pitches Gemma a fake co-listing agreement, silently praying the house will sense the competing agent's cutthroat ambition and devour her. Instead, the heavy oak floorboards remain perfectly still, the room dead silent as Gemma arrogantly critiques the crown molding. The entity flatly refuses the offering, finding Gemma's petty corporate backstabbing entirely unappetizing compared to Toby's pure innocence.

The moment Gemma leaves the property, the mansion violently turns its frustration entirely onto Valerie. The library's towering bookshelves suddenly warp inward, violently launching heavy leather-bound encyclopedias directly at Valerie's head. She dives behind a velvet armchair as the architectural geometry of the room aggressively shifts, shrinking the doorway to trap her inside. A jagged splinter of hardwood shoots from the floorboards, slicing a deep gash across her cheek as a final, bloody warning.

Fleeing back to the safety of the brokerage, Valerie frantically attempts to bandage her face, only to find RICHARD STERLING (55) waiting in her private office. The towering, silver-haired broker physically corners her against her desk, his eyes locked onto the undeniable pattern of high-net-worth clients vanishing immediately after touring her listing. He leans in close, his voice a terrifying, quiet hiss as he delivers a non-negotiable ultimatum. He demands she host a massive, heavily publicized open house gala at the property tonight, threatening to seize the deed and destroy her career if she refuses.
[SEQUENCE 5 END]

[SEQUENCE 6 START]
SEQUENCE 6: The Trap is Sprung

Against her every survival instinct, Valerie stands paralyzed by the mansion's towering front doors as the evening gala aggressively kicks into motion. Richard bulldozes past her, ushering in a massive, bustling crowd of innocent caterers, string quartet musicians, and wealthy socialites. Valerie clutches her clipboard like a shield, her stomach twisting into violent knots as she watches oblivious waiters carry trays of champagne deep into the entity's hungry belly. She desperately tries to invent an excuse to evacuate the building, but Richard shadows her every move, effectively holding her hostage to the event.

Toby bounds up the front steps in an ill-fitting, rented tuxedo, his face flushed with earnest excitement for his first major high-society function. He eagerly stations himself in the grand foyer, nervously stuttering through polite greetings as he meticulously organizes the guests' heavy winter coats. Valerie watches him arrange the garments with heartbreaking care, entirely unaware of the predatory hum vibrating through the floorboards beneath his polished dress shoes. Her icy corporate exterior begins to fracture, torn between her desperate need to protect her wealth and the sickening reality of Toby's impending doom.

The antique grandfather clock in the hallway begins a deep, resonant chime, striking exactly eight o'clock. On the final, deafening toll, thick slabs of reinforced steel violently slam down over every single window and exterior door with bone-rattling force. The deadbolts engage simultaneously, a synchronized, metallic snap that echoes over the sudden, terrified gasps of the trapped partygoers. The elegant string quartet screeches to a halt as the mansion plunges into a suffocating, inescapable lockdown.

The brutalist architecture immediately abandons all subtlety, launching into a horrific, indiscriminate feeding frenzy. The grand marble staircase violently flattens into a steep, frictionless slide, sending a dozen screaming guests plummeting into grinding, mechanical gears that suddenly materialize in the basement. Overhead, the heavy crystal chandeliers detach from their reinforced mounts, plummeting downward to skewer a cluster of fleeing caterers. The pristine white walls rapidly stain crimson as the house gorges itself on the trapped innocents, reveling in the absolute chaos.

Valerie presses herself into a recessed alcove, her trembling hands instinctively clutching the heavy velvet bag of diamonds hidden in her blazer pocket. She watches the horrific slaughter unfold through a haze of absolute, paralyzing terror, the deafening screams of the dying echoing in her ears. The sickening realization washes over her that her insatiable greed and desperate corporate climbing directly engineered this unstoppable massacre. Every drop of blood spilling across the hardwood belongs entirely to her.

Across the foyer, the floorboards beneath Toby violently split open, revealing a dark, fleshy chasm lined with jagged, wooden teeth. Toby screams, his fingers desperately clawing at the slick marble tiles as the vacuum attempts to drag him into the abyss. Staring at the terrified young man she manipulated, Valerie finally breaks, her moral bankruptcy crushing her soul. She hurls the bag of priceless diamonds into the darkness and lunges forward, grabbing Toby's wrists just as the house attempts to swallow him whole.
[SEQUENCE 6 END]
stage5_treatment.act_3:
[SEQUENCE 7 START]
SEQUENCE 7: The Demolition Plan

Valerie yanks Toby through a heavy oak door, throwing her entire body weight against the frame to seal them inside a reinforced utility closet. The muffled, horrific symphony of grinding bones and shattering glass echoes through the walls, vibrating against Valerie's spine as she slides to the floor. Paralyzed by the sheer scale of the slaughter, she frantically rubs at her blazer cuffs, her fingernails digging into the fabric in a desperate, obsessive attempt to scrub away the invisible blood.

The suffocating darkness of the closet forces Valerie to confront the absolute depravity of her actions. She chokes back a sob, confessing the sickening mechanics of the dark pact to a terrified Toby, detailing how she traded innocent lives for velvet bags of flawless diamonds. Her voice cracks as she admits the ultimate, crushing truth: in her desperate crusade to never be poor again, she transformed into the exact same ruthless monster that destroyed her family.

Toby listens to the horrific confession, his trembling hands slowly reaching out to gently grip Valerie's wrists, forcing her frantic scrubbing to a halt. Overcoming his lifelong stutter, he stares directly into her tear-filled eyes and delivers a piercing ultimatum, telling her that while the system is irreparably broken, she still possesses the power to smash it to pieces. The quiet, profound forgiveness in his voice acts as a catalyst, instantly vaporizing Valerie's paralyzing despair and replacing it with a cold, hardened resolve.

Channeling his savant-like obsession with structural engineering, Toby pulls out his cracked smartphone and accesses the municipal blueprints of the estate. He rapidly traces the shifting, impossible geometry of the mansion against the original schematics, his finger landing on a massive, highly pressurized gas main buried beneath the foundation. He calculates that a deliberate rupture of the primary valve will flood the lower levels with enough combustible vapor to level the entire property.

Valerie immediately recognizes the fatal flaw in the strategy: the house is far too hyper-aware to let Toby simply dismantle its internal organs unbothered. She realizes that to buy him the necessary time, she must offer the entity an irresistible, high-value distraction directly inside its primary digestive core. The survival of the only pure person she knows hinges entirely on her willingness to serve as live bait.

Working in synchronized, desperate silence, they pry off a heavy iron grate, allowing Toby to squeeze into the narrow, shifting ventilation shafts. Valerie watches his shoes disappear into the darkness before turning her attention to a glass emergency case mounted on the wall. She shatters the pane with her elbow, gripping the heavy wooden handle of a red fire axe, and kicks the closet door open to hunt down Richard.
[SEQUENCE 7 END]

[SEQUENCE 8 START]
SEQUENCE 8: Closing the Deal

Valerie descends into the suffocating, humid depths of the basement, deliberately dragging the blade of her axe against the exposed pipes to draw attention. She steps into the massive, cavernous furnace room, a horrific chamber where the concrete walls pulse with a sickening, fleshy rhythm. Richard emerges from the shadows, his tailored suit torn, his eyes completely glazed over with a manic, insatiable greed as he spots the heavy velvet bag bulging in Valerie's pocket.

Completely ignoring the supernatural horror surrounding them, Richard lunges at Valerie with a rusted pipe, determined to murder her and claim the diamond-producing core for himself. Valerie ducks beneath his wild swing, driving the blunt end of her axe into his kneecap, sending the towering broker crashing to the grated floor. Before Richard can recover, the mansion suddenly recognizes the immense concentration of greed in the room and violently turns on them both.

The massive concrete walls groan, rapidly animating and contracting inward to crush the two realtors into a terrifying, grinding maw that opens in the center of the floor. Richard screams as a jagged slab of masonry pins his legs, dragging him toward the churning mechanical teeth. Valerie scrambles backward, her boots slipping on the blood-slicked grating as the crushing mechanism threatens to pull her into the abyss alongside her former boss.

Realizing the house feeds entirely on transactional wealth, Valerie reaches into her pocket and pulls out the heavy bag of accumulated diamonds. In a final, definitive rejection of her corporate ambition, she hurls the priceless gems directly into the center of the grinding gears. The flawless stones wedge violently between the mechanical teeth, creating a catastrophic jam that halts the crushing walls with an ear-splitting, metallic screech.

A sudden, deafening hiss echoes through the chamber as Toby successfully breaches the main line above, instantly flooding the basement with thick, choking clouds of raw natural gas. Valerie coughs violently, pulling her engraved, gold-plated corporate lighter from her pocket, the very symbol of her ruthless ascension. She sparks the flint, tossing the flaming lighter directly into the invisible fumes as she dives headfirst into a reinforced steel dumbwaiter.

The spark ignites the gas, triggering a spectacular, earth-shattering explosion that instantly incinerates Richard and vaporizes the entity's fleshy core. A massive shockwave rips upward through the foundation, tearing the brutalist architecture to shreds and blowing the mansion's reinforced steel doors off their hinges. The dumbwaiter rockets upward through the collapsing shaft, violently ejecting Valerie onto the front lawn just as the entire structure collapses into a blazing inferno.

A bruised, soot-covered Valerie sits on the icy concrete curb across the street, quietly watching the smoldering, destroyed rubble of the modernist mansion. The chaotic wail of approaching emergency sirens cuts through the freezing night air, but a profound, heavy silence settles over her exhausted mind. The monstrous entity is dead, and the suffocating weight of her ambition has burned away with it.

Toby limps out of the shadows, his rented tuxedo in tatters, and silently takes a seat on the curb beside her. He unscrews the cap of a surviving plastic thermos, pouring a steaming cup of cheap, bitter coffee and pressing it into her trembling hands. They sit shoulder-to-shoulder in the flashing red and blue lights, watching the empire of greed burn to the ground.

Valerie looks down at her torn, ruined blazer, her eyes locking onto the dark, heavy bloodstains soaking her cuffs. For the first time in her entire life, she makes absolutely no move to scrub the fabric clean, accepting the permanent stain of her past. She is entirely broke, her career is completely destroyed, but as she takes a sip of the cheap coffee, she has never felt more spiritually free.
[SEQUENCE 8 END]

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. SUBURBAN HOME - FRONT LAWN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Movers ruthlessly haul worn furniture and cardboard boxes out the front door of a modest home, dumping them onto the curb. Valerie Vance stands perfectly still on the manicured lawn amidst the chaos, her icy gaze fixed on the property. She checks her luxury watch, mentally calculating the exact minute the dwelling transitions from a family home into a marketable asset.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Opening Image. Establishes Valerie's ruthless baseline and transactional worldview. (><) Valerie wants a clean timeline vs. the messy reality of the eviction. (+/-) Neutral to Dominant (+).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. SUBURBAN HOME - FRONT LAWN - DAY

A scuffed faux-leather sofa hits the curb. HARD. A plume of stale dust erupts into the morning air.

Sweat-drenched MOVERS march in a relentless assembly line out the front door of a modest, single-story house. They haul out cardboard boxes, a plastic tricycle, a framed family portrait. 

They don't place them. They dump them. 

Amidst the chaotic debris stands VALERIE VANCE (30s). She wears a razor-sharp, slate-grey designer suit. A predatory monolith completely unfazed by the destruction around her. 

She lifts her wrist. A heavy, silver Rolex catches the sun. She watches the second hand sweep. Calculating.

A burly FOREMAN drops a taped box at her feet. The bottom splits. Cheap children's winter coats spill onto the damp, overgrown grass.

FOREMAN
That's the last of it. Sheriff said we got ten minutes before he locks the deadbolts. 

Valerie doesn't look at the coats. Her icy gaze remains locked on the house's front bay window. 

VALERIE
(smooth, detached)
Let's expedite that timeline. The mid-century bones of this property are being suffocated by the clutter. 

FOREMAN
Clutter? Lady, that's their life. 

He gestures to the end of the driveway. A rusted sedan idles at the curb. Inside, a MOTHER grips the steering wheel, sobbing silently. A TEENAGER slumps in the passenger seat, staring at the lawn in hollow shock.

Valerie’s jaw tightens. A microscopic fracture in her porcelain mask. 

She begins to vigorously, obsessively rub the edge of her pristine white cuff with her thumb. Scrubbing an invisible stain.

FOREMAN
Kind of makes you sick, don't it?

Valerie stops rubbing. The mask snaps back into place. Flawless. Cold.

VALERIE
Emotional attachments depreciate market value. Clear the driveway. The landscaping crew needs access to the frontage.

The Foreman scoffs, shaking his head. He kicks a discarded, plastic doll out of his path and marches back toward the house.

Valerie finally looks down at the grass. 

The plastic doll lies face up in the dirt. Clinging to its synthetic hair is a struggling, injured HONEYBEE, its wing bent. 

Valerie kneels. The expensive fabric of her trousers brushes the mud, but she doesn't care. 

She extends a manicured finger. The bee weakly crawls onto her nail. 

VALERIE
(softly)
You're just in the wrong market.

She stands, carefully carrying the insect over to a blooming hydrangea bush near the porch. She deposits it safely among the petals. 

Behind her, the rusted sedan finally shifts into gear and drives away, the sound of its failing muffler fading down the street.

Valerie doesn't turn around to watch them leave. She looks up at the empty house. 

VALERIE
Blank canvas. 

She pulls a sleek phone from her pocket and dials.

VALERIE
Toby. The distressed asset on Elm is neutralized. Get the photographer out here. We list by noon.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. SUBURBAN HOME - DRIVEWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: A tearful mother clutching a toddler intercepts Valerie, blocking her path to plead for just a few more days to pack. Valerie does not flinch. She offers zero sympathy, instead treating the crying woman like an invisible physical obstacle. Valerie aggressively steps around her, refusing to make eye contact as she pushes toward the house.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Demonstrates Valerie's active refusal of empathy, cementing her fatal flaw. (><) The mother wants mercy vs. Valerie wants to avoid human connection. (+/-) Desperate (-) to Dismissed (-).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. SUBURBAN HOME - DRIVEWAY - CONTINUOUS

Valerie marches up the cracked concrete. Her designer heels click with metronomic precision, dodging weeds that sprout through the fissures.

A battered minivan idles in the dead grass. Cardboard boxes, black trash bags, and a child’s plastic tricycle are piled haphazardly on the lawn. 

Valerie checks her phone screen. A glaring red notification: ACCOUNT OVERDRAWN - ACTION REQUIRED. She swipes it away, her jaw tightening. 

A TEARFUL MOTHER (30s) steps directly into her path. Exhausted, frantic. She balances a crying TODDLER on her hip.

TEARFUL MOTHER
Miss Vance. Please. 

Valerie doesn't slow down. She veers right, attempting to bypass the woman like a misplaced traffic cone.

VALERIE
The property transfer was finalized at nine a.m. You are currently trespassing on bank-owned equity.

TEARFUL MOTHER
The moving truck broke down on the interstate! I just need until Monday. Two days.

Mother steps sideways, physically cutting off Valerie’s route to the porch. 

Valerie halts. She refuses to make eye contact. Instead, she looks past the woman’s shoulder, her gaze locking onto the roofline. 

VALERIE
The asphalt shingles are curling. That’s a massive liability for the upcoming appraisal. 

TEARFUL MOTHER
(voice breaking)
Where are we supposed to sleep? It's thirty degrees tonight. My daughter has a fever.

Mother reaches out. Her trembling hand grabs the sleeve of Valerie’s immaculate silk blouse.

Valerie freezes. Her eyes dart to the woman’s fingers. 

She yanks her arm back. Her thumb immediately goes to her pristine French cuff, scrubbing aggressively at an invisible smudge. Scrubbing. Hard.

VALERIE
Do not touch me. 

TEARFUL MOTHER
I’m begging you. You have to have some compassion. We have nothing left.

Valerie’s thumb stops. She takes a slow, measured breath. The icy, bulletproof professional mask slides perfectly back into place. 

She points a manicured finger toward the edge of the front lawn. 

VALERIE
Look at the weeping willow by the property line. 

The Mother blinks, thoroughly disoriented by the pivot. She glances back at the tree.

TEARFUL MOTHER
What? 

VALERIE
It looks picturesque. Sentimental, even. But the root system is highly invasive. It’s quietly shattering the foundation of this asset. 

Valerie steps directly into the Mother's personal space, forcing the woman to instinctively back up. The path to the door is now clear.

VALERIE
Weeping isn't an excuse for structural damage. It has to be uprooted. 

Valerie strides past her, ascending the wooden porch steps. 

TEARFUL MOTHER
(sobbing)
You're a monster!

Valerie tests the front doorknob. Locked. She punches a code into the digital lockbox hanging from the railing. 

VALERIE
Leave the remaining trash on the curb. The remediation crew arrives at six.

The lockbox beeps. Valerie extracts the key, slides it into the deadbolt, and pushes into the dark, empty house. 

She shuts the heavy door behind her, instantly severing the sound of the crying child.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. SUBURBAN HOME - SIDEWALK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Ignoring the devastated family crying behind her, Valerie points sharply at the home's weathered facade. She loudly lectures the sweating eviction crew about neighborhood market comps and necessary cosmetic renovations, demanding they work faster. She completely sanitizes the human cost of the eviction into a sterile math equation.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Cements her capitalist armor. (><) Valerie wants absolute efficiency vs. the crew's physical limits. (+/-) Annoyed (-) to In Control (+).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. SUBURBAN HOME - SIDEWALK - CONTINUOUS

A battered minivan idles at the curb. Beside it, a MOTHER (40s) clutches a plastic laundry basket full of loose toys, openly weeping. A TEENAGE BOY stares at the pavement, humiliated. 

Two burly MOVERS muscle a stained floral sofa out the front door, panting in the oppressive heat. 

Standing exactly three feet away, with her back to the crying family, is VALERIE VANCE (30s). She wears an immaculate, razor-sharp beige power suit. She doesn't flinch at the mother’s sobs. Her eyes are locked on the peeling paint of the house's facade. 

FOREMAN (50s, drenched in sweat) drops a box of kitchenware on the grass. He shoots a guilty look toward the weeping mother.

FOREMAN
Look, Miss Vance. It’s ninety-two degrees. They’re trying to get the rest of the kids' stuff out of the back bedrooms--

VALERIE
(interrupting)
The fascia boards are rotting, Mike. Do you see the water damage near the gutters?

FOREMAN
I’m just saying, maybe we give them another twenty minutes to--

VALERIE
If we don't scrape and paint this exterior by Thursday, the curb appeal stays stagnant. Stagnant curb appeal in this zip code drops the price-per-square-foot by twelve percent. 

The Foreman wipes his forehead with a dirty rag. He gestures to the sobbing family.

FOREMAN
They lived here for fifteen years. We're moving as fast as we can. 

Valerie’s eyes dart to the mother. A muscle in Valerie's jaw twitches. She looks down at her pristine white cuff and aggressively rubs an invisible smudge with her thumb. 

VALERIE
Emotion does not amortize, Mike. This is a transitional asset. Currently, it’s a depreciating liability because there is a floral sofa on the lawn. 

A heavy WORK BOOT steps toward Valerie. One of the movers is backing up with a heavy oak dresser. 

Valerie’s eyes track downward. A large, iridescent GREEN BEETLE struggles on its back on the concrete, legs kicking frantically, inches from the mover's descending heel.

VALERIE (CONT'D)
Hold. 

The mover freezes, the dresser trembling in his grip. 

Valerie crouches. Her expensive skirt grazes the dirty sidewalk. With surgical gentleness, she slides a manicured fingernail under the beetle, flipping it upright. She cups it in her palm, stands, and deposits the insect safely into the leaves of a nearby hydrangea bush.

She turns back to the Foreman. The ice is fully restored. 

VALERIE (CONT'D)
The comps in this subdivision demand a modernized entryway. I need the interior gutted by three. If your crew is fatigued, I can find another vendor who understands the velocity of a flip. 

The Foreman stares at her, unnerved by the seamless pivot. He looks at the heavy dresser, then back to Valerie's unblinking gaze.

FOREMAN
(defeated)
We'll have it cleared by three. 

VALERIE
Excellent. Start with the children's rooms. The spatial flow in there is a nightmare. 

Valerie turns her back on him, raising her phone to her ear to make her next call. 

Behind her, the mother’s wails grow louder. Valerie doesn't hear a thing. She just stares at the house, calculating the math.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - LOBBY - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie pushes through the heavy glass doors of a sterile, glass-and-steel luxury brokerage. She strides through the bullpen like a predator on the prowl, her heels clicking sharply against the tile. Toby Higgins scurries frantically behind her, struggling to keep pace while clutching a messy stack of listing agreements.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Transitions Valerie from the field to her corporate ecosystem. (><) Valerie wants to project power vs. Toby's chaotic, trailing presence. (+/-) Confident (+) to Hunted by annoyance (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - LOBBY - LATER

Heavy glass doors swing open. VALERIE VANCE cuts through the threshold. Her designer heels click against the marble floor like a metronome of impending doom. 

The brokerage is a sterile, glass-and-steel terrarium. Agents in tailored suits pretend to work, eyes tracking Valerie's predatory stride. She projects pure, unadulterated solvency.

Right behind her, ruining the illusion, is TOBY HIGGINS. He clutches a teetering Jenga tower of listing agreements, panting slightly.

TOBY
(breathless)
Ms. Vance? The accounting department left three voicemails regarding the Palisades escrow. They, um, they require a definitive timeline on the wire transfer.

Valerie doesn't miss a step. Her thumb aggressively rubs an invisible blemish on her pristine French cuff. 

VALERIE
Notice the ambient lighting in here, Toby? It is a complete misappropriation of lumens. It washes out the structural integrity of the crown molding and makes everyone look fiscally depressed. 

TOBY
Yes, but the bank--

VALERIE
We are projecting high-yield optics today. Walk with a higher cap rate.

Toby tries to adjust his posture. The top half of his paper stack slides off, scattering across the sterile tile. 

He drops to his knees, frantically gathering the documents. His breathing hitches. He begins squaring the edges of the folders against the floor tiles, obsessively aligning them into perfect ninety-degree angles.

TOBY
(stuttering)
I-I can get you a macchiato. If you need a moment to review the--

Valerie stops. She looks down at him. The buzzing bullpen feels suddenly quiet. Her icy veneer hardens, the annoyance bubbling dangerously close to the surface.

VALERIE
I need you to curate an aura of exclusivity, Toby. Not a foreclosure auction. Pick up the collateral. 

She turns on her heel, marching toward the inner offices. Toby remains on the floor, desperately squaring a manila folder against a grout line.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - BULLPEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Toby trails Valerie past rows of sleek desks. To manage his escalating anxiety, he aggressively squares the edges of his listing agreements into perfect right angles as he walks. He stammers out a desperate plea about the evicted family, insisting the brokerage has a responsibility to help displaced people and that humanity inherently matters more than property.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Theme Stated. Toby presents the moral argument of the film. (><) Toby wants ethical accountability vs. Valerie's forward momentum. (+/-) Anxious (-) to Earnest (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - BULLPEN - CONTINUOUS

A labyrinth of brushed steel desks, glass partitions, and Armani-clad sharks. Phones trill softly. The air smells of expensive espresso and cutthroat ambition. 

Valerie Vance strides through the bullpen like a heat-seeking missile. Her posture is a weapon. 

Toby Higgins trails two steps behind her, practically jogging to keep up. He clutches a thick stack of listing agreements to his chest.

TOBY
M-Ms. Vance, if we could just revisit the Martinez file for a moment? 

VALERIE
The file is closed, Toby. The unit is undergoing a rapid revitalization phase.

TOBY
They're being locked out. Today. I-I was wondering, could we perhaps intercept the landlord? Negotiate a temporary hold?

VALERIE
We are agents, not grief counselors. Our mandate is to maximize per-square-foot yield. 

Valerie weaves past a sprawling, acrylic architectural model of a high-rise. She doesn't break stride. 

Toby stops at the edge of a vacant desk. He taps his stack of listing agreements against the glass surface. 

Smack. Smack. Smack. 

He meticulously aligns the corners until the papers form a flawless, razor-sharp ninety-degree angle.

TOBY
But they have a newborn. They've leased that space for twelve years. Is there not some... some ethical contingency we can trigger? 

Valerie halts. The mention of the evicted family lands like a physical blow. She stares down at her pristine, expensive French cuffs. 

Her right thumb begins to rub her left wrist. Hard. Scrubbing at a stain only she can see. 

VALERIE
Look at this Herman Miller chair, Toby.

TOBY
(blinks, thrown)
The... the chair?

VALERIE
Ergonomic mesh. Retail value of eighteen hundred dollars. Do you know why Richard furnished the bullpen with them?

TOBY
To promote lumbar support?

VALERIE
To keep us at our desks for fourteen hours a day without our spines collapsing. It is an investment in relentless productivity. It has a specific, unyielding function. Just like we have a function. 

Toby's hands shake. He presses the perfectly squared files against his chest like a shield. His earnestness breaks through his panic.

TOBY
W-we have a responsibility! We put them in that unit. People are not just distressed inventory to be cleared out. Humanity... humanity inherently matters more than the property value.

The bullpen hums around them. Agents murmur predatory pitches into wireless headsets.
