Uninterrupted flow. Cute buzzwords. 

He shoots his cuffs, exposing a massive, diamond-encrusted timepiece. 

ARTHUR
My Audemars Piguet costs more than the quarry they dug this dust out of. I don't adapt to the house, Val. The house adapts to me. 

Behind Arthur’s shoulders, the bone-white plaster ripples. 

Valerie stops breathing. Her laminated smile falters. 

The wall directly behind Arthur’s expensive blazer softens. The rigid architectural lines warp violently inward, losing their geometric perfection. 

VALERIE
Arthur. Step away from the--

ARTHUR
(interrupting)
Don't tell me how to interact with my property. I'm buying it. I own the damn--

The plaster liquefies. 

A jagged, fleshy maw opens directly beneath his blazer. A terrifying, silent vacuum grips Arthur by the shoulders. 

Arthur’s eyes go wide. His booming arrogance shatters. 

In a sheer panic, he compulsively looks down at his luxury watch, his brain unable to process the physical impossibility of the wall pulling him backward. 

ARTHUR
(voice cracking)
What the--

He barely manages a wet, confused gasp. 

The wall violently inhales him. 

The sheer force folds Arthur backward into the maw. His loafers lift off the hardwood. He vanishes into the plaster in a blur of fabric and flesh. 

SNAP. 

The wall snaps back into perfect, rigid form. Seamless Venetian plaster. Not a single crack. Not a drop of blood. 

It instantly silences his screams. 

The hallway falls dead silent. 

Valerie stands frozen in the center of the vast, empty corridor. The negative space is overwhelming. 

She looks at the pristine wall. She looks at her empty hands. 

Her breath comes in short, ragged, shallow bursts. 

Slowly, her fingers curl inward. She drops her gaze and begins obsessively, violently scrubbing at an invisible stain on her immaculate silk cuff. 

She doesn't stop scrubbing. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MANSION FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie shrieks in pure, primal terror, her polished professional veneer shattering completely. She scrambles frantically down the hall and throws herself at the heavy oak front doors. She claw at the brass handles, her breath coming in ragged, panicked sobs as her survival instincts completely take over, but the doors remain fused shut.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Strips Valerie of her corporate mask, reducing her to a trapped animal. (><) Valerie's desperate need to escape vs. the House's physical lockdown. (+/-) From composed realtor (+) to terrified, trapped prey (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION FOYER - CONTINUOUS

A piercing, guttural SHRIEK shatters the pristine silence.

VALERIE VANCE tears around the corner, her polished professional veneer entirely obliterated. Her eyes are wide, unblinking plates of terror. 

She scrambles frantically down the hyper-minimalist hallway. Her designer stilettos slip on the polished slate floor. She twists her ankle, stumbles, but catches herself against an immaculate white wall, leaving a sweaty, frantic handprint.

She lunges into the cavernous foyer and throws her entire body weight against the heavy oak front doors. 

VALERIE
(hyperventilating)
Open. Open. Open!

She claws at the dual brass handles. Yanks them backward. 

They don't budge. No click of a lock. No rattle in the frame. They feel fused to the architecture, as if the doors are a solid, immovable slab of the house's foundation.

She plants her feet, grips the brass with both hands, and pulls with a violent, animalistic grunt. The tendons in her neck bulge. 

Nothing. 

Valerie beats her fists against the thick oak. 

VALERIE
Somebody! Help! 

Her voice echoes off the vaulted ceilings, swallowed instantly by the suffocating, engineered acoustics of the mansion. 

She backs away, chest heaving, breath coming in wet, panicked sobs. Her gaze darts wildly to the towering, reinforced glass windows flanking the door.

Without hesitating, Valerie snatches a heavy, geometric bronze sculpture off a nearby marble pedestal. 

With a feral cry, she hurls the fifty-pound piece of art directly at the window.

THUD. 

The bronze bounces off the glass like a rubber toy and crashes onto the slate. The window doesn't even bear a hairline fracture. 

Valerie stares at the unbroken glass. The absolute impossibility of it. The reality of the trap closes in around her throat. 

Her hands begin to tremble violently. She looks down at her wrists. 

Her eyes lock onto the cuff of her expensive silk blouse. There is nothing there, but her pupils dilate as if staring at a massive, spreading bloodstain. 

Her breathing turns shallow. She grabs her own wrist and begins to scrub frantically at the invisible stain on the silk with her thumb. 

She scrubs harder. Faster. The friction turning her own skin raw beneath the fabric. 

VALERIE
(whispering rapidly)
It's fine. It's fine. Unparalleled security features. Minor structural quirks. It's fine.

She slides slowly down the immovable oak doors, her tailored skirt scraping against the wood, until she hits the floor. 

She sits in the shadows of the foyer, a trapped animal in a designer cage, obsessively rubbing her cuff as the house hums around her in perfect, terrifying silence.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MANSION FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: A sharp, metallic clatter echoes from down the empty hallway, cutting through Valerie's sobs. She freezes in her tracks, her hands still white-knuckling the brass handles. She slowly turns around, her panicked breathing slowing as she spots a gleaming object resting innocently on the pristine hardwood floor, exactly where Arthur vanished.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Interrupts the flight response with the catalyst of greed. (><) Valerie's primal fear vs. her magnetic curiosity. (+/-) From blind panic (-) to bewildered intrigue (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION FOYER - CONTINUOUS

Valerie throws her entire body weight against the heavy mahogany front doors. Her breath comes in jagged, wet gasps. 

Her hands--shaking violently--white-knuckle the brushed brass handles. She yanks. The door does not budge. 

Tears ruin her flawless makeup. She is a trapped animal. She opens her mouth to scream--

CLACK.

A sharp, heavy impact echoes from deep within the house. 

Tink-tink-tink...

Something dense rolls across the hardwood, slowing to a dead stop. 

Valerie freezes. Her scream dies in her throat. 

The silence that follows is absolute. The house is perfectly still. 

Slowly, she releases the brass handles. Her right thumb immediately drops to her left wrist, obsessively scrubbing at an invisible stain on her white silk cuff. Scrubbing, scrubbing, scrubbing. 

She turns around. 

The cavernous, modernist hallway stretches out before her. The architectural lines are sharp, unforgiving. 

Empty. No Arthur Pendelton. No torn clothes. No blood. 

Just pristine, untouched Brazilian walnut flooring. 

Valerie’s frantic panting begins to slow. She takes a hesitant step away from the exit. Then another. 

A tiny, injured silverfish crawls erratically across the baseboard. Valerie’s designer heel hovers over it. She pauses, delicately adjusts her stride, and steps around the insect. 

She moves deeper into the belly of the hallway. 

Sunlight slices through the floor-to-ceiling windows, illuminating a single spot on the floor. Exactly where the floorboards had opened up and swallowed Arthur whole.

Resting perfectly in the center of a sunbeam is an object. 

Valerie stops. Her eyes narrow. The terror in her chest fights a sudden, magnetic pull. 

She crouches down. 

It is a diamond. 

Roughly the size of a golf ball. Flawless. It catches the ambient light and fractures it into a blinding spectrum of colors across Valerie’s tear-stained face. 

She reaches out. Her trembling fingers hover over the gem. 

VALERIE
(whispering)
Unencumbered... absolute equity.

She touches it. It is warm. 

Valerie picks it up. The sheer weight of it rests in her palm. No blood. No trace of the obnoxious tech bro who stood there sixty seconds ago. 

Her thumb stops scrubbing her cuff. 

The panic completely drains from her eyes, replaced by a dark, intoxicating clarity. The corners of her mouth slowly twitch upward.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MANSION HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie cautiously approaches the spot. A massive, pristine, blood-free diamond sits on the floorboards. She stares at the impossible gem, her trembling hands compulsively scrubbing at invisible stains on her blazer cuffs. The sheer terror in her eyes slowly hardens into cold, calculating greed. She stops scrubbing, reaches down, and pockets the diamond, silently sealing a dark pact with the entity.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Break into Two. Valerie commits the ultimate sin, choosing wealth over morality. (><) Valerie's human conscience vs. her deeply ingrained fear of poverty. (+/-) From terrified victim (-) to calculating, complicit monster (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS

The silence is absolute. 

Then, a low, subsonic hum vibrates through the Brazilian walnut floorboards. The architectural skeleton of the mansion settles. A mahogany beam groans overhead. 

It sounds exactly like a satisfied sigh.

VALERIE (30s) stands frozen ten feet away. Her immaculate posture is gone. Her chest heaves in shallow, jagged gasps. 

She stares at the empty space in the center of the corridor. The exact spot where Arthur Pendelton stood seconds ago. 

There is no blood. No torn Armani fabric. Not a single scuff mark on the pristine, museum-white baseboards. He has simply been erased.

Valerie’s hands shake violently. Her right thumb and forefinger find the edge of her left blazer cuff. 

She rubs the fabric. Hard. Scrubbing at an invisible stain. Faster. Frantic. 

She takes a hesitant step forward. Her designer heel clicks sharply against the wood. The sound echoes down the cavernous, minimalist corridor, swallowed by the shadows.

VALERIE
(whispering)
Sudden foundational shift. A... a localized sinkhole. 

She takes another step. 

A faint, papery scraping sound stops her. 

Valerie looks down. A large, iridescent green June bug lies on its back near her shoe. Its legs kick frantically, trapped in the deep groove between the floorboards. 

Valerie stares at the struggling insect. A tear cuts through the flawless foundation makeup on her cheek. She is unraveling. 

Yet, she slowly drops to her knees. Her shaking fingers reach out. She gently scoops the beetle up, turning it over, and places it safely upright against the wall. 

She tracks her eyes back to the center of the hallway. 

Sitting perfectly centered in a shaft of afternoon sunlight, resting on the bare walnut planks, is a rock.

No. Not a rock.

A diamond.

It is the size of a golf ball. Flawless. The razor-sharp facets catch the sunlight, throwing a blinding prism of colors across the stark white gallery walls. 

Valerie crawls toward it. Her knees drag against the expensive wood. The obsessive scrubbing at her cuffs intensifies--fabric aggressively grinding against fabric. 

She stops inches from the gem. It radiates a faint, impossible chill. 

With her free hand, she pulls her phone from her pocket. The screen lights up, highlighting a web of spiderweb cracks in the glass. 

A glaring red notification sits on the lock screen: INSUFFICIENT FUNDS - OVERDRAFT ALERT. 

Her trembling thumb swipes the alert away. She opens the keypad. 

9. 

1. 

1. 

Her thumb hovers over the green CALL button. Her breathing is ragged. The terrified little girl facing eviction is right behind her eyes, drowning in panic. 

The house shifts again. The ambient temperature in the hallway drops, cooling the cold sweat on Valerie's neck. A vent exhales a soft breeze, carrying the faint scent of ozone and old dust. 

Valerie looks from the cracked phone screen to the diamond. 

The prism of light hits her face, illuminating the sharp, predatory angles of her jaw. 

Her thumb twitches over the screen. 

She slowly lowers the phone. 

The frantic scrubbing at her cuff slows. 

Then, it stops entirely. 

Valerie leans in close to the gem. Her eyes narrow, analyzing the clarity, the cut, the sheer weight of it. The primal terror in her eyes begins to crystallize, hardening into cold, absolute arithmetic. 

VALERIE
Flawless integration. 

She looks at her phone. She hits the backspace button. 

VALERIE
Turnkey condition. 

She deletes the numbers. The 9-1-1 vanishes. 

VALERIE
High-yield return. 

Valerie reaches out. Her hand, previously trembling, is now completely steady. 

Her manicured fingers close around the diamond. It is heavy. Ice cold. Perfect. 

She slips it deep into her blazer pocket. 

Valerie stands. She smooths the lapels of her jacket, her posture snapping back to perfect, icy professionalism. She wipes the single tear from her cheek, erasing the last trace of the victim. 

She looks down the long, empty hallway. The shadows stretch toward her like open arms. 

VALERIE
(to the house)
I have an extensive portfolio of motivated buyers. 

The floorboards hum in response. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. BROKERAGE BULLPEN - THE NEXT MORNING
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie struts into the glass-and-steel brokerage bullpen, radiating a dangerous, flush-with-cash confidence. She completely bypasses the senior agents she usually defers to, her heels clicking aggressively on the tile. She zeroes in on Toby, who is nervously hiding past-due bills under his scattered paperwork.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes Valerie's new dominant status and targets her moral counterweight. (><) Valerie's new predatory confidence vs. the bullpen's established hierarchy. (+/-) From a desperate, drowning agent (-) to a powerful, untouchable shark (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. BROKERAGE BULLPEN - THE NEXT MORNING

Glass, steel, and the predatory hum of morning cold calls. 

The sharp, rhythmic CLACK of new Louboutins echoes over the chatter. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) doesn't just walk; she conquers the aisle. Her cheap poly-blend blazers are gone, replaced by an impeccably tailored, icy-white silk suit. She radiates a dangerous, untouchable gravity.

GEMMA FROST (30s) steps into the walkway holding a stack of comps, expecting Valerie to yield the right-of-way. 

Valerie doesn't break stride. She clips Gemma’s shoulder, forcing the rival agent to stumble back against a glass partition. Gemma's jaw drops, her manicured fingers instantly moving to aggressively pick at her own cuticle. Valerie doesn't even look back.

A dying honeybee twitches on the edge of a filing cabinet. Valerie pauses, gently scooping the insect onto a glossy brochure, and deposits it safely into the soil of a potted orchid. 

She zeroes in on a tiny laminate desk in the corner. 

TOBY HIGGINS (20s) is frantically sliding a stack of red-stamped 'PAST DUE' notices underneath a sprawling pile of floor plans. His hands tremble. He aligns three highlighters into a perfect, ninety-degree triangle.

VALERIE
Cancel the mid-market open houses, Toby. We are pivoting our portfolio.

Toby jolts, knocking a highlighter out of alignment. He immediately snaps it back into place.

TOBY
Valerie. Good morning. I-- I was just about to get the drip coffee going. Do you need a dark roast?

VALERIE
I need the structural schematics for the Blackwood property. Every ingress, egress, and sub-floor anomaly.

TOBY
(stuttering)
The-- the Blackwood? But Mr. Pendelton hasn't signed the disclosures. Did he... did he back out?

Valerie’s thumb drops to her pristine silk cuff. She aggressively rubs at an invisible stain on the fabric.

VALERIE
Mr. Pendelton was absorbed by a sudden, aggressive market shift. The listing remains exclusively mine.

Toby glances at her wrist. A heavy, flawless diamond-encrusted Rolex gleams under the fluorescent lights. 

TOBY
I don't... I don't understand. The agency protocols--

VALERIE
The agency lacks vision. I am hosting a highly curated, private viewing tomorrow night. I need a savant to map the architectural blind spots. 

Valerie’s eyes flick down, locking onto the edge of a collection agency notice peeking out from his blueprints. 

VALERIE
That laminate desk is depreciating your potential. We are upgrading your equity.

TOBY
Upgrading? Valerie, I-- I don't think I have the operational authority to--

Valerie drops a thick, crisp manila envelope onto his desk. It lands with a heavy THUD, perfectly in the center of his highlighter triangle. 

VALERIE
Retainer. 

Toby stares at the envelope. He nudges the flap. Stacks of hundred-dollar bills stare back at him. He swallows hard, his earnest eyes darting up to hers.

TOBY
For... for mapping blind spots? Valerie, what exactly are we facilitating here?

VALERIE
We are facilitating absolute financial liquidity, Toby. 

Before Toby can utter another syllable, Valerie turns and strides away. Her heels echo like a ticking clock, leaving Toby alone with the money, the blueprints, and a mounting sense of dread.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. BROKERAGE BULLPEN (TOBY'S DESK) - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie corners Toby at his desk, slamming a thick, velvet-bound ledger down over his hidden bills. She pitches him an irresistible offer: act as the exclusive concierge for a highly classified, invite-only property viewing experience. She deliberately uses his own earnest language, framing the venture as a masterclass in 'elite, ethical real estate' to bait the hook.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The temptation of the B-Story character. Valerie weaponizes Toby's morality against him. (><) Valerie's manipulative agenda vs. Toby's genuine desire to do good. (+/-) From financially panicked (-) to intrigued and hopeful (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. BROKERAGE BULLPEN (TOBY'S DESK) - CONTINUOUS

Fluorescent lights hum over a sea of identical cubicles. 

TOBY HIGGINS (20s) sits trapped behind his desk. He breathes in short, jagged gasps. 

Spread before him: three FINAL NOTICE envelopes, printed in aggressive, neon pink ink. 

Toby's hands tremble. He squares the envelopes, nudging them until they form a flawless ninety-degree angle against his mousepad. 

He slides open his desk drawer to bury them. 

BAM. 

A heavy, velvet-bound ledger slams down onto the desk, catching the edge of the drawer and slamming it shut. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) stands over him. Her tailored blazer is immaculate. Her posture is a weapon. 

VALERIE
Cancel your afternoon cold-calls. You are transitioning to a bespoke operational role.

Toby flinches, his knee cracking against the underside of the desk.

TOBY
I-- Valerie. I haven't finished the comparative market analysis for the triplex. 

VALERIE
Low-yield busywork. 

Valerie’s eyes dart to the sliver of pink paper protruding from the jammed drawer. 

Toby quickly grabs a ceramic pen cup and places it over the exposed neon paper. 

TOBY
(stuttering)
W-would you like a macchiato? I can run down to the lobby. The espresso machine here is--

VALERIE
Sit still, Toby. 

Valerie glances down at her pristine white cuff. She begins to aggressively rub at an invisible spot near the button. 

VALERIE
(continuing)
I have secured a premier, off-market asset. A modernist marvel. The kind of architectural pedigree that redefines a portfolio. 

TOBY
Off-market? But the broker-dealer bylaws stipulate that all new acquisitions must be logged in the MLS within forty-eight hours to ensure equitable--

VALERIE
Notice the binding on that ledger, Toby. 

Valerie taps the velvet cover, forcing him to look at it. 

VALERIE
(continuing)
Hand-stitched Florentine velvet. It communicates permanence. It commands absolute trust. This isn’t a transaction. It’s a highly classified, invite-only viewing experience. 

Toby stares at the velvet. He reaches out, aligning the ledger so it sits perfectly parallel to his keyboard. 

TOBY
An invite-only experience. Are there... blueprints? I’d really need to review the load-bearing tolerances. If we're hosting a volume of people, the cantilevered stress on a modernist frame--

VALERIE
The structural integrity is flawless. The property is simply hungry for the right demographic. 

Toby pulls his hand back, gripping his own wrist. 

TOBY
I don't know. A secret showing? It feels... circumventive. I want to help people find homes, Valerie. Honestly. 

Valerie stops rubbing her cuff. She leans in, lowering her voice to a velvet purr. 

VALERIE
And you will. This is the pinnacle of elite, ethical real estate. 

Toby looks up. The phrase hits him like a warm blanket. 

VALERIE
(continuing)
We are curating a sanctuary for high-net-worth individuals. I need a concierge. Someone with an uncorrupted, earnest presence to guide them across the threshold. A steward. 

TOBY
A steward. 

VALERIE
A masterclass in client relations. And the concierge fee... 

Valerie reaches out and casually moves the ceramic pen cup, fully exposing the neon pink FINAL NOTICE. 

VALERIE
(continuing)
...will immediately eradicate any temporary liquidity friction you might be experiencing. 

Toby stares at the pink envelope. The threat of eviction. The ghost of his childhood trauma, neatly printed in a windowed envelope. 

He looks back at the velvet ledger. His breathing slows. The stutter vanishes. 

TOBY
I just... guide them inside? 

VALERIE
You open the door. You welcome them home. I handle the closing. 

Toby squares his shoulders. He places his hand flat on the velvet cover. 

TOBY
Okay. Yes. I can do that. An ethical showing. 

Valerie’s lips curve into a razor-thin smile. 

VALERIE
Wear a dark suit tonight, Toby. 

TOBY
(confused)
Dark? Why? 

VALERIE
Aesthetic cohesion. And stains are a liability. 

Valerie turns on her heel and strides down the aisle, her heels clicking a steady, merciless rhythm against the linoleum. 

Toby watches her go. He gently pulls the ledger toward him, opening the heavy cover.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. BROKERAGE BULLPEN (TOBY'S DESK) - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Toby desperately attempts to mask his mounting financial panic behind a wide, overly enthusiastic smile. He stammers out a pledge to handle the clients with the utmost care, eagerly accepting the promotion as his golden ticket out of the bottom-feeding bullpen. Valerie simply nods, her predatory smile widening as she secures her unwitting accomplice, and strides away.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Locks in the B-Story partnership, sealing Toby's fate. (><) Toby's hidden desperation vs. Valerie's flawless trap. (+/-) From insecure and drowning (-) to validated and employed (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. BROKERAGE BULLPEN (TOBY'S DESK) - CONTINUOUS

Fluorescent lights buzz overhead. The bullpen is a cacophony of ringing phones and desperate cold-calls, but at this specific desk, the chaos is fiercely micro-managed.

TOBY HIGGINS (20s) sits rigid. His eyes are locked on his glowing phone screen: "LOAN REPAYMENT: PAST DUE. INITIATING DEFAULT."

His breathing goes shallow. A bead of sweat traces his temple. 

With trembling fingers, Toby places the phone face down. He immediately grabs his stapler, sliding it across the laminate wood until it forms a perfect ninety-degree angle with his tape dispenser. 

He grabs a stack of business cards. Taps them against the desk. Perfectly flush.

A shadow falls over him. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) stands there. Her tailored suit is an armor of sharp lines and aggressive wealth. 

She drops a thick, black leather dossier onto the center of Toby's desk. It scatters his business cards.

VALERIE
(smooth, practiced)
Cancel your afternoon cold-calls, Toby. We are pivoting your trajectory.

Toby flinches, his hands hovering over the ruined symmetry of his desk. He forces a massive, teeth-baring smile.

TOBY
Valerie! Hi. Absolutely. Pivot. I can pivot. What are we, um, pivoting toward?

VALERIE
The Blackwood Estate. I am restructuring the asset's showing protocol. I need a dedicated concierge to manage the overflow of high-net-worth acquisitions.

Toby blinks. His smile remains frozen, but his right hand twitches, inching toward the scattered business cards to straighten them.

TOBY
The Blackwood... me? But Mr. Sterling usually assigns Gemma to the luxury tier. I haven't even finalized my broker's exam.

VALERIE
Gemma operates on a volume-based paradigm. She lacks the bespoke empathy required for this specific demographic. These clients are... unique. They require a delicate touch before transitioning into the property.

Toby's hand finds a rogue business card. He aligns it with the edge of the dossier. 

TOBY
Right. Empathy. I can do empathy. Just to clarify, when you say 'transitioning', is there a standardized protocol for the handover? A specific vetting metric?

Valerie watches a small brown moth flutter erratically around Toby's cheap desk lamp. It bumps against the hot bulb, buzzing in distress.

VALERIE
The protocol is seamless integration. You greet them at the gate. You validate their purchasing power. You walk them to the threshold.

Valerie reaches out. With astonishing gentleness, she cups her hands around the frantic moth, capturing it safely.

VALERIE
(softly)
The house handles the closing.

Toby swallows hard. His eyes dart to his overturned phone. The debt is a crushing weight in his chest.

TOBY
And compensation? I only ask because, well, the administrative base salary is--

VALERIE
Two percent of the gross commission per closed client. Disbursed immediately upon their... successful integration.

Toby does the math. The frozen smile cracks, transforming into a look of genuine, breathless shock.

TOBY
T-two percent? Of a multi-million dollar asset? Valerie, just for walking them to the door? That seems... unprecedented for a junior liaison. 

VALERIE
We are facilitating dreams, Toby. Upward mobility. But if you feel the compensation outpaces your value, I can always ask Gemma to step in.

Toby stands up too fast. His knee slams into the desk. He doesn't even wince. He extends a trembling hand.

TOBY
No! No, I-I can do it. I won't let you down. I pledge to you, I will handle every single client with the utmost care. I'll make sure they feel completely at home.

Valerie looks at his extended hand. She doesn't take it. Instead, she opens her cupped hands toward the ceiling air vent. The moth flutters up, escaping into the dark grate.

She looks back at Toby. Her eyes are dead, but her lips stretch into a flawless, predatory smile.

VALERIE
I know you will, Toby. You are exactly what the property needs.

Valerie turns on her heel. As she strides away through the bustling bullpen, her thumb aggressively scrubs at an invisible stain on her pristine white cuff.

Toby watches her go, his chest heaving with the thrill of salvation. He looks down at the black dossier. 

He carefully aligns it so its edges are perfectly parallel with the edge of the desk.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. BROKERAGE BULLPEN (TOBY'S DESK) - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Left alone, Toby immediately sets to work. His stutter vanishes as he aggressively organizes the new VIP guest list into perfect right angles on his desk. With cheerful precision, he color-codes the dossiers of corrupt politicians and predatory landlords, completely unaware he is drafting a highly specific, gourmet menu for a starving supernatural predator.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Dramatic irony and setup for the escalation. Toby unknowingly curates the monster's meals. (><) Toby's earnest desire for structural order vs. the chaotic, bloody reality of his task. (+/-) From aimless assistant (-) to purposeful, doomed concierge (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. BROKERAGE BULLPEN (TOBY'S DESK) - LATER

The bullpen is a graveyard of empty ergonomic chairs and glowing monitors. 

At a cramped corner desk, TOBY HIGGINS sits frozen. His hands tremble. He stares at a crooked tape dispenser. 

He reaches out. Nudges it. Exactly ninety degrees. 

He exhales. The panic drains from his shoulders. 

Toby unzips his worn briefcase. He pulls out a towering stack of glossy dossiers: THE VIP GUEST LIST. 

He drops them onto the desk. The chaotic thud makes him wince. He immediately squares the edges. 

Toby uncaps a set of highlighters. RED. GREEN. BLUE. He aligns them parallel to the keyboard. 

He opens the first dossier. A bloated, smiling face stares back. 

TOBY
(murmuring)
Councilman Graves. Zoning committee. 

Toby uncaps the red highlighter. He draws a flawless, ruler-straight line under the Councilman's name. 

TOBY
Requires a wide berth. A heavy footprint. 

He moves the dossier to the top right corner of his desk. 

Next folder. A sharp-suited man with dead eyes. 

TOBY
Marcus Vance. Predatory acquisitions. 

Toby’s cheerful mask slips. He stares at the word 'Predatory'. His thumb digs into the edge of the folder, crinkling the expensive cardstock. 

He swallows hard, forcing a bright, empty smile. 

TOBY
Green. For growth. 

He highlights the name in green. Snaps the cap back on. 

Toby clears the center of his desk. He reaches into a cardboard tube and unrolls a massive, complex STRUCTURAL BLUEPRINT of the Modernist Mansion. 

He weighs the corners down with a stapler, a mug, a hole-punch, and a brass paperweight. Perfect symmetry. 

Toby leans over the blueprint. His eyes dart across the schematic, tracing the load-bearing walls, the HVAC flow, the peculiar, windowless voids in the architecture. 

He is in his element. A savant reading sheet music. 

He picks up a stack of color-coded sticky notes. He begins migrating the VIPs from the dossiers to the blueprint. 

TOBY
(rapid-fire, no stutter)
Tech executives in the sunken atrium. The floor joists there... excellent tension. They won't feel crowded. 

He slaps a blue note onto the atrium. 

TOBY
The politicians. The dining room. Naturally. 

He places three red notes over the dining room. 

TOBY
And Mr. Sterling... 

Toby picks up a gold-embossed folder. RICHARD STERLING. He doesn't open it. He just stares at the gold lettering. 

He carefully places Sterling's sticky note in the exact center of the blueprint. The Master Suite. The belly of the house. 

Toby picks up his desk phone. He dials a four-digit extension. It goes to voicemail. 

TOBY
(into phone)
Valerie, it's Toby. I have the gala seating finalized. 

Toby stands up, looking down at his desk. 

TOBY
(into phone)
I reviewed the structural loads. I've placed the heaviest investors where the foundation is... most accommodating. Everything is in order. 

He hangs up. 

From an OVERHEAD ANGLE: Toby’s desk is a masterpiece of geometry. The dossiers of the city's most toxic parasites, perfectly color-coded, laid out like a Michelin-star tasting menu over the blueprint of a starving predator. 

Toby beams, completely at peace. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. BROKERAGE BULLPEN (TOBY'S DESK) - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Toby’s hands move with relentless speed, aligning the color-coded VIP dossiers into perfect right angles on his desk. Valerie enters, her icy professional veneer fully intact. She snatches the meticulously organized stack. Toby stutters a warning about the clients' horrific reputations, compulsively straightening a pen to avoid eye contact. Valerie ignores him, flipping through the 'menu' of slumlords and politicians. She taps a manicured nail against a file, a predatory smirk breaking through her mask as she turns and struts out of the bullpen.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Launch the 'feeding' montage by establishing the curated target list of victims. Micro-Conflict: (><) Toby wants to warn Valerie about the bad clients vs. Valerie wants to exploit them as food. Value Shift: (+/-) Toby goes from focused (+) to anxious (-); Valerie enters desperate (-) and leaves empowered (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. BROKERAGE BULLPEN (TOBY'S DESK) - DAY

Fluorescent lights buzz over a sea of beige cubicles. At the center: TOBY HIGGINS (20s). His hands move with relentless, terrifying speed. 

Thick, color-coded VIP dossiers slide across the laminate wood. Red. Gold. Black. Toby aligns the edges. Perfect ninety-degree angles. He nudges the red folder a millimeter to the left. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) strides into the bullpen. Her icy, tailored blazer projects absolute authority, but her thumb ruthlessly scrubs at an invisible stain on her French cuff. 

She doesn't slow down. She snatches the meticulously organized stack right out from under Toby's hovering hands.

VALERIE
Tell me we have liquidity, Toby.

TOBY
(blinking, hands frozen)
I-- I pulled the exclusive waitlist like you asked. But Valerie, the vetting process...

Valerie flips through the heavy cardstock. Her eyes dart over the net-worth figures. The frantic scrubbing on her cuff slows. 

VALERIE
These are all Tier-One buyers. Exceptional liquidity.

TOBY
They're-- they're highly problematic assets.

Toby’s hands drop to his desk. He grabs a silver fountain pen. Aligns it perfectly parallel to his keyboard. He refuses to look up at her.

TOBY
I cross-referenced the public records. The man on top, Marcus Vance-- no relation, obviously-- he's...

Valerie pauses on the top file. A glossy photo of a bloated, grinning politician. 

VALERIE
A motivated buyer with a robust acquisition strategy.

TOBY
He rezoned three low-income districts last month. Displaced four hundred families to build a golf course. And the woman in the black folder--

Toby reaches out, desperate to take the files back. Valerie smoothly pivots, stepping out of his reach. She inspects a dying fern on the corner of his desk. 

VALERIE
Your foliage is suffering from root rot, Toby. You’re overwatering.

TOBY
(stuttering)
S-she owns a shell company. Buys up subsidized housing and shuts off the heating during winter to force evictions. Valerie, these aren't just difficult clients. They're... they're predators.

Valerie stops. The word hangs in the sterile office air. Evictions. 

A tiny, exhausted honeybee crawls weakly across the edge of Toby's monitor. Valerie extends a manicured finger. The bee climbs aboard. She gently, almost tenderly, lowers the insect into the dry soil of the fern. 

VALERIE
In this market, Toby, you are either the concrete or the bulldozer.

TOBY
I could get you a coffee? A macchiato? We can look at the secondary list. The commercial developers. They have clean capital.

Valerie ignores the offer. She looks back down at the black folder. The slumlord. The evictor. 

Her thumb stops scrubbing her cuff entirely. The desperation in her posture evaporates, replaced by a chilling, absolute stillness. 

VALERIE
Clean capital doesn't appreciate the unique architectural footprint of the property. This house requires a very specific... appetite.

Toby nudges his mousepad. A perfect square. His voice drops to an anxious whisper. 

TOBY
I don't understand why we're targeting them.

Valerie taps her manicured index finger against the black folder. Tap. Tap. Tap. 

A slow, predatory smirk breaks through her icy veneer. 

VALERIE
Because they deserve an exclusive showing.

She turns on her heel. The sharp clack of her stilettos echoes like gunfire as she struts out of the bullpen, taking the menu of monsters with her. 

Toby stares at the empty space on his desk. He reaches out, his hand trembling slightly, and straightens a loose paperclip.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie leads a sweating, notorious SLUMLORD (55) into the brutalist foyer. The Slumlord loudly complains about the acoustics, treating the luxury space with utter disdain. Valerie maintains a flawless, icy smile, pivoting the conversation away from his complaints to the aesthetic brilliance of the custom glass elevator. She gestures gracefully, insisting he step inside first to truly appreciate the craftsmanship.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Set up the first deliberate kill of the sequence. Micro-Conflict: (><) The Slumlord wants to belittle the property vs. Valerie wants to maneuver him into the trap. Value Shift: (+/-) Slumlord enters arrogant (+) to intrigued (+); Valerie enters annoyed (-) but leaves eagerly positioned (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - DAY

A brutalist cathedral of poured concrete and shadow. 

Footsteps slap against the polished stone, echoing with deafening hostility. 

LEONARD (55), a notorious slumlord sweating entirely through a wrinkled linen suit, sneers at the cavernous ceiling. 

LEONARD
Sounds like a damn parking garage. You drop a pin in here, it’s gonna wake up the neighbors. If there were any.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) glides beside him. Her tailored suit is armor. Her smile is a terrifyingly flawless, icy mask. 

VALERIE
It’s deliberate monolithic minimalism, Leonard. The acoustics are engineered to emphasize the sheer, unbridled volume of your domain. 

Leonard scoffs, aggressively wiping his damp forehead with a crumpled handkerchief. He drags a greasy hand along a pristine concrete pillar, treating it like cheap drywall in a Section 8 walk-up. 

LEONARD
It’s cold. Walls are bare. Can’t even drive a nail into this to hang a television. Who wants to pay fifty million to live in a bunker?

Valerie’s smile remains frozen. Her right thumb aggressively, imperceptibly scrubs at an invisible stain on her left silk cuff. 

She pauses. On the floor, a large, exhausted stag beetle struggles on its back, legs kicking at the empty air. 

Without breaking stride, Valerie gently slides the toe of her Louboutin under the beetle, flipping it upright and nudging it safely into the shadow of a planter. 

VALERIE
A bunker implies defense. This property projects absolute, untouchable authority. It filters out the noise of the desperate.

Leonard snorts. He stops, hands on his hips, clearly bored. 

LEONARD
Look, sweetie. I buy properties that bleed. Subdivisions, strip malls. Places where people have no choice but to pay me. This? This is a dead asset. I’m out.

He turns toward the massive front door. 

Valerie steps into his path. The icy smile widens. 

VALERIE
Then let’s bypass the living spaces. I want to show you the kinetic centerpiece. The ultimate return on investment.

She pivots gracefully, extending an arm toward the center of the foyer. 

Rising from the basement floor, slicing up through the brutalist stairwell, is a breathtaking custom elevator. It is a perfect cylinder of seamless, curved glass, glowing faintly from within like a deep-sea organism. 

VALERIE
The pneumatic lift. Bespoke, hand-blown Venetian glass. Frictionless descent. 

Leonard squints at it, unimpressed. 

LEONARD
It’s a tube. 

VALERIE
It is an architectural marvel that independently appraised for two point four million dollars last week.

Leonard freezes. The sweat on his brow seems to cool. The number hangs in the cavernous room, echoing perfectly. 

LEONARD
Two million? For a glass tube?

VALERIE
Two point four. 

He steps toward it, drawn by the gravity of the price tag. He circles the glass, tapping it with a thick, gold-ringed finger. 

LEONARD
Seems fragile. 

VALERIE
It is virtually indestructible. I insist you step inside. You cannot truly comprehend the craftsmanship until you experience the isolation.

Leonard hesitates, then hits the call button. The curved glass doors slide open with a soft, wet hiss. 

He steps into the cylindrical chamber. It is a tight fit. He turns back to Valerie, expecting her to follow. 

LEONARD
You coming?

Valerie remains perfectly still on the concrete floor. Her hands clasp neatly in front of her. 

VALERIE
I’ll take the stairs. The hydraulic calibration is strictly curated for the primary resident. 

Leonard frowns, confused by the jargon. 

LEONARD
The hell does that mean?

VALERIE
It means the house prefers to dine alone. 

Before Leonard can process the sentence, the glass doors snap shut. Not a hiss, but a violent, airtight seal. 

Leonard jumps. He hits the button. Nothing happens. 

He glares through the glass at Valerie. His muffled voice barely penetrates the thick Venetian shell. 

LEONARD
(muffled)
Hey! Open this thing! 

Valerie’s icy smile drops. Her face goes completely dead. She watches him pound his fists against the two-million-dollar glass.

The elevator begins its descent, pulling the screaming slumlord down into the pitch-black belly of the house. 

Valerie turns her back to the shaft, calmly checking her pristine cuffs. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GLASS ELEVATOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The Slumlord steps inside. The glass doors glide shut. Valerie remains safely in the foyer. Suddenly, the elevator's steel cables shriek with unnatural tension. The glass walls violently shrink inward. The Slumlord screams, slamming his fists against the glass before being crushed into a compact cube of twisted metal and shattered glass. Valerie does not flinch; she calmly watches the blood pool, checking her reflection in the remaining intact glass.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Execute the first kill of the montage, proving the house is a reliable, lethal weapon. Micro-Conflict: (><) The Slumlord wants to survive vs. the house wants to consume him. Value Shift: (+/-) Slumlord goes from arrogant (+) to dead (-). Valerie goes from waiting (-) to victorious (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GLASS ELEVATOR - CONTINUOUS

The SLUMLORD (50s, cheap suit, reeking of stale cigar smoke and unearned confidence) steps into the pristine, transparent belly of the glass elevator. 

Valerie remains safely outside in the cavernous foyer. Her posture is immaculate. Her hands are neatly clasped over her tablet. 

SLUMLORD
(scoffing)
HOA fees are a joke, sweetheart. I buy it, I gut the load-bearing walls. Subdivide the master suite into four micro-units. Rent to desperate undergrads. Maximum yield. 

VALERIE
A truly visionary approach to square footage. 

SLUMLORD
You're damn right it is. Send the paperwork to my girl. 

He hits the glowing floor button. 

The heavy glass doors GLIDE shut. A vacuum seal of silence. 

Valerie does not move. She watches him through the glass. 

Inside the cab, the Slumlord taps his leather wingtip impatiently. He looks up. Frowns. 

The elevator isn't moving. 

High-tensile steel cables suddenly SHRIEK. It is an agonizing, unnatural frequency that vibrates through the imported marble floorboards. 

The Slumlord hits the button again. Harder. 

SLUMLORD
(muffled through glass)
Hey! Your toy box is stuck!

The architectural geometry of the house shifts. 

The four glass walls of the elevator violently SHRINK INWARD. The ceiling drops. The floor rises. 

The Slumlord's eyes go wide. He drops his leather briefcase. He slams his meaty fists against the reinforced glass. 

SLUMLORD
Hey! Open the damn doors! OPEN THE--

The walls accelerate. 

The glass does not shatter. It folds. A grotesque, sentient hydraulic press. 

The Slumlord's screams are abruptly silenced by a wet, catastrophic CRUNCH. 

Bones snap like dry timber. Blood violently splatters against the transparent walls, painting the interior a thick, opaque crimson. 

Then, dead silence. 

The elevator cab has been violently compressed into a dense, perfect three-foot cube of twisted chrome, shattered safety glass, and pulverized flesh. 

Valerie stands in the foyer. She does not flinch. Her breathing remains steady, measured. 

A thick, dark pool of blood seeps from the bottom of the cube, creeping slowly toward the elevator track. 

Valerie steps closer to the macabre terrarium. She looks down at the carnage. 

She raises her eyes, finding the only remaining intact pane of glass on the exterior door. 

Valerie stares at her own reflection over the bloodbath. 

Her icy facade holds. She calmly smooths a stray hair behind her ear. 

She looks down at her wrists and obsessively adjusts the links on her expensive French cuffs, scrubbing her thumb over an invisible stain on the fabric. 

Perfect. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION / EXT. LUXURY BOUTIQUE - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie expertly pries open the crushed elevator doors with a crowbar, reaching into the wreckage to retrieve a pristine, golf-ball-sized diamond. MATCH CUT: The diamond drops onto a jeweler's velvet tray. Valerie strides out of a high-end boutique wearing a bespoke designer suit. She tosses a heavy gold coin to a valet, who hands her the keys to a brand-new luxury sports car. She revs the engine, her face glowing with euphoric invincibility.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Demonstrate the immense financial reward of her dark pact. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie wants to instantly convert murder into wealth vs. the slow pace of normal transactions. Value Shift: (+/-) Valerie enters the jeweler anxious about the diamond's authenticity (-) and leaves dripping in undeniable, arrogant wealth (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - DAY

The heavy steel doors of the private elevator are crushed inward, crumpled like a discarded soda can. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) stands before the wreckage. Her normally immaculate blouse is torn at the shoulder. She is breathing hard, sweat beading on her forehead. 

The house around her emits a low, almost imperceptible hum. A satisfied settling of the foundation.

Valerie wedges a heavy steel crowbar into the mangled seam of the elevator doors. She throws her entire body weight against the iron bar. 

The metal groans but holds fast. 

Valerie stumbles back, gasping. Panic flashes in her eyes. She looks down at her right wrist and begins to obsessively, aggressively scrub her thumb against her frayed cuff, trying to erase an invisible stain. 

She stares at the crushed doors. She needs this to be real. 

Valerie grips the crowbar again. She plants her expensive heels against the marble floor, leveraging her back, and HEAVES. 

With a deafening SCREECH, the steel doors buckle and snap apart, leaving a jagged two-foot gap.

Valerie drops the crowbar. She carefully reaches her arm into the dark, jagged maw of the elevator shaft. 

Inside the wreckage, resting on a shredded scrap of Arthur Pendelton’s gaudy luxury blazer, sits a pristine, golf-ball-sized DIAMOND. 

Valerie’s trembling fingers close around it. She pulls it out into the light. 

The stone is impossibly clear, catching the sunlight pouring through the floor-to-ceiling windows and scattering a kaleidoscope of prisms across Valerie’s exhausted face. 

MATCH CUT TO:

INT. LUXURY BOUTIQUE - DAY

The diamond THUDS heavily onto a black velvet tray. 

GIDEON (60s), a meticulously groomed jeweler, lowers his jeweler's loupe. His hands are visibly shaking. 

GIDEON
(hushed)
No inclusions. Zero carbon anomalies. The clarity is... it defies natural geology. Where did you acquire a piece of this magnitude?

VALERIE
It was a distressed asset. A highly motivated seller. 

Valerie stands on the opposite side of the glass display case. She is still in her ruined blouse, but her posture is rigid, projecting absolute icy authority. 

GIDEON
There is no GIA laser inscription. No provenance paperwork. To properly appraise this, I must send it to the lab in Antwerp. It will take four to six weeks to--

Valerie’s jaw tightens. Her left hand immediately drops to her right wrist. She begins to scrub at the frayed cuff. 

VALERIE
Weeks is a stagnant listing, Gideon. I am not interested in letting a prime asset sit on the market. 

GIDEON
Ms. Vance, the protocols for a stone of this--

VALERIE
(interrupting)
Notice the light dispersion. The structural integrity is flawless. It’s a turnkey investment, and I am offering you exclusive representation. 

Gideon wipes his brow with a silk handkerchief. He stares at the diamond, intoxicated by it. 

GIDEON
What exactly are you proposing?

VALERIE
A bridge loan. Against the equity of the stone. I need seven figures, loaded onto a limitless corporate card, activated today. You hold the property in escrow until your lab in Antwerp catches up to reality. 

GIDEON
(stammering)
That is highly irregular. The underwriting alone--

VALERIE
Irregular is just another word for an off-market opportunity. 

Valerie leans over the glass, her eyes locking onto Gideon's. She stops scrubbing her cuff. 

VALERIE
Do you want the commission, Gideon? Or should I take my portfolio to the boutique across the street?

Gideon looks at the diamond. He looks at Valerie's ruined clothes, then back at the diamond. The greed wins. 

He reaches under the counter and slides a heavy, matte-black metal credit card across the glass. 

EXT. LUXURY BOUTIQUE - DAY

The glass doors of the boutique swing open. 

Valerie strides out onto the sun-drenched sidewalk. The torn blouse is gone. She is now wearing a bespoke, razor-sharp designer suit that radiates lethal sophistication. 

A VALET (20s) stands at the curb, holding a leather key fob. Beside him idles a brand-new, shark-like LUXURY SPORTS CAR. It purrs like a caged predator. 

Valerie approaches. She doesn't break stride. She reaches into her tailored pocket, pulls out a heavy, solid-gold Krugerrand coin, and flicks it through the air. 

The gold coin flashes in the sunlight. 

The Valet catches it, his eyes going wide at the sheer weight of the metal. 

VALET
Uh, thank you, ma'am! 

He scrambles to hand her the keys. 

Valerie slides into the driver's seat. The smell of untouched, expensive leather fills the cabin. She grips the steering wheel, feeling the immaculate stitching under her fingertips. 

She presses the ignition button. 

The engine ROARS to life, a deep, violent sound that rattles the boutique windows. 

Valerie looks at herself in the rearview mirror. The desperate, drowning woman from this morning is dead. A slow, euphoric smile spreads across her face—the undeniable, arrogant glow of total invincibility. 

She slams the car into gear and peels out into traffic. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - INFINITY POOL - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie guides a deeply corrupt, arrogant MAYOR (60) out to the property's breathtaking, cliffside infinity pool. The Mayor kicks off his shoes and dips his bare feet into the crystal-clear water, loudly bragging about his latest embezzlement scheme. Valerie stands several feet back from the edge, crossing her arms. She watches the water intently, waiting for the surface to change.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Escalate the feeding frenzy with a second, distinct kill. Micro-Conflict: (><) The Mayor wants to brag and relax vs. Valerie wants to keep him in the strike zone. Value Shift: (+/-) Mayor goes from boastful (+) to slightly confused (-). Valerie goes from impatient (-) to eagerly anticipating the kill (+).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - INFINITY POOL - DAY

The sheer cliff drops three hundred feet into a jagged canyon. Perched on the very edge: a breathtaking infinity pool. The water is impossibly still, a perfect mirror reflecting the cloudless sky.

The MAYOR (60s, a walking heart attack squeezed into a bespoke linen suit) struts onto the sun-baked travertine patio. 

MAYOR
...so I told the zoning committee, if we’re reallocating the pension surplus, we might as well reallocate it into something tangible. Right? 

VALERIE VANCE follows a few paces behind. Her tailored blazer is immaculate. Her arms are tightly crossed.

VALERIE
A highly strategic pivot. Maximizing dormant assets.

MAYOR
Exactly. Why let it sit in a municipal fund when my brother-in-law’s contracting firm can lay fresh asphalt in District 9? It’s public service, Valerie. 

He reaches the edge of the pool. Stops.

MAYOR
God almighty. Look at that drop.

Valerie halts exactly four feet from the wet stone. Safe distance. 

VALERIE
The architect prioritized a dominant sightline. You own the horizon. Notice the seamless integration of the basalt tiling along the waterline.

The Mayor isn't looking at the tiling. He kicks off his Italian loafers. 

MAYOR
My podiatrist says I need to decompress. The city is bleeding me dry. Every union rep wants a piece of my flesh.

He unceremoniously plops down on the edge of the pool, hiking up his linen trousers. 

Valerie’s jaw tightens. Her thumb finds the edge of her silk cuff, obsessively scrubbing at an invisible stain. 

The Mayor dangles his pale, hairy legs over the edge. His bare feet break the surface of the water. 

Valerie stops scrubbing. She watches the ripples radiate outward. Waiting.

VALERIE
You carry a heavy burden. You deserve a sanctuary that protects your... investments.

MAYOR
(grunting)
Damn right. 

He kicks his feet. The splash is sluggish. 

MAYOR
Water's thick. Feels almost like a hot spring. Is it heated?

VALERIE
Geothermal regulation. The property intuitively adjusts to the immediate needs of the occupant.

The Mayor swishes his calves. The water sloshes, but the sound is muted. Heavy. 

MAYOR
A bit too warm, actually. Makes my skin prickle. 

He plants his hands on the stone, preparing to push himself up. 

MAYOR
Let's head inside. Show me this subterranean wine cellar.

Valerie’s eyes dart to his lifting legs. The water hasn't changed yet. She is losing him.

VALERIE
We will. But you haven't experienced the cantilevered acoustics yet.

The Mayor pauses, halfway up. 

MAYOR
The what?

VALERIE
Lean out. Just over the infinity edge. 

She takes a single, measured step forward. 

VALERIE
The basin captures the updraft from the canyon. If you position yourself correctly, the wind actually harmonizes. It’s an acoustic monopoly. Completely irreplicable. 

The Mayor considers this. His ego cannot resist a monopoly. 

Slowly, he lowers his weight back onto the stone. He slides his legs deeper into the pool, right up to the knees. He leans forward, hovering over the terrifying drop.

MAYOR
Harmonizes, huh? I paid three million for a home theater that just rattles my floorboards. 

He closes his eyes, listening to the canyon breeze. 

Behind him, Valerie doesn't look at the view. She stares directly into the pool. 

The crystal-clear water around the Mayor’s submerged calves slowly begins to darken. A faint, rhythmic pulsing ripples the surface. 

Thump. Thump.

Valerie uncrosses her arms. Her posture straightens. The icy veneer gives way to a faint, hungry smirk. 

MAYOR
I don't hear any wind, Valerie. 

VALERIE
Give it a moment. The environment is just... acclimating to you. 

The water at his knees shifts from liquid to a viscous, gelatinous resin. It grips his skin. 

MAYOR
(frowning)
Actually, my legs are going numb. What kind of saline system is this? 

He tries to pull his left leg out of the water. 

It doesn't move. 

MAYOR
Hey. 

He yanks harder. Tendons bulge in his neck. The resin stretches, pulling his flesh downward. 

MAYOR
What the hell is this? Valerie!

He twists back in a panic, clawing at the wet stone. 

Valerie remains anchored four feet away. She smooths a non-existent wrinkle from her blazer. 

VALERIE
It’s a seller’s market, Mr. Mayor. Try not to splash.

The dark resin violently surges up his thighs, dragging him backward toward the edge.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - INFINITY POOL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The pool water instantly thickens into a sentient, gelatinous mass. It wraps around the Mayor's ankles like a vice. He thrashes wildly, screaming for Valerie to grab his hand. Valerie simply takes another step back to avoid getting splashed. The mass violently drags the Mayor into the deep end. The surface quickly smooths over into perfect, glass-like stillness. Valerie calmly walks over to the pool's skimmer basket, lifting the lid to extract another massive diamond.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Solidify the house's creative lethality and Valerie's total loss of empathy. Micro-Conflict: (><) The Mayor wants to be saved vs. Valerie wants her payment. Value Shift: (+/-) Mayor goes from alive (+) to consumed (-). Valerie goes from observing (-) to richer (+).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - INFINITY POOL - CONTINUOUS

The infinity pool's crystal-clear surface SHUDDERS. 

The MAYOR (60s, dripping in false tan and entitlement) stands waist-deep near the edge. He tries to lift his leg. It doesn't break the surface. 

The water around his calves isn't water anymore. It is thick. Gelatinous. Like industrial resin. 

MAYOR
Valerie! The hell is wrong with the filtration system? Get over here!

VALERIE (30s, immaculate tailored suit) stands safely on the dry deck. She checks her reflection in the glass facade of the house, casually adjusting her collar.

VALERIE
It's a zero-edge design, Mayor. Seamless transition between the architecture and the horizon. Truly immersive.

The gelatinous mass creeps up the Mayor's thighs. He thrashes, slapping the heavy liquid. It doesn't splash. It absorbs the impact, pulling him an inch deeper.

MAYOR
It's pulling my legs! Grab my hand!

He lunges toward the coping, extending a desperate, heavy-ringed hand. 

Valerie looks at his outstretched fingers. Then down at her pristine silk trousers. 

A drop of thick pool-sludge lands inches from her Italian leather pumps. 

Valerie takes a deliberate step back.

VALERIE
(calm)
We actually just had the coping resealed. Imported travertine. Highly porous.

The mass snaps around the Mayor's waist like a vice. He GASPS, his chest slamming against the coping. 

MAYOR
I'm sinking, you stupid bitch! Grab my hand or I will personally bury your entire agency!

VALERIE
The seller is remarkably firm on the terms of the showing. 

Underneath her icy composure, Valerie's right thumb aggressively rubs at an invisible stain on her left French cuff. Scrubbing. Scrubbing.

The Mayor thrashes harder, his fingernails scraping bloody trails across the travertine stone. The gelatinous grip tightens, crushing the air from his lungs. 

A sickening CRACK echoes from beneath the surface. 

His threats turn into a wet, suffocated gurgle. 

The pool violently YANKS him backward into the deep end. 

He goes under. 

The surface churns for three seconds--a chaotic blender of tailored fabric, flailing limbs, and thick, heavy liquid. 

Then... stillness. 

The water thins out. The gelatinous mass dissolves. In seconds, the infinity pool is perfect, glass-like, reflecting the twilight sky. Not a single ripple.

Valerie stops scrubbing her cuff. She exhales, smoothing the fabric flat. 

She walks along the edge of the pool, her heels clicking rhythmically against the stone. 

She stops at the skimmer basket embedded in the deck. 

Kneels. Lifts the plastic lid. 

Inside, nestled among a few dry eucalyptus leaves, rests a flawless, golf-ball-sized DIAMOND. It gleams with pristine, blood-free clarity. 

Valerie plucks it out. Holds it up to the dying light. 

VALERIE
(murmuring)
Unbeatable yield.

She drops the diamond into her blazer pocket.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. RICHARD STERLING'S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie kicks open the heavy doors to Richard Sterling's cavernous office. Richard sits behind his desk, projecting unbothered dominance. He smiles calmly, beginning a pseudo-philosophical threat about her job security. Valerie cuts him off by slamming a heavy metal briefcase onto his desk. The latches pop, revealing stacks of hundred-dollar bills. Richard's sophisticated facade breaks; he leans forward, invading her space, but Valerie simply turns her back on him and walks out, leaving him speechless.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Fulfill Valerie's initial conscious desire (financial freedom and dominance over her boss). Micro-Conflict: (><) Richard wants to maintain his predatory control over Valerie vs. Valerie wants to buy her way out of his thumb. Value Shift: (+/-) Richard enters dominant (+) and is left humiliated (-). Valerie enters as a subordinate (-) and leaves as an untouchable queen (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. RICHARD STERLING'S OFFICE - DAY

Heavy mahogany doors FLY OPEN off a vicious kick. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s, sharp, draped in defensive couture) strides into the cavernous room. Her grip is white-knuckled around the handle of a heavy, brushed-steel briefcase. 

Behind a massive expanse of black glass desk sits RICHARD STERLING (50s, tailored suit, predatory stillness). 

He doesn't flinch at the booming doors. He is hunched over a meticulously detailed, 1:12 scale Victorian dollhouse. Using silver tweezers, he delicately places a microscopic porcelain teacup onto a miniature dining table.

RICHARD
(calmly)
The beauty of a miniature, Valerie, is absolute control. The architecture bends to the creator. No rogue elements. No structural rot.

Valerie stops dead in the center of the Persian rug. She stares at the back of his perfectly combed head.

VALERIE
I’m restructuring my position at this firm.

Richard finally sets the tweezers down. He swivels his leather chair around, steepling his fingers. A placid, chilling smile spreads across his face.

RICHARD
You don't restructure gravity. You simply survive it. Or you are crushed by it.

He gestures vaguely to the empty chairs in front of his desk. She doesn't sit.

RICHARD
I’ve been reviewing the analytics on the modernist property. You’re withholding viewings. Stalling. The ecosystem of this firm relies on apex performance. When a worker bee hoards the pollen, the hive starves.

Valerie’s eyes dart to the dollhouse. 

VALERIE
Mahogany shingles. Authentic Victorian crown molding. The appraisal value on a piece like that must be staggering. Though, historically, miniatures yield a terrible return on investment.

Richard’s smile doesn’t reach his eyes. 

RICHARD
Deflection is a prey response. I am taking the exclusive listing, Valerie. Effective immediately.

Valerie’s thumb twitches. She instinctively begins scrubbing at an invisible stain on her pristine silk cuff. A frantic, microscopic motion. 

VALERIE
The seller signed exclusively with me. The deed, the escrow, the title-- it’s all under my LLC. 

RICHARD
(softly)
You are a drowning woman holding a very heavy, very shiny rock. I am offering to take it from you before you sink to the bottom.

He leans forward, resting his forearms on the glass. 

RICHARD
Hand over the keys. Or I will personally ensure your license is revoked by the state board before lunch. You will be blacklisted from every agency in this time zone. You'll be back on the street, begging for scraps.

Valerie stares at him. Her thumb stops scrubbing her cuff. The frantic energy evaporates, replaced by absolute, sub-zero ice.

She steps up to the black glass desk.

She hoists the heavy steel briefcase and SLAMS it down. 

The impact rattles the desk. Inside the dollhouse, the microscopic porcelain teacup tips over. 

Richard blinks. The calm smile falters.

Valerie pops the left latch. CLACK.
She pops the right latch. CLACK.

She flips the lid open. 

Row after row of tightly banded, immaculate, hundred-dollar bills. A mountain of cash. Blood-free diamond money, liquidated into undeniable leverage.

Richard’s sophisticated veneer shatters. The color drains from his face. 

He shoots up from his chair, rounding the desk in two massive strides. He invades her personal space, looming over her, his chest inches from her face.

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
What is this? What the hell did you do, Valerie? Who did you steal this from?

Valerie doesn't step back. She looks down at the cash, then up into his terrified eyes.

VALERIE
Consider it a premium buyout of my non-compete clause. Market value for my absolute autonomy. 

RICHARD
(trembling)
You can't just buy your way out of--

VALERIE
The market has shifted, Richard. You're no longer the apex.

She turns her back on him. 

Valerie marches toward the open doors, her heels clicking a steady, victorious rhythm against the hardwood. 

Richard stands frozen over the briefcase, dwarfed by the cash, his mouth opening and closing without a sound.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie escorts a vile, elderly BILLIONAIRE (80) into the mansion's grand foyer. The Billionaire sneers at the modernist architecture, aggressively tapping his gold-tipped cane against the hardwood floors. Valerie doesn't even bother with her usual sanitized sales pitch. She simply crosses her arms, flashing a smug, expectant smile, mentally calculating the size of the diamond this massive meal will produce.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Set up the Midpoint false victory and the expectation of another easy meal. Micro-Conflict: (><) The Billionaire wants to complain and dominate the space vs. Valerie wants him to stand still and get eaten. Value Shift: (+/-) Billionaire enters aggressive (+) to irritated (-). Valerie enters supremely confident (+) to annoyed he isn't dying fast enough (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - DAY

THWACK. 

A heavy, gold-tipped cane strikes the pristine Brazilian walnut floor. 

The BILLIONAIRE (80) sneers. Liver-spotted, draped in a bespoke suit that costs more than a car, he radiates vile entitlement. He squints at the soaring, geometric ceiling of the massive, echoing space.

VALERIE VANCE stands perfectly still by the frosted-glass front door. She doesn't rush to take his coat. She doesn't offer her usual sanitized, breathless sales pitch. 

She simply crosses her arms. A smug, expectant smile plays on her lips. Her eyes track him, scanning his frail, wealthy frame, mentally weighing the carat size he is about to produce. 

BILLIONAIRE
It echoes. I sneeze in the master bath, the maid hears it in the kitchen. Where's the warmth? It feels like a damn clinic.

VALERIE
(coolly)
It’s a brutalist masterpiece. The architect designed the space to consume all unnecessary distractions. 

The Billionaire paces. He drags his cane along the baseboards. Disrespectful. Destructive. 

Valerie watches the floorboards beneath his expensive leather shoes. She waits for the familiar softening of the wood. The subtle unhinging of the architecture. 

Nothing happens. The floor remains completely solid. 

BILLIONAIRE
Consume. Nonsense. It’s a concrete box for tech-infants who don't know what crown molding is. Look at this lighting. Harsh. Unforgiving. 

He points his cane at a massive, jagged chandelier hanging directly over the center of the room.

BILLIONAIRE
My third wife would have hung herself from that fixture just to escape the glare.

VALERIE
The recessed lighting offers unparalleled exposure. It ensures nothing remains hidden. Complete transparency.

BILLIONAIRE
(scoffs)
Transparency is for the poor. I pay for walls. Thick ones. 

He taps a marble console table with his cane. CLINK. 

BILLIONAIRE
Imported? Looks local. Looks cheap. 

Valerie’s smile tightens. She glances at the ceiling. A faint groan of settling pipes echoes through the walls, but the house remains frustratingly dormant. 

Annoyance flares behind her eyes. She drops her arms, her right hand instinctively moving to the cuff of her silk blouse. She rubs the fabric. Hard. 

VALERIE
It's a bespoke piece. Sourced directly from the earth. Very sustainable. 

BILLIONAIRE
I don't care about the earth. I care about equity. 

He turns his back to her, wandering toward the hallway. Skirting the edges of the room. Staying entirely out of the dead center of the foyer. 

Valerie stops rubbing her cuff. She checks her gold Cartier watch. A subtle, impatient tap of her heel against the floorboards. 

BILLIONAIRE
You're awfully quiet. Usually, you bloodsuckers are practically tap-dancing for a commission this size. Offering me champagne. Laughing at my terrible jokes. 

Valerie forces a tight, utterly hollow smile, stepping forward to draw his attention. 

VALERIE
I prefer to let the architecture speak for itself. It has a very commanding presence. 

BILLIONAIRE
It's mute. And frankly, so are you. 

He pivots, taking a definitive step toward the front door. Away from the sweet spot. 

BILLIONAIRE
Get my driver on the phone. We're leaving. 

Valerie’s meal is walking away. She steps directly into his path, blocking the heavy oak door. 

VALERIE
Leaving so soon? We haven't even discussed the subterranean features. 

BILLIONAIRE
Move, girl. Before I make a phone call and have your real estate license shredded. 

Valerie raises her chin. She drops the customer-service facade entirely, her eyes turning to ice. 

VALERIE
Of course. I should have known. 

BILLIONAIRE
Known what?

VALERIE
That the sheer scale of this property would be too intimidating for you. It requires a buyer with true vision. Someone who isn't afraid to be swallowed up by their own legacy. 

The Billionaire stops dead in his tracks. His face flushes a dangerous, mottled purple. 

VALERIE
We have smaller, more traditional listings. For clients who prefer to feel insulated. Safe. I completely understand if a property of this magnitude is simply out of your depth. 

BILLIONAIRE
(low, guttural)
Out of my depth?

He aggressively closes the distance. He marches right back to the dead center of the foyer, directly beneath the jagged chandelier, planting his cane firmly between them. 

THWACK. 

He leans in, invading her personal space, his sour breath washing over her face. 

BILLIONAIRE
Listen to me, you glorified tour guide. I own the ground you stand on. I own the air you breathe. I could buy you, this house, and your entire pathetic lineage with pocket change. 

Valerie doesn't flinch. Her eyes dart down to the floor beneath his imported leather shoes. 

The Brazilian walnut planks begin to warp. The wood grain shifts, swirling like thick, dark syrup. 

A low, guttural hum vibrates through the soles of Valerie's heels. 

Her icy, smug smile returns. 

VALERIE
You certainly do. It’s a completely immersive investment. 

The Billionaire opens his mouth to bark another insult. 

The floor beneath him violently drops two inches. 

He stumbles, his gold cane sinking straight into the solid wood as if it were quicksand. He looks down, his aggressive sneer melting into pure, unadulterated terror. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Instead of the floor smoothly opening, the hardwood boards violently buckle and heave. A deafening, sickly groan reverberates through the walls. The house violently regurgitates the Billionaire, launching his bruised and terrified body through the air. He crashes hard against the marble foyer floor. Valerie's smug smile vanishes. She stumbles backward, her arrogant posture collapsing into sudden, jarring confusion.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Deliver the Midpoint twist—the monster changes the rules. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie wants the house to consume the client vs. the house rejecting the tainted meal. Value Shift: (+/-) Valerie goes from arrogant control (+) to sudden, jarring confusion (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS

Arthur stands dead center in the vast, minimalist expanse. He aggressively taps his foot against the seamless hardwood. 

ARTHUR
(booming)
I don't know, Vance. The feng shui is totally neutered. I bring Elon or Zuck in here for a tasting, they're going to think I went soft. It lacks a certain... bite.

Valerie stands a few feet away, her posture a weapon of perfectly curated angles. A smug, icy smile touches her lips.

VALERIE
The Brazilian cherrywood is imported, Arthur. It’s designed for a completely seamless transition. Just stand right there. Let the space absorb you.

The floor beneath Arthur trembles. 

Valerie clasps her hands together. Her eyes gleam with dark anticipation. She waits for the drop. The meal. The diamonds.

But the floor doesn't slide open. 

It BUCKLES.

A deafening, sickly GROAN reverberates through the load-bearing walls. It sounds wet. Deeply nauseating. 

The floorboards splinter and HEAVE upward. 

Arthur is violently REGURGITATED. 

The house launches him into the air. He flies backward, a flailing, bruised mass of bespoke cashmere, and crashes hard against the Calacatta marble.

Valerie’s smug smile vanishes. 

She stumbles backward, her heels clicking erratically against the stone. Her arrogant posture collapses into sudden, jarring confusion.

ARTHUR
(voice cracking)
What the hell was that?!

Arthur scrambles away from the splintered epicenter, terrified, a thin line of blood trickling down his forehead. 

His trembling fingers snap to his wrist. He obsessively taps the face of his Richard Mille watch, his wild eyes staring at the glass without registering the time at all.

VALERIE
(breathless)
It’s... structural settling. 

ARTHUR
Settling?! The floor just threw me!

A low, rumbling GURGLE echoes from the crawlspace beneath them. 

The house spits up a single, saliva-coated piece of splintered wood. It smacks against the marble, right next to Arthur’s knee. 

Valerie’s breathing turns shallow. Her fingers twitch. She looks down at her wrists and aggressively scrubs at the edge of her pristine white cuff, trying to erase a stain that isn't there.

VALERIE
(scrubbing harder)
The subfloor tension on these cantilevered builds can be highly volatile. A minor atmospheric pressure shift. I can easily factor a credit into the closing costs to replace the damaged planks.

Arthur wheezes, clutching his bruised ribs. He stares at the jagged, gaping maw of splintered wood in the center of the room. 

The broken planks slowly, rhythmically rise and fall.

ARTHUR
(stammering)
Vance... it's breathing.

Valerie stops scrubbing her cuff. She stares at the ruined floor, then at the terrified, very much alive billionaire. 

The house rejected the meal.

> SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Every heavy exterior door slams shut with bone-rattling force. Deadbolts slide into place with a synchronized, metallic snap. Valerie lunges for the exit, violently rattling the brass handles, but they refuse to budge. The pristine walls begin to pulsate with a sickening, organic rhythm. A clear, viscous sap bleeds from the recessed lighting fixtures, dripping onto the floorboards. The sap slowly pools, shifting to form the word 'TOBY' in jagged, glowing letters. Valerie stares at the name, absolute horror washing over her face as she obsessively scrubs at invisible stains on her expensive cuffs.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establish the new, terrifying stakes: the house demands innocence (Toby) and Valerie is now its prisoner. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie wants to escape the house vs. the house trapping her and making a demand. Value Shift: (+/-) Valerie goes from confused (-) to absolute, sickening horror and powerlessness (--).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS

The heavy, custom-milled mahogany front doors SLAM shut. 

The bone-rattling force sends a shockwave through the cavernous foyer, violently swaying the minimalist chandelier overhead.

CLACK. CLACK. CLACK. 

A chorus of steel deadbolts slide into their reinforced frames. Synchronized. Final. 

Valerie drops her pristine leather portfolio. She lunges across the Italian marble floor.

She grabs the heavy brass handles, yanking them backward. They refuse to give. She plants her stiletto against the doorframe, throwing her entire body weight into the pull. 

The brass doesn't even jiggle. 

VALERIE
(breathless)
No. No, no. Open.

She pounds her fists against the frosted sidelight glass. It’s reinforced. Unbreakable. 

Panic threatens to crack her icy veneer. She digs into her tailored blazer, pulling out her phone. 

No signal. The screen tears with a pulsing, magnetic static. 

VALERIE
Listen to me. We have a binding arrangement. 

Silence. Just the low, hollow hum of the central air conditioning. 

VALERIE
I brought you Pendelton. High net-worth. High caloric value. You are fully capitalized! We are in the black!

The house disagrees. 

A deep, subsonic bass vibrates up through the floorboards, rattling Valerie’s teeth. She stumbles back, catching herself on a floating glass credenza. 

The pristine, eggshell-white walls begin to shift. 

They expand and contract. Breathing. A sickening, organic rhythm hiding just beneath the expensive drywall and plaster. 

VALERIE
Stop it. You’re damaging the structural integrity. Do you have any idea what this property appraises for? 

She points a trembling finger toward the ceiling, desperate to pivot the reality of the nightmare into a simple maintenance issue. 

VALERIE
The fixtures. You’re ruining the custom fixtures!

Above her, the sleek, recessed halogen lights flicker and dim. 

A thick, clear SAP oozes from the brushed-metal rims of the bulbs. It hangs suspended for a terrible second, catching the dying light like liquid glass. 

SPLAT. 

A heavy drop hits the hardwood mere inches from Valerie’s shoes. 

She flinches back. Another drop falls. Then another. A rhythmic, wet drumming echoing through the foyer. 

The sap is viscous. Unnatural. The air suddenly reeks of ozone and digested copper. 

VALERIE
(voice shaking)
I can get you Richard. My boss. He’s a corporate shark. Endless appetite. Prime real estate. Just... unlock the door.

The sap doesn't stop. It pools on the hardwood. But it doesn't spread randomly. 

It stretches. Crawls. Guided by an unseen, intelligent gravity. 

Valerie watches, completely paralyzed. 

The clear liquid thickens, glowing with a faint, bioluminescent amber hue. It drags itself across the grain of the wood, forming sharp, jagged lines. 

A vertical stroke. A horizontal cross. 

A 'T'.

Valerie’s breath hitches. 

VALERIE
No. We don't mix business with personal. Not him.

The sap curves into an 'O'. 

Then a 'B'.

It finishes the 'Y'.

TOBY.

The glowing name pulses in time with the breathing walls. A literal demand etched into the floor plan. The ultimate asking price. 

Valerie stares at the name. The absolute horror washes over her face, draining the last drop of color from her cheeks. 

Her hands begin to shake violently. She doesn't scream. 

Instead, she looks down at her wrists. 

Her thumbs dig into the expensive, pristine silk cuffs of her blouse. She scrubs them. Hard. 

Frantically rubbing at invisible stains, trying to clean away a filth that has already soaked straight down to her soul. 

The walls pulse around her. The house waits for its closing costs.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Picking up exactly where we left off: Valerie stands frozen in the foyer, obsessively scrubbing her cuffs as the glowing sap spells 'TOBY'. The deadbolts remain snapped shut. Forcing her panic down, Valerie pivots to her transactional speech patterns. She speaks aloud to the pulsating walls, pitching a counter-offer: she cannot afford to lose Toby, but she will bring the house a ruthless, high-net-worth rival instead. A better meal. The sap slowly recedes into the fixtures. The deadbolts violently snap open. Valerie bursts out the front door, gasping for air.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establishes the house's specific demand and Valerie's refusal to sacrifice Toby. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie vs. the House's immediate hunger. Value Shift (+/-): Trapped and horrified (-) to temporarily reprieved (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS

The heavy oak front door is sealed. Three solid brass deadbolts remain SNAPPED perfectly in the locked position. 

Valerie stands frozen on the imported Italian marble.
