At her feet, thick, amber-colored sap oozes from the grout lines. It pools and connects, glowing with a sickly, bioluminescent pulse. 

The sap forms four distinct letters: T - O - B - Y.

Valerie stares at the name. Her breathing turns shallow, rapid. She reaches for the solid brass door handle. 

It sears her skin. She yanks her hand back, stifling a yelp.

The walls of the foyer subtly contract. The mahogany panels groan, mimicking the sound of an empty, starving stomach. 

Valerie looks down at her pristine white silk blouse. Her right hand aggressively grips her left cuff. Her thumb scrubs back and forth over the immaculate fabric, erasing a stain that isn't there.

She scrubs harder. The fabric frays. 

The glowing sap begins to climb the baseboards, inching toward her designer heels.

Valerie abruptly stops scrubbing. She rolls her shoulders back, lifting her chin. Her posture snaps into a flawless, icy boardroom stiffness. 

VALERIE
(steady, projecting)
We need to discuss asset allocation. 

The house falls dead silent. The sap stops moving. 

VALERIE
Toby Higgins is an entry-level employee. A starter property. He possesses zero capital, zero influence, and frankly, zero ambition. His current valuation is entirely negligible. 

A low, guttural vibration hums beneath the floorboards. The chandelier above rattles, crystal teardrops clinking together like chattering teeth. 

VALERIE
(deflecting)
Look at the craftsmanship of this floating staircase. The cantilevered treads. The bespoke glass balustrade. You are a masterpiece of mid-century restoration. You demand a certain caliber of clientele. 

The sap bubbles. It spits a single drop of hot resin onto the toe of Valerie’s shoe. It burns a hole straight through the leather.

Valerie doesn't flinch. She maintains terrifying eye contact with the empty air.

VALERIE
You are asking for a low-yield investment. I am offering a counter-proposal. 

The walls press inward. The air pressure in the foyer drops, popping Valerie’s ears. 

VALERIE
A premium acquisition. High-net-worth. A competitor with an aggressive, diversified portfolio of actual sins. 

The floorboards cease their vibration. The crystal chandelier stills. 

VALERIE
Toby doesn't even appreciate the structural integrity of your foundation. I will bring you a shark. Someone marbled with absolute, unadulterated greed. 

Valerie takes a single step forward, stepping directly over the glowing 'T' on the floor. 

VALERIE
(lowering her voice)
A much better meal. Unmatched ROI. I will deliver them right to your foyer. But the assistant stays off the market. Do we have a deal?

For a long, agonizing moment, nothing happens. 

Then, the bioluminescent glow fades. The thick sap slowly slithers backward, retreating off the baseboards, sinking deep into the grout lines until the marble is spotless.

The temperature in the foyer normalizes. 

CLACK. CLACK. CLACK.

The three brass deadbolts violently snap open. 

Valerie’s icy veneer shatters. She lunges for the handle, throws her entire body weight against the heavy oak door, and bursts out into the blinding sunlight. 

She collapses against the exterior stone pillar, gasping for air, her hands shaking violently as she clutches her ruined cuff.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. REAL ESTATE OFFICE - BULLPEN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie marches into the bustling brokerage bullpen, her icy professional veneer fully restored. She spots GEMMA checking her reflection in her phone screen while passively ignoring a junior agent. Valerie interrupts, pitching a fake 50/50 co-listing agreement on the modernist mansion. Gemma’s eyes narrow with suspicion, but her greed wins out. She slowly reapplies her lip gloss, dripping with fake sweetness as she accepts the bait.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Sets up the sacrificial bait to appease the house. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to lure Gemma vs. Gemma's inherent distrust of Valerie. Value Shift (+/-): Desperate (-) to having a proactive plan (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. REAL ESTATE OFFICE - BULLPEN - DAY

A chaotic symphony of ringing phones and aggressive negotiations. 

VALERIE VANCE marches through the sea of glass desks. Her posture is a weapon. The icy, high-end professional veneer is flawless--a stark contrast to the financial guillotine currently hanging over her head.

Across the room, GEMMA FROST ignores a panicked JUNIOR AGENT hovering by her desk. Gemma stares into the black mirror of her iPhone screen, captivated by her own reflection as she smooths a perfect blonde wave.

JUNIOR AGENT
I just need a signature for the escrow release, Gemma. They're threatening to pull out--

GEMMA
(without looking up)
Let them pull out, sweetie. If they can't handle a simple delay, they don't deserve the square footage. Now shoo. You're blocking my light.

The Junior Agent wilts. Valerie steps directly into Gemma's airspace. The Junior Agent takes the out and scatters.

Valerie stands over the desk. Gemma finally lowers her phone, her lips curling into a weaponized, saccharine smile.

GEMMA
Valerie. Look at you. I'm shocked Richard hasn't repurposed your desk into a coffee station yet. I love that blazer. Is it vintage?

VALERIE
I'm restructuring the marketing footprint on the Crestview property.

Gemma's smile freezes. Her eyes narrow, scanning Valerie for the trap.

GEMMA
The modernist glass box. I heard you locked that down. Funny. You usually only share a listing when there's a lawsuit attached.

Valerie's thumb instinctively finds the edge of her expensive silk cuff. She rubs at a microscopic, phantom stain.

VALERIE
It's a legacy asset, Gemma. The seller is demanding a high-volume pedigree. An aggressive co-listing strategy maximizes the bidding war, and frankly, your... aggressive socialite demographic is exactly the target acquisition profile.

Gemma doesn't immediately bite. Her thumb moves to her index finger, a manicured nail digging viciously into her own cuticle. 

GEMMA
Are you drowning, Valerie? Is that what this is? The bank finally calling in those leveraged loans? 

VALERIE
(icy)
Liquidity is a fluid concept. I'm offering you a turnkey opportunity to elevate your portfolio. 

GEMMA
A fifty-fifty split on a thirty-million-dollar property. From the woman who scalped the Hawthorne commission out from under me while I was at my mother's funeral.

Valerie doesn't blink. She stops rubbing her cuff, pivoting smoothly to the aesthetic of the desk.

VALERIE
Hawthorne was a distressed teardown. This is turnkey luxury. Look at this marble paperweight, Gemma. Italian? It takes a certain eye to appreciate flawless execution. That's the eye I need in the room. If you prefer selling stucco split-levels to middle-management, I can easily take this to Richard.

Gemma picks harder at the cuticle. A tiny bead of bright red blood wells up. She doesn't notice. The sheer gravity of the commission is doing cartwheels behind her eyes. The fear of being left behind extinguishes her caution.

GEMMA
Who's the primary on the signage?

VALERIE
Joint billing. But I need you to handle the twilight walkthroughs. The architecture really comes alive at dusk. I need a closer in the room when the sun goes down.

Gemma stops picking. She pulls a tube of Chanel lip gloss from her designer bag. Slowly, deliberately, she glides the wand over her lips, locking eyes with Valerie's reflection in the mirrored cap. 

Greed wins.

GEMMA
I suppose I can carve out an hour. Send the digital keys to my assistant.

VALERIE
Physical keys only. The seller is eccentric about security. Off the grid.

Valerie reaches into her pocket and drops a heavy, geometric brass key onto the glass desk. 

It hits with a definitive, heavy CLACK.

Gemma picks it up, admiring the cold metal. She flashes that dripping, fake-sweet smile.

GEMMA
See you at twilight, Val. Try to look alive.

Valerie turns on her heel. As she walks away, her thumb furiously resumes scrubbing the invisible stain on her cuff.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie and Gemma step through the heavy front doors of the mansion. Gemma immediately launches into backhanded compliments about the entryway's acoustics. Valerie stays two steps behind her, her eyes darting to the floorboards and ceiling, physically bracing herself for the house to open its maw and consume her rival. The house remains perfectly still.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Builds tension for the expected supernatural attack. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie waiting for the strike vs. the house remaining dormant. Value Shift (+/-): Anticipatory (+) to confused (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - DAY

The massive oak front doors GROAN open. 

GEMMA FROST steps inside. Draped in aggressive Prada, she owns the space instantly. 

VALERIE VANCE trails exactly two steps behind. Her posture is rigid. Her eyes dart to the herringbone floorboards, then up to the steel-reinforced ceiling. Waiting.

GEMMA
(clapping twice, sharply)
Listen to that echo.

The sharp sound bounces off the concrete walls. Valerie flinches, physically bracing for the floor to split open. 

GEMMA
It’s almost aggressively hollow. I love that for you, Val. It really mirrors where you are in your career right now. Just so much... empty space to fill.

Valerie’s thumb finds the edge of her pristine silk cuff. She scrubs at an invisible speck of dirt. Hard.

VALERIE
The acoustics are a byproduct of the imported Italian concrete. It’s a highly sought-after brutalist feature. 

GEMMA
Brutalist. Is that what we’re calling it? I was going to say 'clinical.' But you always did have a gift for dressing up a disaster.

Gemma takes another step forward. She stands dead center in the foyer. The exact spot where Arthur Pendelton was swallowed whole two days ago.

Valerie stops breathing. Her muscles lock. She waits for the sickening crunch of marble turning to teeth. The low, guttural groan of the foundation shifting to feed.

Nothing happens. Just the gentle, rhythmic hum of the central air.

Gemma pulls her phone from her Birkin bag. She uses the dark screen to check her reflection, tilting her chin, entirely oblivious to the kill floor beneath her designer heels.

GEMMA
Honestly, Val, I’m surprised Richard let you keep this listing. It’s so... out of your tax bracket. You must be exhausted trying to keep up appearances.

Valerie glares at the pristine floorboards directly beneath Gemma's feet. Her jaw tightens. *Eat her. Come on, just eat her.*

The house remains perfectly, stubbornly dormant. 

VALERIE
The structural integrity of this property is unmatched. The foundation provides absolute, unbreakable security.

GEMMA
(sliding lip gloss from her purse)
Security. Right. Because we all know how much you hate being left out in the cold. 

Gemma slowly applies the gloss, smacking her lips. She finally turns back, catching the sheer, white-knuckled tension radiating from Valerie's frame.

GEMMA
Are you going to show me the kitchen, or just stand there vibrating?

Valerie blinks. Deep confusion washes over her icy veneer. She glances at the walls, then back to Gemma. Why isn't it happening? 

VALERIE
The kitchen. Of course. It features pristine, zero-porosity quartzite counters. Follow me.

Valerie steps forward, her heels clicking against the terrifyingly solid wood. The house isn't hungry. Or worse-- it's becoming a picky eater.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND LIBRARY - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie leads Gemma into the shadowy grand library. Gemma arrogantly taps her designer nails against the woodwork, loudly critiquing the crown molding. Valerie presses her back against the heavy oak door, silently praying for the floor to swallow Gemma. Nothing happens. The room is dead silent. The entity flatly refuses the offering, finding Gemma’s petty corporate backstabbing unappetizing compared to Toby's pure innocence.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Proves the house has a specific palate and rejects corrupted souls. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie silently urging the house to eat Gemma vs. the house's stubborn refusal. Value Shift (+/-): Hopeful for a quick kill (+) to terrified realization the plan failed (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND LIBRARY - DAY

The heavy oak door SLAMS shut. 

VALERIE presses her back against the thick wood. Her chest heaves once, then stills. She grips the brass handle behind her back, locking it into place. 

GEMMA stalks through the cavernous, shadowy library. She doesn't notice the trapped door. She runs a hand along the towering, floor-to-ceiling bookshelves. 

GEMMA
I have to admit, Val. When I heard you bagged the Pendleton estate, I was shocked. But seeing it in person? 

Gemma stops at a section of custom woodwork. She taps her acrylic nails against the trim. 

CLICK. CLICK. CLICK.

GEMMA
It’s a bit... derivative. Don't you think? It screams new-money desperation. 

Valerie’s eyes dart to the floorboards beneath Gemma’s designer heels. She waits for the wood to soften. For the shadows to stretch and the house to wake up. 

VALERIE
It’s imported Brazilian rosewood. The structural integrity is unparalleled. Step toward the fireplace, Gem. Feel the radiant heat in the hearth.

Gemma doesn't move toward the fireplace. Instead, she pulls out her phone, checking her reflection in the dark screen. 

GEMMA
Radiant heat is so 2018. My clients want smart climate control. Honestly, this place feels completely dead. 

Valerie’s knuckles turn white against the brass door handle. She stares intensely at the ornate Persian rug in the center of the room. 

VALERIE
It’s a historical property. It has a distinct... appetite. A character. Just stand on the center rug. You’ll feel the foundation beneath you. 

Gemma sighs, a breathy, exaggerated sound. She saunters over to the center of the rug, crossing her arms. 

GEMMA
Fine. I’m standing. I’m feeling. 

Valerie holds her breath. 

Dust motes drift in the shafts of sunlight. A grandfather clock ticks in the far corner. 

Nothing else happens. The floorboards remain rigid. The walls do not breathe. The house is completely, utterly dormant. 

Gemma pops open a tube of lip gloss. She slowly, meticulously applies a fresh coat, staring right at Valerie. 

GEMMA
I feel absolutely nothing, Valerie. I thought you were hiding some architectural marvel in here. It’s just old wood and bad lighting. 

Valerie’s pristine mask slips. Her breathing goes shallow. She looks up at the vaulted ceiling, then back to Gemma. 

Nothing. The house refuses to bite. 

GEMMA
(fake pout)
You know, when you stole the Miller account from me, I thought you were playing chess. But looking at you now? Pushing this dusty mausoleum? You're just drowning. 

Gemma’s thumb aggressively picks at her own cuticle. She tears a strip of skin. A tiny bead of blood forms on her finger. 

Valerie zeroes in on the blood. It’s a perfect appetizer. The house has devoured people for less. 

VALERIE
There’s usually a... a settling. A massive shift in the load-bearing walls. Just wait a second. 

GEMMA
The only thing shifting is your market share, sweetie. 

Gemma wipes the drop of blood on the edge of a pristine leather armchair. 

Still, the room remains dead silent. The entity flatly rejects the offering. It doesn't want Gemma’s toxic, synthetic soul. 

The horrific realization washes over Valerie. The house's palate has evolved. It wants something clean. It wants Toby's pure innocence. 

Valerie’s thumb begins to obsessively scrub at the cuff of her silk blouse. She rubs the expensive fabric over and over, trying to erase an invisible stain. 

Gemma drops the lip gloss back into her Prada bag. She marches toward the oak door. 

GEMMA
I've seen enough. I’m going to call my broker and tell him we pass. Open the door, Val. 

Valerie stays planted against the wood. She stares at Gemma, bitter disappointment mixing with a new, icy terror. 

VALERIE
The appraisal is flawless. You’re making a massive financial mistake. 

Gemma stops inches from Valerie’s face. The fake sweetness completely vanishes from her eyes. 

GEMMA
Move. 

Valerie stops scrubbing her cuff. She steps aside and pulls the heavy oak door open. 

The bright light of the hallway spills over Gemma as she exits without a backward glance. 

Valerie stands alone in the dead, silent library. The house won.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Bored and unimpressed, Gemma turns down the co-listing, calling the property 'derivative,' and struts out of the library. Valerie follows her to the foyer, watching in stunned silence as Gemma safely exits the front door. The heavy door clicks shut. Valerie is left completely alone in the suffocating silence of the mansion.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Isolates Valerie with the angry entity. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie trying to maintain composure vs. the looming supernatural threat. Value Shift (+/-): Anxious (-) to deeply vulnerable (--).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS

Gemma’s designer heels CLACK against the imported terrazzo floor, setting a brisk, dismissive tempo. 

Valerie tracks her, two steps behind. Her posture is rigid, an icy mask of professional grace.

GEMMA
It’s a valiant effort, sweetie. Truly. But I just don’t see my buyers settling for... whatever this is trying to be.

VALERIE
The spatial fluidity is unprecedented, Gemma. We are talking seamless architectural integration. It’s a legacy asset.

Gemma stops at the massive oak front door. She doesn't look at Valerie. Instead, she lifts her phone, checking her flawless reflection in the dark screen.

GEMMA
Maybe five years ago. 

Gemma pops the cap off a tube of gloss. She slowly, meticulously traces her bottom lip, letting the silence stretch.

GEMMA
Honestly? It feels a little derivative. 

Valerie’s jaw tightens. She glances at the floor. A large, green Luna moth flutters weakly against the baseboard, trapped and exhausted.

VALERIE
Derivative? The brutalist aesthetic is heavily trending. If we co-list, we monopolize the waterfront demographic. 

Valerie steps forward. With a swift, practiced motion, she scoops the injured moth into her palm and slips it safely into her blazer pocket. 

GEMMA
(laughing softly)
Co-list? Val, honey. I’m not running a charity. But it’s a cute stepping stone for you. I’m so glad you’re finally finding your level.

Deep within the walls, the plumbing GROANS. A low, metallic screech vibrates through the soles of Valerie's shoes. The house is getting impatient.

Valerie’s eyes dart to the vaulted ceiling, then back to Gemma. 

VALERIE
Just walk through the west wing with me. The sunken atrium has a bespoke—

GEMMA
I’m late for a chemical peel. 

Gemma pushes the heavy brass handle. The door swings open. Bright, blinding daylight spills into the gloomy foyer. 

Valerie braces herself. Her breath hitches. 

Nothing happens. The floorboards remain solid. The house lets the prey walk right out.

GEMMA
Good luck, Val. Try not to look so tense. It ages you.

Gemma steps over the threshold, totally oblivious to the jaws she just walked out of. 

The heavy door swings shut on its hydraulic hinges. 

CLICK.

The deadbolt slides into place on its own. The daylight vanishes, plunging the foyer back into sharp, angular shadows.

Valerie stands completely still in the center of the cavernous room. 

The silence is suffocating. Heavy. It presses against her eardrums.

Then, the floorboards pulse. A slow, rhythmic thrumming, like a massive heartbeat beneath the terrazzo. 

Valerie looks down at her pristine white silk cuff. 

Her fingers twitch. She begins to rub the fabric. Hard. 

The shadows in the corners of the room elongate, creeping unnaturally across the floor toward her ankles. 

She scrubs at the invisible stain on her wrist, her breath coming in shallow, ragged gasps. 

The house isn't just hungry anymore. It's angry.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND LIBRARY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The moment Gemma is gone, the mansion turns its frustration entirely onto Valerie. The library’s towering bookshelves suddenly groan and warp violently inward. Heavy leather-bound encyclopedias are launched like cannonballs directly at Valerie’s head. She shrieks, diving hard behind a heavy velvet armchair as books smash into the plaster where she just stood.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The monster turns on the protagonist for failing to deliver the promised meal. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie vs. the physical assault of the shifting architecture. Value Shift (+/-): Vulnerable (-) to physically under attack (--).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND LIBRARY - CONTINUOUS

The heavy oak doors click shut. Gemma’s staccato footsteps fade down the corridor. 

Valerie stands alone in the cavernous space. 

Silence. 

Then, a low, sub-bass hum vibrates through the herringbone floorboards. The temperature in the room plummets. 

Valerie’s breathing goes shallow. She aggressively rubs the cuff of her silk blouse. Scrubbing an invisible stain. 

VALERIE
Look, we experienced a slight buyer pull-back. It’s a fluctuating market.

The floorboards groan. The hum deepens into a predatory rumble. The shadows in the corners of the room seem to stretch.

VALERIE
(pacing, calculating)
Gemma wasn't qualified anyway. Low liquidity. Terrible aesthetic palate. I can pivot. We just need to restructure the showing schedule--

CRACK. 

The towering mahogany bookshelves flanking the room violently warp inward. The thick wood bends like wet clay, defying gravity, bowing toward the center of the room.

Valerie freezes. 

A massive, leather-bound Encyclopedia Britannica ejects from the top shelf. It rockets across the room.

SMASH. 

It craters the plaster wall mere inches from Valerie’s head. 

Valerie shrieks. 

The shelves ripple. A dozen heavy tomes launch simultaneously. Cannonballs of literature. 

Valerie dives hard. She hits the Moroccan rug and scrambles behind a massive velvet armchair. 

Books obliterate the space where she just stood. Pages explode like shrapnel. Plaster dust chokes the air. 

THUD. THUD. THUD. 

The heavy volumes pummel the back of the armchair. Valerie curls into a tight ball, covering her head as the velvet tears under the assault. 

The architecture itself is screaming. The bookshelves curve over her, forming a suffocating, claustrophobic dome. The house is closing in to digest her. 

A Daddy Longlegs spider scurries frantically across the rug, fleeing the impact zone. 

Valerie, trembling violently, cups her manicured hand gently over the insect. Protecting it from the falling debris. 

VALERIE
Stop! Stop, you structural nightmare!

A heavy medical dictionary slams into the floorboard inches from her knee. The wood splinters. 

VALERIE
I have a whale! An institutional investor!

The barrage of books hesitates. One last novel drops lazily onto the rug. 

Valerie breathes in ragged gasps. She keeps her hand cupped over the spider. Her eyes dart around the warped, looming room. 

VALERIE
Richard Sterling. My broker. High net worth, extremely aggressive portfolio. He has more meat on his bones than Gemma ever did.

The room holds its breath. The dust swirls in the dim light. 

VALERIE
(pitching the deal)
A premium acquisition. You want a feast? I’ll bring you the whole damn buffet.

The warped mahogany shelves creak. Slowly, agonizingly, they straighten back into perfect, load-bearing right angles. 

The suffocating pressure in the room lifts. The hum fades into silence. 

Valerie slowly uncups her hand. The spider scurries safely into a brass floor vent. 

She stands up. Her knees shake, but she forces them still. She vigorously brushes plaster dust off her designer skirt. 

She checks her reflection in the darkened window pane. Her icy veneer slams back into place. 

VALERIE
I’ll arrange a private walkthrough. Just...

She kicks a torn, pulverized encyclopedia out of sight beneath the armchair. 

VALERIE
Fix the drywall before he gets here. Curb appeal is everything.

Valerie turns on her heel and strides toward the door. 

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND LIBRARY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The architectural geometry of the library aggressively shifts, the walls groaning as they physically close in. Valerie scrambles on her hands and knees toward the exit, but the doorway begins to rapidly shrink, threatening to trap her inside. As she dives for the narrowing gap, a jagged splinter of hardwood shoots up from the floorboards, slicing a deep, bloody gash across her cheek. She tumbles through the shrinking door just as it snaps shut behind her.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Delivers a physical consequence and warning from the house. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie trying to escape vs. the house trying to trap her. Value Shift (+/-): Under attack (-) to injured but escaped (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND LIBRARY - CONTINUOUS

The mahogany bookshelves don't just shift--they inhale.

A sickening CRACK echoes as a perfect 90-degree corner bends into an impossible, claustrophobic acute angle. First-edition books vomit onto the floor, crushed instantly into paper pulp by the narrowing walls.

VALERIE VANCE is on her hands and knees. Her tailored blazer is dusted with pulverized plaster. She isn't running yet. 

She’s staring at a massive, flawless DIAMOND resting on the Persian rug.

The walls groan. A deep, guttural vibration rattles the teeth in her skull. The ceiling descends a full inch.

Valerie snatches the diamond.

VALERIE
(muttering)
Bespoke... dynamic spatial volume.

She furiously rubs at her pristine white cuff. There's no stain, but she scrubs it raw.

The heavy oak double doors at the far end of the room slam shut. Well, not shut. The frame itself begins to buckle inward. The wood screams as the doorway physically shrinks.

TOBY (O.S.)
(muffled, panicked)
V-Valerie?! The floorplan... it's rewriting itself! The corridor is eating the foyer!

VALERIE
It’s an aggressive open-concept, Toby! High-yield spatial dynamics!

She scrambles forward. The floorboards undulate like a wooden ocean. A heavy brass globe rolls off a desk, flattening into a brass pancake under the immense, invisible pressure of the shifting room.

The doorway is now half its original size. The gap is rapidly closing.

TOBY (O.S.)
I'm trying the handle! It's-- it's hot! The brass is bleeding!

VALERIE
Just hold the door!

The Persian rug suddenly slides backward, pulled toward the center of the room. The house is trying to swallow her.

Valerie digs her manicured nails into the exposed hardwood, dragging herself forward against the grain.

TOBY (O.S.)
The blueprints! The blueprints show a load-bearing wall here! It's not up to code! Walls shouldn't move!

VALERIE
Fluid architecture! It’s a seller’s market, Toby, we adapt!

Valerie's heel catches in a widening fissure in the floor. She pulls. The house grips her five-hundred-dollar Louboutin like a vice.

She yanks her foot, tearing the shoe from her heel. She crawls, frantic, abandoning all pretense of grace.

The doorway is a mere three feet high. Two feet. 

Valerie pushes through the debris. Her hand lands inches from a STRAY MOTH, fluttering weakly on the shrinking floor. Its wing is torn.

A micro-second of pure paradox. The cutthroat capitalist pauses. 

The ceiling drops another foot. The chandelier shatters against the floor, showering her back in jagged crystal.

Valerie scoops the moth, cradling it in her palm.

She dives for the shrinking exit.

The house fights back. 

A jagged, foot-long splinter of oak erupts from the floorboards like a spear.

It whips across Valerie's face as she lunges through the gap. Blood sprays across the threshold.

She tumbles hard onto the cold marble floor of the hallway.

Behind her, the library door SNAPS shut with the deafening boom of a closing vault. 

The wood fuses together. No seam. No doorknob. Just a solid, blank wall.

Valerie lies on the marble, chest heaving. Blood drips from a deep gash on her cheek, staining her white cuff. 

She doesn't scrub it this time. 

She just opens her palm. The moth crawls onto her trembling thumb.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DRIVEWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie bursts out of the mansion’s front doors, clutching her bleeding face. She sprints down the driveway, her heels kicking up gravel, and throws herself into her luxury sedan. She slams the car into reverse and speeds away from the property, her chest heaving in absolute terror.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Transitions Valerie back to the human world, carrying the physical mark of her failure. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie vs. her own rising panic. Value Shift (+/-): Terrified (-) to fleeing (+).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DRIVEWAY - CONTINUOUS

The massive, custom-milled oak front doors of the mansion violently BURST OPEN.

VALERIE (30s) stumbles out into the blinding daylight. Her usually immaculate, icy veneer is entirely shattered. 

She clutches the left side of her face. Dark, crimson blood spills through her manicured fingers, dripping onto the concrete threshold.

She doesn't look back. She sprints down the sprawling, minimalist driveway.

It's an ocean of crushed white quartz gravel. A nightmare for her red-soled stilettos. 

Valerie's ankle twists viciously. She stumbles hard, her knee slamming into the jagged rocks. 

A normal person would kick the shoes off. Valerie refuses. The armor stays on. She forces herself back upright, limping and sprinting simultaneously.

Every agonizing step kicks up a violent spray of white dust.

Her leased black Lexus sedan sits at the end of the driveway like a designated lifeboat. 

Valerie reaches the driver's side door and slams her bloody hand against the tinted glass. She catches her fractured reflection.

Pale. Terrified. A jagged smear of blood across her cheekbone.

Then, her eyes drop lower. To her wrist.

A single drop of blood has soaked into the pristine, French-cuffed silk of her designer blouse.

The mortal terror in her eyes shifts into a manic, obsessive panic. The house just tried to consume her, but the stain is what breaks her mind. 

Valerie scrubs at the cuff. Hard. Frantic. 

Her bloody fingers only smear the crimson deeper into the white silk. She rubs faster, her breath hitching into jagged, hyperventilating gasps.

VALERIE
(muttering, breathless)
Value holds. The foundation is solid. It's just a cosmetic blemish...

She realizes the stain won't come out. The silk is ruined. The veneer is gone.

A deep, unnatural GROAN echoes from the foundation of the mansion behind her. The architectural equivalent of a stomach rumbling. The heavy front doors slowly begin to swing shut on their own.

Valerie snaps out of her trance. She yanks the car door handle and throws herself inside.

INT. VALERIE'S SEDAN - CONTINUOUS

The heavy car door SLAMS shut, sealing her in a soundproof luxury vacuum. The sudden, terrifying silence of the cabin is deafening.

Valerie's chest heaves violently against the leather steering wheel. She gasps for oxygen, her lungs burning, smearing blood onto the pristine upholstery.

Her trembling hand fumbles for the push-to-start button. Her fingers shake so badly she misses twice, blindly jabbing at the dashboard.

VALERIE
(sobbing, furious)
Turn over. Turn over!

She punches the button. The V8 engine ROARS to life, the digital dashboard illuminating her blood-streaked face.

Valerie grips the leather gear shift, her knuckles turning bone-white. She rips it down into REVERSE.

EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DRIVEWAY - CONTINUOUS

The Lexus lurches backward. The tires shriek, violently churning up the expensive quartz gravel in a massive white cloud.

Valerie doesn't even bother turning the car around. She floors the accelerator.

The sedan rockets backward down the winding, tree-lined driveway. It swerves wildly, clipping a perfectly manicured hedge, before disappearing into the shadows of the canyon road.

The imposing, geometric facade of the mansion sits in absolute, hungry silence. 

The front doors CLICK shut.

> SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. REAL ESTATE OFFICE - VALERIE'S PRIVATE OFFICE - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie sneaks into her darkened private office at the brokerage. She pulls a first-aid kit from her desk and frantically attempts to bandage the bleeding gash on her cheek, using the reflection of her blank computer monitor. She desperately tries to smooth her hair and restore her flawless facade. As she turns around, a towering figure emerges from the shadows.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establishes a false moment of safety before the external antagonist strikes. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie trying to hide her physical trauma vs. the reality of the bleeding wound. Value Shift (+/-): Relieved to be away from the house (+) to startled (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. REAL ESTATE OFFICE - VALERIE'S PRIVATE OFFICE - LATER

Moonlight slices through the Venetian blinds, painting zebra stripes across the minimalist furniture. 

The heavy glass door clicks shut. VALERIE (30s) leans her weight against it. Her chest heaves. Her usually immaculate Prada suit is rumpled, dusted with an unrecognizable gray ash.

A jagged, two-inch gash weeps thick blood down her right cheek.

She pushes off the door, her boots completely silent on the plush carpet. She drops her leather tote onto the desk. Her hands tremble violently. 

She yanks open the bottom drawer and pulls out a white plastic FIRST-AID KIT. 

No lights. She taps the spacebar of her desktop. The monitor wakes, casting a cold, blue glow across her face. 

She leans in close, using the blank login screen as a mirror. She rips open an alcohol swab with her teeth. 

She presses the swab to the gash. She winces, a sharp hiss escaping her teeth. 

A heavy drop of blood falls, blooming a crimson stain on her stark white French cuff.

Valerie freezes. She drops the swab and frantically scrubs at the cuff with her thumb. It only smears. She scrubs harder, her breath hitching, eyes wide with disproportionate panic. 

A tiny, injured crane fly struggles on the glowing keyboard, missing a leg. Valerie stops scrubbing. She gently scoops the fragile insect onto a piece of letterhead, carrying it to the windowsill and placing it safely behind the blinds.

She takes a deep, shuddering breath. She smooths her hair back into a severe, flawless bun. She adjusts her blazer. The icy mask is back on.

RICHARD (O.S.)
Nature is remarkably efficient, isn't it?

Valerie jolts. 

In the corner shadows, a towering silhouette shifts. RICHARD STERLING (50s) leans back in a leather guest chair. He strikes a match, the sudden flare illuminating his sharp, predatory features.

VALERIE
Richard. The lights work, you know.

RICHARD
(extinguishing the match)
I find the artificial glare ruins the ambiance. And you clearly prefer the dark right now.

He stands. He is massive, moving with the slow, deliberate grace of a starved apex predator. He steps into the blue light of the monitor. 

His eyes lock onto her cheek.

RICHARD
Rough closing?

VALERIE
(touching the bandage)
Minor structural snag at the modernist property. A low-hanging fixture. The staging is completely turnkey otherwise.

RICHARD
A fixture.

VALERIE
We're pivoting the aesthetic. High-impact minimalist. 

Richard steps closer. He reaches out, tracing a finger over the smeared blood on her desk.

RICHARD
You see, Valerie, a healthy ecosystem relies on transparency. The wolves know where the elk graze. The elk know where the wolves hunt. When an agent goes off the grid with my firm's most lucrative exclusive... the ecosystem gets confused.

VALERIE
The listing is under control. I'm leveraging a highly motivated buyer pool. 

RICHARD
Arthur Pendelton's office called. He missed a board meeting. His platinum card hasn't swiped in twelve hours.

Valerie's jaw tightens. She subtly shifts her weight, placing the desk between them. 

VALERIE
Pendelton is an eccentric. He's likely admiring the imported Italian marble. The ROI on that property is going to shatter our quarterly projections. Look at the comps--

Richard smiles. It doesn't reach his eyes. He circles the desk.

RICHARD
I don't care about the comps right now, Valerie. I care about the foundation. I built this firm from the rubble my father left behind. I don't do blind spots.

He stops inches from her. The proximity is suffocating. 

VALERIE
There are no blind spots. Just a highly specialized asset requiring a... delicate touch.

Richard leans down. His pristine suit brushes against hers. He invades her space, staring dead into her eyes.

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
I am taking the modernist listing. Hand over the keys, the gate codes, and the client roster by 8:00 AM. Or I will strip your license and ensure you are selling swamp-land out of a rusted Honda for the rest of your miserable life.

Valerie stares up at him. Her thumb finds her stained cuff, pressing hard into the fabric. She doesn't blink.

VALERIE
That property requires a unique management style. You don't have the stomach for the upkeep.

RICHARD
I have the stomach for everything.

Richard turns, his massive frame swallowing the moonlight as he walks to the door. 

RICHARD
8:00 AM, Valerie.

The glass door clicks shut behind him. 

Valerie stands alone in the blue glow. The silence is deafening. She looks down at the smeared blood on her cuff, then up at her reflection in the monitor. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. REAL ESTATE OFFICE - VALERIE'S PRIVATE OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: RICHARD STERLING steps into the dim light. He notes the bandage on her face with a calm, unnerving smile. Speaking in predatory metaphors about apex predators, he slowly advances, physically cornering Valerie against the edge of her desk. He lists the undeniable pattern: every single high-net-worth client who toured her exclusive listing has vanished without a trace.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The external human antagonist tightens the noose. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie attempting to deflect with real estate jargon vs. Richard cornering her with facts. Value Shift (+/-): Startled (-) to cornered (--).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. REAL ESTATE OFFICE - VALERIE'S PRIVATE OFFICE - CONTINUOUS

Dim light filters through the slanted blinds. Valerie breathes heavily, her eyes darting toward the doorway.

RICHARD STERLING steps from the shadows. His bespoke suit seems to absorb the low light. A calm, unnerving smile stretches across his face. 

He stares intently at the fresh gauze taped over Valerie's cheekbone.

RICHARD
(softly)
Do you know what happens to a wounded animal in a healthy ecosystem, Valerie?

Valerie straightens her posture. She instantly freezes her expression into an icy, professional mask.

VALERIE
Richard. It's late. The market is closed, and so is my door.

RICHARD
The scent of copper draws the apex predators. They don't even have to hunt. They just wait for the limp.

Valerie steps behind her heavy mahogany desk. She gestures sharply toward a sleek, glass-encased architectural model of a modern high-rise resting on a side table.

VALERIE
If you are here about the downtown high-rise, the cap rate is already stabilized. The aesthetic value alone guarantees a massive return on investment. I have secured the anchor tenants.

Richard doesn't look at the high-rise. He takes a slow, deliberate step forward. The smile remains.

RICHARD
Arthur Pendelton.

Valerie's hand twitches.

RICHARD
Tech money. Loud. Obnoxious. Toured your modernist listing on Tuesday. Missed his own board meeting on Wednesday.

VALERIE
Clients experience market volatility. Arthur is likely reconsidering his liquid assets in a tax haven. It is a turnkey property, Richard. It requires a highly specific buyer.

Richard takes another step. The office suddenly feels entirely too small.

RICHARD
Marcus Vance. CEO of Vance Logistics. Toured the property Thursday. His wife filed a missing persons report this morning.

Valerie's thumb finds the edge of her silk French cuff. She rubs the fabric. Hard.

VALERIE
High-net-worth individuals value their privacy. I do not track my buyers' personal itineraries once the showing concludes.

Richard reaches the desk. He looks down at a small, antique wooden replica of a Victorian home sitting next to her pen holder. His large hands gently, almost lovingly, trace the miniature roofline.

RICHARD
I have always admired the craftsmanship of these little structures. Every room perfectly visible. Every tiny, fragile inhabitant placed exactly where the creator wants them. No secrets. No locked doors.

He looks up from the miniature. The calm smile vanishes.

Richard rounds the desk. Valerie backs up, but her spine hits the edge of the mahogany. Trapped.

Richard leans in, invading her personal space. The smell of expensive cologne and stale coffee washes over her.

Valerie scrubs frantically at an invisible stain on her cuff.

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
Three billionaires walk into your exclusive listing, Valerie. Zero billionaires walk out. The food chain is broken. And you are holding the leash to a very hungry asset.

VALERIE
The listing agreement is ironclad. I have the exclusive right to sell.

RICHARD
I am the managing broker. I own the ecosystem. You are going to transfer the listing to me. Tonight.

Valerie stops rubbing her cuff. She looks up, her icy veneer locking back into place, driven by sheer survival instinct.

VALERIE
The seller demands a discrete, highly curated transactional experience. You lack the delicate touch required for this architecture.

Richard leans closer. His nose is inches from her injured cheek.

RICHARD
Transfer the listing. Or I call the police, and we see how well your flawless aesthetic holds up in a cage.

Richard straightens up, adjusting his lapels. He gives the miniature Victorian roof one last tap.

RICHARD
I will expect the transfer paperwork on my desk by eight in the morning. Do not make me devour you, Valerie.

Richard turns and strides out into the darkened bullpen.

Valerie remains pinned against the desk. Her chest heaves. She looks down at her silk cuff. 

The fabric is frayed.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. REAL ESTATE OFFICE - VALERIE'S PRIVATE OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie attempts to deflect, pivoting the conversation to the architectural value of the property, but Richard drops his sophisticated facade. He physically invades her personal space, leaning in so close she can feel his breath. Through clenched teeth, he delivers a terrifying, quiet hiss: she must host a massive, heavily publicized open house gala at the property tonight. If she refuses, he will seize the deed and destroy her career. Trapped between a starving monster and a corporate shark, Valerie obsessively scrubs at her cuffs.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Sets the ticking clock and the climax of the sequence. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie's desire to keep the house a secret vs. Richard's demand to open it to the public. Value Shift (+/-): Cornered (-) to utterly doomed (--).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. REAL ESTATE OFFICE - VALERIE'S PRIVATE OFFICE - CONTINUOUS

Valerie stands rigidly behind her massive glass desk. A fortress of her own making. She meticulously aligns a stack of heavy-stock, glossy property brochures. 

Richard paces the perimeter of her office. He runs a finger along the frame of a vintage architectural rendering, intentionally leaving a smudge on the glass.

VALERIE
It is a masterclass in brutalist symmetry, Richard. The cantilevered concrete, the bespoke load-bearing glass. You do not just throw open the doors of a property like this to the general public. The architectural pedigree demands a highly surgical approach.

Richard stops pacing. He turns to her, offering a perfectly capped, unnerving smile. 

RICHARD
Surgical. I like that word. It sounds clean. But you see, Valerie, an apex predator doesn't use a scalpel. It unhinges its jaw. It consumes the entire ecosystem in one bite.

VALERIE
(cool, detached)
The exclusivity is the primary asset. By strictly curating the buyer pool, I am protecting the firm's overarching prestige. 

RICHARD
You’ve been hoarding this listing for four weeks. Zero public showings. Just whisper campaigns and a handful of clients who suddenly drop off the map. You are starving the firm. And I do not tolerate parasites on my ledger.

Valerie’s posture remains flawless, icy. Her thumb subtly drops to the edge of her silk cuff, grazing the fabric.

VALERIE
The seller is highly particular. If we flood the foyer with unqualified looky-loos, the mystique evaporates. I will secure the right buyer when the market aligns with the property's... unique appetite.

RICHARD
(chuckling)
The market is a slaughterhouse. We don't wait for the stars to align. We ring the dinner bell.

VALERIE
It is simply not ready for a mass viewing. The plumbing infrastructure, the--

Richard steps forward. The polite smile vanishes instantly. The temperature in the room seems to plummet. He rounds the glass desk with heavy, deliberate steps.

Valerie holds her ground, but her eyes rapidly track his approach. 

He doesn't stop. He closes the distance until he is mere inches from her face, physically trapping her against the sharp edge of her own desk. 

Valerie’s breath hitches. Her flawless mask cracks, her eyes widening as she leans back, but there is nowhere left to go.

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
You are hosting a gala. Tonight.

VALERIE
(voice trembling)
Tonight? You can't. The property isn't safe for that kind of volume--

RICHARD
Champagne, caviar, and every high-net-worth vulture in this city walking through those oversized mahogany doors. 

VALERIE
Richard, listen to me--

RICHARD
No, you listen. If those doors do not open at eight o'clock, I will personally seize the deed. I will strip your license. I will liquidate your pending commissions, and I will personally ensure you spend the rest of your pathetic life showing roach-infested studio apartments to people who beg for rent extensions.

Valerie stares into his dead, shark-like eyes. The threat of the slums--the poverty, the powerlessness--hits her like a physical blow to the stomach. 

Her hands drop sharply to her sides. Her nails dig into the pristine white fabric of her expensive silk sleeves. 

She begins to scrub at her cuffs. Hard. Obsessively trying to remove a stain that isn't there.

RICHARD
Do we have an understanding?

Valerie continues scrubbing. The fabric rasps loudly in the suffocating quiet of the office. She looks down at her trembling hands, then slowly back up to Richard's unblinking stare.

VALERIE
I will arrange the catering.

Richard straightens up, casually smoothing his silk tie. The sophisticated, unbothered facade snaps instantly back into place.

RICHARD
Excellent. I love a good party.

Richard turns and strides out of the office. The heavy glass door clicks shut behind him.

Valerie stands completely alone. The scrubbing intensifies, frantic and raw, her breathing quickening as the reality of the impending slaughter settles over her.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. REAL ESTATE OFFICE - VALERIE'S PRIVATE OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Picking up exactly where we left off, Richard stands inches from Valerie's face in her private office. Valerie obsessively scrubs at her expensive cuffs, her eyes darting to the locked door. Richard straightens his suit, flashes a calm, unnerving smile, and drops a heavy stack of VIP gala invitations onto her desk. He turns and strides out, leaving the door wide open. Valerie hyperventilates, staring at the invitations. She opens her floor safe, grabs her clipboard and the heavy velvet bag of diamonds, shoving the bag deep into her blazer pocket before marching out.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Bridges the previous sequence's threat into immediate physical action, forcing the protagonist into the climax. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to find a way out vs. Richard cementing the trap. Value Shift (+/-): From cornered (-) to resigned and doomed (- -).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. REAL ESTATE OFFICE - VALERIE'S PRIVATE OFFICE - CONTINUOUS

Richard stands so close to Valerie they are breathing the same recycled, air-conditioned oxygen. 

The silence in the room is heavy, suffocating. 

Valerie’s eyes dart to the heavy brass lock on the closed office door. Trapped. Her right hand drifts to her left wrist. Her thumb aggressively SCRUBS at the edge of her pristine silk cuff. Back and forth. Erasing an invisible stain.

RICHARD
(calm, conversational)
Do you know how a strangler fig operates, Valerie? 

Valerie keeps her chin high. The icy veneer holds, but her thumb keeps scrubbing the silk.

VALERIE
The Q3 projections on the mansion are robust, Richard. If we flood the market with a massive showing, we dilute the exclusivity. The imported Italian marble alone dictates a curated, single-buyer approach.

RICHARD
It wraps around the host tree. Quietly. Patiently. By the time the host realizes it’s being consumed... the canopy is already gone.

Richard reaches up. He lazily adjusts his perfectly straight silk tie. He smiles. It’s a calm, terrifyingly hollow expression. 

VALERIE
I am protecting the firm’s liability. The architecture has... anomalies. The foundation requires a specialized appraisal before we invite a crowd.

RICHARD
Anomalies. Like Arthur Pendelton evaporating into thin air during his private walkthrough?

Valerie’s thumb digs so hard into her cuff the fabric tears slightly.

VALERIE
Arthur had a sudden offshore relocation. I forwarded his escrow cancellation to accounting. It’s fully documented.

RICHARD
Flawless paperwork. As always.

Richard reaches inside his tailored jacket. He pulls out a thick, heavy stack of gold-foil envelopes. 

He drops them onto her glass desk. THWACK. 

RICHARD
VIP gala invitations. Fifty names. The apex predators of this city. Senators. Tech acquisitions. Gemma’s entire rolodex. They're all expecting a show tonight at your little modernist marvel.

VALERIE
Gemma is a liability. She doesn't understand the asset's underlying value. 

The unnerving smile vanishes from Richard's face. The sophisticated facade shatters. He invades her personal space again, leaning down until his nose almost touches hers.

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
She understands hunger. You’ve been holding out on me, Val. A property that practically generates wealth overnight, and you thought you could keep the feast to yourself? 

Valerie stops scrubbing her cuff. Her hands tremble.

RICHARD
You open the doors tonight. You feed them whatever it is you’re feeding them. And I take my cut. If you embarrass me, I won't just fire you. I will make sure the only property you ever list again is a cardboard box on Skid Row.

Richard steps back. He straightens his suit jacket, restoring his pristine armor. 

He turns on his heel, walks to the door, and unlocks it. He pulls it open and strides out into the hallway. 

He leaves the door WIDE OPEN. 

The chaotic, bustling noise of the bullpen bleeds into the private office. A blatant violation of her sanctuary. 

Valerie is left alone. 

Her icy mask completely dissolves. She grips the edge of the glass desk. She hyperventilates, her chest heaving as she stares at the stack of gold-foil invitations. 

They look like a stack of polished tombstones. 

She drops to her hands and knees behind the desk. She rips back the corner of the Persian rug, revealing a heavy steel floor safe. 

She spins the dial. 34. 12. 88. 

CLICK. 

She pulls the heavy door open. Inside sits a pristine leather clipboard and a bloated, black velvet bag. 

Valerie reaches in and hefts the velvet bag. 

CLINK. The unmistakable, heavy sound of raw, blood-free diamonds shifting against each other. The physical weight of her damnation. 

She stares at the bag. Her breathing slows. The panic hardens into something cold and resigned. 

She shoves the heavy bag deep into the pocket of her tailored blazer. The weight pulls the fabric down, ruining the silhouette. 

She grabs the leather clipboard and stands up. 

Valerie adjusts her torn cuff, smoothing it out. The ice queen returns. 

She marches out through the wide-open door, stepping directly into the chaotic noise of the bullpen.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - EVENING
scenes.narrative_action: A fleet of luxury cars and catering vans idle in the driveway. Valerie stands paralyzed by the towering front doors of the mansion, clutching her clipboard like a shield against her chest. Richard bulldozes past her, physically grabbing her shoulder and steering her inside as he ushers in a massive, bustling crowd of caterers carrying trays of champagne and a string quartet hauling cellos.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establishes the scale of the impending disaster and the sheer volume of innocent victims arriving. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to block the entrance vs. Richard physically forcing the event forward. Value Shift (+/-): From hesitant resistance (-) to completely overwhelmed (- -).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - EVENING

Valets sprint like panicked ants. A fleet of matte-black Mercedes, Teslas, and idling catering vans clog the circular driveway.

The MANSION looms. Brutalist concrete and jagged, asymmetrical glass cutting into the twilight sky. It doesn't look like a home. It looks like an open jaw.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) stands dead center on the limestone portico. The towering mahogany front doors are cracked open behind her. The abyss waits.

She clutches a leather-bound clipboard to her chest. A shield. Her knuckles are stark white.

A stray JUNE BUG buzzes erratically, smacking into the glass pane beside her. It drops to the welcome mat, landing on its back, legs kicking frantically against the inevitable.

Valerie kneels. Her icy, tailored exterior cracks for a microsecond. She gently scoops the struggling insect onto the edge of her clipboard and tips it into the safety of the hydrangeas.

She stands, immediately scrubbing her thumb over an invisible smudge on her pristine white French cuff. Scrubbing hard.

RICHARD STERLING (50s) materializes from the chaos of the driveway. Bespoke suit, silver hair, moving with the casual, terrifying stride of an apex predator inspecting a fresh kill.

RICHARD
Look at them, Val.

Valerie flinches, halting her frantic scrubbing. She forces her spine rigid.

RICHARD
(gesturing to the driveway)
Salmon swimming upstream. The sheer density of capital migrating here tonight... it alters the local gravity.

VALERIE
Richard. The staging isn't optimal. The smart-home integration is glitching on the lower levels. We need to push the gala to next quarter.

Richard smiles. It is a terrifyingly calm expression.

RICHARD
Push the gala.

VALERIE
It’s a liability. The foundation is settling. I'm seeing micro-fractures near the load-bearing walls. The aesthetic ROI simply isn't there yet to justify this volume of foot traffic.

A CATERING CREW marches up the steps, carrying massive silver trays stacked with crystal champagne flutes and towers of raw oysters.

Valerie steps sideways, physically blocking the center of the double doors.

VALERIE
(to the Lead Caterer)
Hold. The foyer flow isn't prepped for service.

The Lead Caterer stops, confused, trays wavering.

Richard's smile vanishes. The sophisticated facade drops. He steps directly into Valerie's personal space. Too close.

He doesn't raise his voice. He leans down, his breath grazing her ear.

RICHARD
(through clenched teeth)
Nature abhors a vacuum, Valerie. I gave you the oxygen to breathe at my firm. Do not choke on it now.

VALERIE
(voice tight, trembling)
You don't understand the structural integrity of this property. It can't handle them all at once.

RICHARD
I understand leverage. And right now, you have absolutely none.

Richard's hand shoots out. His fingers clamp down on Valerie's shoulder. Heavy. Immovable.

He physically pivots her away from the doorway.

RICHARD
(barking at the caterers)
Move in! Set up by the infinity pool! Let's get some blood flowing in this place!

The caterers surge forward. The dam breaks.

A STRING QUARTET follows right on their heels, hauling bulky cello cases up the steps. A thick fiberglass case bumps hard against Valerie's hip, forcing her further backward into the doorframe.

Valerie's breathing turns shallow, panicked. She scrubs at her cuff again. Faster. Harder. The skin underneath turns red.

VALERIE
(over the rising noise)
Richard, there are too many variables! We cannot control the flow of traffic once they're inside!

RICHARD
We don't control the river, Val. We just build the dam and collect the power.

He keeps his iron grip on her shoulder, his fingers digging deep into the expensive silk of her blazer. He uses her own momentum against her, steering her backward, over the threshold.

RICHARD
Smile. You're about to be a very rich woman. Assuming you don't drown in the shallow end.

Valerie’s eyes dart wildly to the passing faces flooding through the doors. A tech bro in a velvet jacket. A politician's wife laughing loudly. A rival agent dripping in diamonds.

Dozens of them. Walking blindly into the mouth.

Richard shoves her gently but firmly inside. The dark shadows of the foyer swallow them both.

The massive front doors stand wide open. Waiting for the rest.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie desperately tries to block the hallway, loudly inventing a lie about a sudden plumbing emergency to evacuate the building. Richard immediately steps in front of her, cutting off her path. He smiles calmly, whispering a threat about seizing her real estate license, and physically pivots her by the shoulders toward the arriving guests. Defeated, Valerie forces a flawless, icy professional smile and begins handing out glossy property brochures.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Removes Valerie's last chance to verbally stop the event, locking her into compliance. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie attempts to sabotage the party with a lie vs. Richard overriding her authority with threats. Value Shift (+/-): From desperate hope (+) to trapped compliance (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND FOYER - CONTINUOUS

The grand mahogany double doors stand wide open. Beyond them, a fleet of black town cars idles in the driveway. 

VALERIE VANCE stands dead center in the foyer, acting as a human barricade in a tailored, immaculate pantsuit. 

Her right thumb violently rubs the cuff of her left silk sleeve. Scrubbing an invisible stain. Over and over. Her breathing is shallow, rapid.

A cluster of WEALTHY GUESTS steps over the threshold, their designer heels clicking sharply against the polished concrete. 

Valerie steps directly into their path. She raises her hands, projecting her voice over the ambient chatter.

VALERIE
Ladies and gentlemen, I must ask you to step back outside. We are experiencing a catastrophic hydrostatic pressure anomaly.

The guests pause, exchanging confused, irritated glances. 

VALERIE
(forcing a tight smile)
A severe greywater backflow in the primary laterals. The structural integrity of the lower basin is temporarily compromised. For your own safety, we need to vacate the floor plan immediately.

RICHARD STERLING materializes from the shadow of the floating staircase. He does not walk; he glides. A shark in a bespoke suit. 

He smoothly steps between Valerie and the guests. He flashes a warm, blindingly confident grin at the crowd.

RICHARD
A slight exaggeration from my top associate. 

Richard turns his back to the guests, facing Valerie. The smile remains plastered on his mouth, but his eyes are dead, black stones. 

VALERIE
Richard, look at the imported marble near the baseboards. It’s buckling. If we don’t clear the perimeter, the liability will be--

RICHARD
(calmly)
Do you know what happens when you introduce a new species into an established terrarium, Valerie?

Valerie stares at him. Her thumb digs harder into her cuff, the silk beginning to fray under her nail. 

VALERIE
This isn't a conversation about the landscaping. The foundation cannot support this volume of foot traffic tonight. 

RICHARD
The ecosystem panics. The weaker insects scurry to the glass, desperate to escape. But the glass is thick. And the predator is already inside.

He takes a step forward. The scent of expensive cologne and peppermint washes over her. 

VALERIE
(voice tight)
I am telling you, we need to lock those doors. 

Richard steps entirely into her personal space. The polished toes of his oxfords touch her shoes. He towers over her, blocking her from the view of the guests. 

He leans down. His mouth hovers mere inches from her ear. The sophisticated, philosophical veneer vanishes. 

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
You are a very small doll in a very expensive house. If you open your mouth again to ruin this showing, I will personally revoke your broker's license. 

Valerie freezes. Her thumb stops rubbing the cuff. 

RICHARD
I will bankrupt you. I will ensure you are sleeping on a public bus bench by Tuesday morning. Do you understand the architecture of that reality?

The blood drains from Valerie's face. The ghost of her childhood eviction flashes behind her eyes. The bags on the curb. The powerlessness. 

Her hands fall limply to her sides. 

RICHARD
Good.

Richard grabs Valerie by the shoulders. His grip is iron, his fingers digging into her collarbone. He physically pivots her one hundred and eighty degrees.

She is now facing the arriving guests. Facing the waiting feast. 

Richard reaches into his breast pocket and extracts a thick stack of heavy-stock, glossy property brochures. He presses them hard into Valerie's chest.

She takes them. Her hands tremble slightly against the glossy paper. 

RICHARD
(booming, cheerful)
Welcome! Please, take a brochure from Valerie. The property speaks for itself. 

Richard steps aside, leaving Valerie exposed. The guests flow past her, their chatter echoing in the cavernous, hungry space. 

A WOMAN IN DIAMONDS pauses in front of Valerie, adjusting a fur wrap. 

WOMAN IN DIAMONDS
Is the backflow issue sorted, dear?

Valerie looks at the woman. Then she looks down the long, dark hallway behind her. The shadows seem to stretch, and a warm, metallic draft exhales from the depths of the house. 

Valerie’s posture snaps straight. The trembling stops. Her flawless, icy professional mask locks firmly into place. 

VALERIE
Completely resolved. The flow of the house is absolutely unhindered. 

She peels a glossy brochure from the stack and hands it to the woman. 

VALERIE
Enjoy the showing. 

The woman smiles, entirely oblivious, and walks into the belly of the mansion. 

Valerie stands rigid at the threshold, dealing out brochures to the passing guests like a blackjack dealer at the gates of hell. 

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - FRONT STEPS - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Toby bounds up the front steps wearing an ill-fitting, clearly rented tuxedo. He clutches a clipboard of his own, his face flushed with earnest excitement. He pauses to admire the brutalist architecture, completely oblivious to the danger, before eagerly marching through the massive front doors.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Introduces the moral compass into the kill zone, raising the emotional stakes. Micro-Conflict (><): Toby wants to do a perfect job vs. the house's silent, looming threat. Value Shift (+/-): From nervous anticipation (-) to eager excitement (+).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - FRONT STEPS - MOMENTS LATER

Concrete and glass. A brutalist monolith against the twilight. It doesn't sit on the landscape; it anchors into it like a tick.

TOBY bounds up the floating concrete steps. He wears a rented tuxedo. The sleeves are a half-inch too short. The collar chokes his Adam's apple. 

He clutches a plastic clipboard to his chest like a bulletproof vest. His face is flushed, slick with eager sweat.

Toby stops at the landing. He taps the clipboard against his thigh. Tap. Tap. Tap. 

He pulls out a cheap flip-phone, hits speed dial.

TOBY
(into phone)
Good evening, Ms. Vance. It's Toby. I have arrived at the perimeter.

He clears his throat, tugging at his restrictive collar.

TOBY
(into phone)
Just confirming I have the finalized guest roster. And the liability waivers. All printed.

Toby looks up. He really looks at the mansion's facade. His cheerful smile falters. His eyes narrow, tracing the massive cantilevered rooflines.

TOBY
(into phone)
Ms. Vance, looking at the exterior load distribution... the cantilever overhang on the west wing doesn't make structural sense. The tension cables are anchored into nothing. It defies basic tensile physics.

A deep, sub-bass HUM vibrates through the concrete beneath his cheap dress shoes. It sounds like a starving stomach rumbling.

Toby looks down at his feet. The vibration rattles his bones. 

His hands tremble. The clipboard slips. Papers scatter across the pristine, blood-cold concrete.

Toby drops to his knees. Panic flashes in his eyes. 

TOBY
(muttering)
N-no, no. The r-roster.

He scrambles to gather the pages. He stacks them. The edges aren't flush. His breathing hitches. He taps the stack against the stone step until it forms a perfect, flawless right angle. 

He does it again. Perfect 90 degrees. 

He breathes out. The stutter recedes. The cheerful optimism returns, pasting itself firmly over his face.

He stands and adjusts his crooked clip-on bow tie.

TOBY
(into phone)
Must be a high-yield geothermal HVAC system. Incredible. 

He snaps the phone shut. 

Toby strides toward the massive, obsidian front doors. They are heavy, imposing, designed to keep the world out. 

Before he even touches the handle, the heavy doors unlatch with a wet, metallic CLACK. They swing inward. A silent invitation.

Toby smiles, utterly blind to the jaws opening around him.

TOBY
Automated entry. Fancy.

He marches over the threshold, swallowed by the darkness of the foyer. 

The heavy doors slam shut behind him.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - CLOAKROOM / FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Toby eagerly stations himself at the coat check in the grand foyer. He nervously stutters through polite greetings with wealthy socialites, meticulously organizing their heavy winter coats into perfect right angles on the brass hangers. Across the room, Valerie watches him arrange the garments. Her icy corporate exterior visibly fractures; her hands tremble, and she drops a stack of brochures. She stares at the floorboards beneath Toby's polished dress shoes, imagining the predatory hum vibrating through them.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Highlights Valerie's internal guilt by contrasting her terrifying knowledge with Toby's earnest innocence. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to warn Toby vs. her paralyzing fear of Richard and financial ruin. Value Shift (+/-): From numb compliance (-) to active, sickening guilt (- -).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - CLOAKROOM / FOYER - CONTINUOUS

A heavy, snow-dusted mink coat is thrust over the cloakroom counter. 

TOBY takes it. His cheap, ill-fitting suit jacket rides up his wrists. 

TOBY
(earnest)
Good evening, sir. I will ensure this is securely stored for the duration of the gala.

The wealthy GUEST ignores him, striding past the velvet ropes. 

Toby drapes the mink over a heavy brass hanger. He places it on the rack. He stares at the spacing between the coats. It is uneven. 

His hand twitches. He carefully slides the hangers until the gaps are identical. Perfect right angles.

Across the cavernous foyer, VALERIE stands half-hidden behind a monolithic concrete pillar. She clutches a thick stack of glossy property brochures against her chest. 

She isn't looking at the billionaires admiring the vaulted ceilings. She is staring dead at Toby. 

Her immaculate, icy composure is fracturing. Her breathing is shallow. Her right thumb aggressively, rhythmically scrubs at an invisible stain on the cuff of her silk blouse. 

A SOCIALITE glides past Valerie, trailing expensive perfume.

SOCIALITE
The space is simply to die for, Valerie.

VALERIE
Unparalleled square footage. It truly consumes you.

The Socialite laughs and drifts away. Valerie doesn't smile. She looks back at the cloakroom. 

Toby steps out from behind the counter, walking to the center of the foyer to adjust a velvet rope stanchion. 

Toby's scuffed dress shoes rest on the pristine, imported floorboards. 

Valerie’s hands begin to tremble. The glossy brochures vibrate against her chest. 

Toby looks up and catches her eye. He offers a bright, eager wave and hurries over to her. 

TOBY
Valerie. The brass claim tickets. Should I cross-reference them alphanumerically with the VIP registry? I want to make sure everyone's property is fully protected.

VALERIE
Just facilitate a seamless entry. High-touch service yields high-yield retention. Do not overcomplicate the logistics.

She scrubs her cuff harder. The skin beneath the silk is turning red. 

TOBY
Of course. It's just...

Toby looks down at his feet. He shifts his weight. 

TOBY
The floorboards. They're vibrating.

Valerie freezes. Her thumb stops rubbing. 

TOBY
I reviewed the structural blueprints again last night. There shouldn't be a sub-floor mechanical unit in this quadrant. Is it an engineering anomaly?

Valerie stares at the floor beneath his cheap shoes. She knows exactly what is beneath the wood. She can almost hear the wet, predatory hum vibrating through the joists. Waiting. 

VALERIE
It is radiant heating. Bespoke Brazilian walnut. Fifty dollars a square foot, Toby. It is designed to resonate thermal energy.

Toby crouches down. He presses his bare palm flat against the wood. 

TOBY
It feels... organic. Like a pulse.

Valerie’s tremor violently returns. Her fingers spasm. 

The heavy stack of brochures slips from her grip. 

SMACK. Dozens of thick, glossy pages scatter across the Brazilian walnut. 

Toby immediately drops to both knees. Right onto the mouth of the house. 

VALERIE
Don't.

Toby ignores her, his hands frantically gathering the scattered paper. He squares the edges of the brochures against the floor, tapping them into perfect right angles. 

TOBY
I've got them. Don't worry. You know, if the heating coils are misaligned, I could crawl into the sub-basement and take a look. Save the firm a contractor fee.

Valerie looks down at him. He is so small, so desperate to be useful. 

The floor hums louder, a low, guttural vibration that rattles the brass hangers in the distant cloakroom. 

She opens her mouth. The warning is right there on her tongue. 

But she pictures Richard's smiling, predatory face. She pictures her empty bank account. The eviction notice from her childhood pinned to a rotting door. 

Valerie takes a slow, deliberate step backward. Off the walnut. Onto the cold, hard marble. 

VALERIE
Leave them on the counter. And stay out of the sub-basement, Toby. That is a... a liability.

Toby looks up, smiling brightly, clutching the perfectly squared stack of lies. 

TOBY
You can count on me, Valerie.

Valerie turns her back to him. She walks away, leaving him kneeling on the humming floor.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND HALLWAY - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: The gala reaches peak capacity. Waiters weave through the dense crowd, carrying silver trays of champagne deep into the house's belly. The string quartet plays a lively, elegant piece in the corner. Valerie paces near the back wall, her stomach visibly churning as she obsessively checks her watch. The antique grandfather clock in the hallway begins a deep, resonant chime. One. Two. Three. As it approaches eight o'clock, the floorboards subtly vibrate under the guests' feet.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The ticking clock expires, acting as the calm before the storm. Micro-Conflict (><): The guests enjoy the luxury vs. the house preparing to strike. Value Shift (+/-): From oblivious celebration (+) to the brink of terror (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND HALLWAY - LATER

The gala is a living organism. A dense, suffocating throng of bespoke tuxedos and backless designer gowns. 

Waiters weave through the crowd like red blood cells, carrying silver trays of champagne deeper into the cavernous, dimly lit belly of the house. 

Tucked into a sharp architectural alcove, a STRING QUARTET saws through a frantic, elegant Vivaldi piece. The music barely cuts through the deafening roar of oblivious wealth.

VALERIE (30s) paces near the back wall of the grand hallway. Her stomach visibly rises and falls beneath her tailored blazer. She checks her Rolex. 7:56 PM.

Her icy veneer is fracturing. She aggressively scrubs at an invisible stain on her pristine white French cuff, her fingernails digging into the expensive fabric.

TOBY (20s) approaches from the kitchen corridor. He clutches a stack of glossy property brochures to his chest like a shield. He looks pale, sweating through his cheap suit.

TOBY
M-Ms. Vance?

Toby places the brochures onto a marble console table. He compulsively squares the edges, aligning them into perfect, ninety-degree angles with the table's corners. 

TOBY
(stuttering)
I was reviewing the sub-basement schematics in the utility room. The square footage doesn't match the exterior dimensions. There's a... a massive void directly beneath us. 

VALERIE
It's an architectural negative space, Toby. It maximizes thermal efficiency. 

Valerie scrubs her cuff harder. The fabric begins to fray.

VALERIE
You've logged your hours. Your ROI on this evening has peaked. Go home.

TOBY
But the structural integrity-- the acoustic dampening isn't up to code. If there's a fault line--

VALERIE
These are high-net-worth individuals. They require a frictionless environment, not a lecture on zoning laws. Leave. Now.

GEMMA (30s) materializes from the dense crowd, gliding forward like a shark in a sequined dress. 

GEMMA
Sending the help away before the main course, Val? How terribly nouveau riche of you.

Gemma holds up her phone, pretending to check a text message. Instead, she uses the black screen to meticulously inspect her own reflection, adjusting a stray hair. 

GEMMA
Though I suppose keeping overhead low is your signature move. That blazer is incredibly brave. Is it vintage?

Valerie drops her hands to her sides, forcing her posture into a rigid, flawless line. 

VALERIE
It’s bespoke, Gemma. And this is a closed showing. The guest list is strictly curated.

GEMMA
Oh, please. Richard is already upstairs measuring the primary suite. You can’t gatekeep a commission this fat forever. We all know you're hiding something massive in this property.

At the far end of the hallway, a massive antique GRANDFATHER CLOCK clicks. The heavy brass pendulum swings.

CHIME. 

One.

The deep, resonant sound cuts beneath the chatter. The string quartet plays on, oblivious. 

Valerie glances at her Rolex again. 7:59 PM. 

VALERIE
I'm not gatekeeping, Gemma. I'm providing an immersive market experience. 

CHIME. 

Two.

Toby frowns. His eyes dart to the floorboards. He reaches out and nudges the stack of brochures a millimeter to the left. 

TOBY
Do you... do you feel that?

CHIME. 

Three.

A subtle vibration hums through the imported hardwood. Inside a passing waiter's tray, the champagne in a dozen crystal flutes ripples with concentric circles. 

Gemma ignores Toby, stepping directly into Valerie's personal space. Under the guise of a friendly smile, Gemma aggressively picks at her own thumbnail. A tiny bead of blood wells up on her cuticle.

GEMMA
Whatever gimmick you're pulling to inflate the asking price, it won't save your sinking portfolio. I'm taking this listing, Val. 

CHIME. 

Four.

Valerie looks at Gemma. Then at Toby, who is now staring at the floor in absolute horror. 

VALERIE
It's all yours.

Valerie takes a slow, deliberate step backward. Out of the center of the hallway. She retreats into the deep shadows of the archway, her face slipping into darkness.

CHIME. 

Five.

The vibration intensifies. It’s no longer a hum. It’s a low, guttural purr emanating from the very foundation of the house. 

Toby drops a brochure. He doesn't pick it up. 

TOBY
Ms. Vance. The foundation is shifting. We need to initiate an evacuation protocol--

CHIME. 

Six.

VALERIE
(from the shadows)
The market is correcting itself, Toby. Stay exactly where you are.

CHIME. 

Seven.

The guests continue to laugh, tossing back expensive liquor. The string quartet hits a feverish, chaotic crescendo. 

The floorboards beneath Gemma and Toby groan loudly. The wood warps upward, bowing like a giant ribcage expanding to take a breath. 

Gemma finally looks down, her fake smile vanishing. 

CHIME. 

Eight.

The grand chandelier above them violently flickers out. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - VARIOUS ROOMS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: On the final, deafening toll of the grandfather clock, thick slabs of reinforced steel violently slam down over every single window and exterior door with bone-rattling force. Deadbolts engage simultaneously with a synchronized, metallic snap. The string quartet screeches to a terrified halt. The sudden, panicked gasps of the trapped partygoers echo through the suffocating, sealed rooms.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Executes the point of no return; the trap snaps shut, sealing the victims inside. Micro-Conflict (><): The guests want to leave vs. the house physically locking them inside. Value Shift (+/-): From confused (-) to absolute panic (- -).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - VARIOUS ROOMS - CONTINUOUS

The grandfather clock in the grand foyer swings its heavy brass pendulum. 

BONG. 

The twelfth and final toll. It does not just ring; it reverberates through the pristine hardwood floors, vibrating up through the soles of sixty designer shoes. 

Valerie stands near the entryway arch, frozen. Her icy professional smile remains plastered on her face, but her right thumb digs aggressively into the silk of her left cuff. Scrubbing at a stain that isn't there. Scrubbing hard.

Richard looms over her, swirling a two-finger pour of scotch. He tracks her movements with dead, shark-like eyes.

RICHARD
The ecosystem always corrects itself, Val. You can’t just overgraze the market and expect the sheep not to notice the blood on the grass. 

Valerie opens her mouth to deflect, to point out the imported Italian marble of the archway--

But the clock’s reverberation does not fade. It amplifies into a mechanical, grinding roar. 

Above the massive floor-to-ceiling windows, the seamless architecture betrays them. 

SLAM. 

A three-inch-thick slab of reinforced steel violently drops over the foyer glass, obliterating the moonlight. The floor literally shakes. 

SLAM. SLAM. SLAM.
