A deafening domino effect ripples through the mansion. In the dining room, the lounge, the conservatory. Heavy steel guillotine blades crash down over every single window and exterior door, severing the house from the outside world.

In the corner of the living room, the string quartet violently jerks. The cellist drags his bow across the strings in a harsh, screeching discord. The music dies instantly.

SNAP-CLACK-SNAP.

A synchronized chorus of heavy metallic deadbolts engaging echoes down the suffocating hallways. 

Total, suffocating silence follows. The ambient glow of the city is gone, replaced only by the harsh, sterile buzz of the crystal chandeliers. 

By the hors d'oeuvres table, Gemma drops her champagne flute. It shatters. Her bubbly facade evaporates into pale horror. Her thumb finds her index finger. She begins picking at her cuticle. Tearing the skin until a bead of dark red blood wells up. 

By the front door, Toby stands paralyzed. He reaches out, grabbing the heavy brass handle. He pulls. It doesn't budge. 

Toby’s breathing goes shallow. He drops a stack of glossy property brochures onto the console table, his shaking hands frantically sliding them into perfect, flush right angles.

TOBY
(stuttering)
Th-these structural tolerances. Th-this isn't on the load-bearing b-blueprints. 

Richard’s unbothered dominance shatters. He drops his scotch. He steps directly into Valerie’s personal space, backing her against the wall. He is inches from her face. 

RICHARD
(clenched teeth)
What kind of cheap, theatrical stunt is this? Open the doors. Now. 

Valerie doesn't look at him. She stares blankly at the solid steel wall where the glass used to be. Her thumb scrubs her silk cuff faster, harder, frantic. 

VALERIE
I didn't do this. 

A woman in the lounge whimpers. A man by the bar shouts a panicked curse. 

The collective, terrified gasp of the trapped partygoers sucks the remaining oxygen from the room. The air pressure drops. 

The house is sealed. The house is hungry.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The brutalist architecture immediately shifts into a weapon. The grand marble staircase violently flattens, the steps retracting to form a steep, frictionless slide. A dozen screaming guests lose their footing and plummet downward. At the bottom of the slide, the basement doors burst open, revealing grinding, mechanical gears that pull the screaming victims into the darkness.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The monster unleashes its full, indiscriminate power, beginning the massacre. Micro-Conflict (><): The guests fight for physical survival vs. the house's geographical manipulation. Value Shift (+/-): From trapped panic (-) to lethal, bloody chaos (- -).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS

A bottleneck of bespoke tuxedos and diamond necklaces. Fifty panicked GUESTS surge up the floating marble steps, trapped like rats in a designer cage. 

VALERIE VANCE clings to the brass handrail. Her flawless posture remains, but her thumb obsessively, furiously scrubs at an invisible stain on her silk French cuff.

RICHARD STERLING presses up right behind her. He physically invades her space, his chest brushing her shoulder. He wears a calm, dead-eyed smile.

RICHARD
(whispering)
An apex predator knows when the trap is sprung, Valerie. Unlock the front doors. Now. Or I will personally ensure you never sell a tent on Skid Row.

VALERIE
The biometric locks are simply... recalibrating. It’s a feature of the smart-home integration. Top-tier exclusivity ensures absolute privacy.

Valerie’s voice is ice, but her knuckles are bone-white against the brass. 

Below them, GEMMA FROST aggressively picks at her own cuticles. A drop of blood wells on her thumb. She forces a bubbly, hollow laugh, checking her reflection in her dead phone screen.

GEMMA
Honestly, Val? If this is your idea of a captive audience, it’s a bit derivative. I’m missing a gala in Malibu for this concrete tomb.

TOBY HIGGINS is pressed against the brutalist wall. His eyes dart wildly, mapping the ceiling joints. He compulsively straightens the right angles of his clipboard.

TOBY
M-Ms. Vance? The load-bearing columns... they’re a-a-uncoupling. The blueprints didn't have hydraulic sub-strata. 

A DEAFENING GROAN reverberates through the walls. 

The architecture itself breathes.

Beneath the guests' feet, the ninety-degree angles of the grand marble staircase violently SNAP.

The vertical risers retract. The horizontal treads tilt. 

In a single, fluid motion, the entire staircase flattens into a forty-five-degree, frictionless marble slide.

Gravity takes the room.

A dozen guests instantly lose their footing. High heels snap. Tuxedo shoes skid. 

They PLUMMET downward in a tangle of limbs and shrieks.

Valerie lunges, wrapping her arms around a heavy, brutalist wall sconce. Her legs dangle over the slick, polished slope.

Richard doesn't reach for the wall. He grabs the shoulders of an ELDERLY TECH CEO beside him, violently shoving the old man down the chute to propel himself upward onto the second-floor landing.

RICHARD
Gravity always favors the heavyweights!

At the bottom of the slide, the reinforced basement doors BLAST OPEN.

No wine cellar. Just a roaring, mechanical abyss. 

Massive, rusted iron gears grind against each other. The house’s digestive tract.

The first wave of sliding guests hits the threshold. 

The gears catch silk, flesh, and bone. Blood sprays across the pristine white walls in a horrific, Jackson Pollock arc. 

The shrieks are swallowed by the deafening crunch of machinery.

Gemma slides past Valerie, her manicured fingers clawing desperately at the frictionless stone. Her bubbly facade shatters completely. She bleeds onto the marble.

GEMMA
Valerie!

Valerie reaches down with her free hand, her cuff sliding up. 

VALERIE
Grab my hand! The structural integrity is holding on the fixture!

Gemma reaches up, her bleeding fingers slipping against Valerie’s palm. Their eyes meet. Even now, facing the abyss, Gemma’s lip curls in a bitter sneer.

GEMMA
I don't need your charity!

Gemma violently flinches away from Valerie's grip. She plummets backward, sliding down the marble chute. She screams, disappearing into the churning gears below.

Valerie stares at her empty hand. She scrubs at her cuff again, harder, smearing real blood into the silk this time. 

Toby hangs from a retracted handrail bracket across the chasm, his legs swinging over the slide. He stares down at the grinding maw, his relentless optimism fracturing into sheer terror.

TOBY
It's f-f-feeding! It's not a foundation, it's a jaw!

Valerie looks from the massacre below to Richard. He stands safely on the upper landing, adjusting his designer tie with a satisfied smirk, entirely unbothered by the slaughter.

The house rumbles, hungry for more. The wall sconce groans under Valerie's weight, the screws pulling loose from the drywall. 

She looks down at the gears. Then up at Richard. 

Valerie swings her body weight, planting a stiletto into a tiny architectural groove in the wall. She hauls herself up over the ledge, abandoning the slide and leaving the remaining guests to the dark.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - BALLROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Chaos erupts in the ballroom as the remaining guests stampede toward the sealed steel doors. Overhead, heavy crystal chandeliers detach from their reinforced mounts. They plummet downward, skewering a cluster of fleeing caterers. Blood rapidly splatters and stains the pristine white walls as the house gorges itself on the trapped innocents.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Escalates the slaughter and demonstrates the house's multi-pronged, inescapable attack. Micro-Conflict (><): The caterers try to flee vs. the house attacking from above. Value Shift (+/-): From desperate flight (-) to brutal death (- -).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - BALLROOM - CONTINUOUS

A tidal wave of Armani tuxedos and designer silk crashes against the ballroom's towering double doors. 

Fists pound. Nails crack against solid steel. The doors have fused seamlessly into the doorframe. No handles. No hinges. Just a sheer, impenetrable wall of metal.

RICHARD STERLING bulldozes through a weeping socialite, shoving her to the floor. His sophisticated veneer is entirely gone. He grabs the steel frame, veins bulging in his neck.

TOBY HIGGINS is pressed against the adjacent wall. His eyes dart frantically along the architrave. He compulsively traces the seamless metal joint, desperately trying to find a perfect right angle that no longer exists. 

His voice trembles, a severe stutter breaking his cheerful facade.

TOBY
I-it's not locked, Mr. Sterling. The structural track-- it's g-gone. The house completely erased the exit!

Richard violently grabs Toby by the collar, invading his personal space. He yanks the younger man inches from his face.

RICHARD
(through clenched teeth)
Nature always provides an exit, Toby. Find the weak point before I use your skull to make one.

Above them, the ceiling lets out a deep, reverberating groan. It doesn't sound like settling wood or shifting pipes. It sounds like a massive stomach rumbling.

A cluster of CATERERS in crisp white uniforms sprint across the herringbone floor. They abandon silver trays of caviar, desperately making a break for the floor-to-ceiling windows.

The youngest, a TEENAGE BUSBOY, slips on a puddle of spilled champagne. He scrambles, trying to find purchase on the slick wood.

Overhead, the massive, three-tiered crystal chandeliers detach from their reinforced mounts. 

There is no snap of cables. No mechanical failure. The house simply lets them go.

Gravity takes over. Three tons of razor-sharp crystal and brass plummet downward.

The caterers don't even have time to scream.

The chandeliers spear directly into the fleeing group. Crystal stalactites skewer through torsos and limbs, pinning the caterers to the floorboards like insects on a display board.

The Teenage Busboy is crushed beneath the central prism of the largest fixture.

Blood erupts. A violent, high-pressure crimson geyser sprays across the pristine, gallery-white walls. 

The trapped guests shriek, recoiling from the carnage. 

The floorboards beneath the shattered chandeliers begin to ripple. The wood parts like a thirsty maw, actively absorbing the pooling blood. Drinking it in.

VALERIE VANCE stands frozen behind a marble pillar, perfectly safe from the drop zone. She watches her exclusive listing gorge itself on the innocent staff. 

Her icy professional mask shatters. Her hands shake violently. She stares at the blood soaking into the floorboards, then down at her own wrists. 

She begins to obsessively, frantically scrub at the invisible stains on her expensive silk cuffs, her breathing ragged and shallow.

Then, she sees it.

In the center of the gore, the shattered, blood-soaked crystal shards begin to pulse. They calcify, hardening and shifting under the ambient light. 

Fresh, pristine diamonds.

Valerie stops scrubbing. She stares at the glittering gems resting in the viscera. The horrific dilemma paralyzes her: the unbreakable financial future she desperately needs, paid for in absolute slaughter.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - RECESSED ALCOVE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie presses her back hard into a recessed alcove, dodging the stampeding crowd. Her trembling hands instinctively dive into her blazer pocket, clutching the heavy velvet bag of diamonds. She stares through the chaos, watching the horrific slaughter unfold. She looks at the blood spilling across the hardwood, then down at the bag of diamonds in her hand, her breathing turning into ragged, hyperventilating sobs as the realization of her guilt crushes her.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Forces Valerie to directly witness the physical, bloody cost of her corporate greed. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie seeks physical safety vs. the inescapable visual evidence of her sins. Value Shift (+/-): From terrified observer (-) to soul-crushed realization (- -).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - RECESSED ALCOVE - CONTINUOUS

Deafening SCREAMS bounce off the Venetian plaster. A blur of torn tuxedoes, shattered champagne flutes, and raw panic. The exclusive gala has become an abattoir.

VALERIE slams backward into the shadows of a recessed architectural alcove. She presses her spine against the cold, unyielding concrete, desperately trying to merge with the architecture.

A terrified TECH EXECUTIVE sprints past her hiding spot. The mahogany floorboards beneath him suddenly RIPPLE like a disturbed pond. 

A massive fissure opens. He drops into the dark with a wet, bone-snapping CRUNCH. The floor seals shut seamlessly.

Valerie squeezes her eyes shut. She doesn't scream. Her jaw locks, fighting to maintain the perfect, icy veneer of the ultimate closer. 

But her hands betray her. They tremble violently, shaking against her sides. 

Her right hand dives deep into the pocket of her tailored blazer. Her fingers find it. They wrap tightly around a heavy, velvet bag. The harvest. 

She opens her eyes. The slaughter continues in the great room beyond. The house GROANS, physically shifting its load-bearing walls to corral the fleeing elite. 

A thick, dark pool of crimson slithers across the pristine hardwood. It creeps toward the alcove. Toward the toe of Valerie's five-thousand-dollar Louboutins.

Valerie stares at the creeping blood. She pulls the velvet bag from her pocket. The drawstrings are loose. 

Inside, raw, blood-free diamonds catch the flickering strobe of the failing overhead lights. They are perfect. Flawless.

VALERIE
(whispering)
Just a market correction. High yield... high risk.

A horrible, wet TEARING sound echoes from the foyer. A woman's scream is abruptly silenced by the sickening sound of grinding mortar.

Valerie's breath hitches. The sanitized real estate buzzwords turn to ash in her mouth. 

She looks at the blood soaking into the floorboards. She looks at the flawless diamonds in her palm.

The weight of the stones feels completely different now. Heavy. Suffocating.

She looks down at her immaculate white silk cuffs. Her breathing accelerates, shifting from controlled exhales to ragged, hyperventilating gasps.

She spots a stain. Invisible to anyone else, but glaring to her. 

Valerie begins to scrub at her left cuff. Her manicured nails dig into the delicate silk. There is no blood on the fabric, but she scrubs harder, her movements frantic and obsessive.

VALERIE
(voice breaking)
Maximum return on investment. It's... it's a secure asset class...

The silk tears under her nails. She doesn't stop. She scrubs at her bare wrist, her skin turning red and raw. 

Tears break through her flawless makeup. The icy mask shatters entirely. 

She slides down the rough concrete wall, her knees hitting the floor just inches from the expanding pool of blood. 

Valerie clutches the bag of diamonds to her chest and sobs, drowning in the inescapable evidence of her own monstrous success.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Across the foyer, the floorboards beneath Toby violently split open. A dark, fleshy chasm lined with jagged, wooden teeth expands outward. Toby screams, falling forward. His fingers desperately claw at the slick marble tiles, his legs dangling into the abyss as a vacuum-like suction attempts to drag him down into the house's digestive tract.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Puts the primary moral innocent in direct, fatal jeopardy, forcing a final choice. Micro-Conflict (><): Toby fights to hold onto the floor vs. the house's physical suction dragging him down. Value Shift (+/-): From terrified bystander (-) to active prey (- -).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS

The pristine herringbone floorboards beneath TOBY buckle with a sickening, wet CRUNCH. 

Wood splinters upward. A massive fissure tears across the center of the foyer. 

There is no concrete foundation beneath the subfloor. It is a pulsing, dark-purple fleshy chasm. Jagged, broken floorboards protrude from the muscular walls like rows of splintered teeth.

Toby plummets.

TOBY
(stuttering terror)
V-V-Val--!

His chest slams against the edge of the floor. His hands frantically slap onto the slick Italian marble border, fingers splayed wide. 

His legs dangle into the warm, wet darkness.

A deep, guttural INHALE echoes from the bowels of the house. A violent vacuum suction grabs Toby’s ankles. It yanks him downward.

Toby’s fingernails screech across the polished marble. He slides an inch toward the abyss.

Across the foyer, VALERIE stands paralyzed. Her icy professional mask evaporates. Her hands tremble violently as she obsessively scrubs at an invisible stain on her silk cuff.

TOBY
(straining)
The s-s-steel joists! Th-there should be an I-beam under the l-l-load wall!

Toby kicks his left leg wildly in the dark, searching for structural steel. His dress shoe strikes only soft, yielding tissue. 

The tissue spasms.

The house groans-- a sound like grinding concrete. The wooden teeth flex inward, snapping at the empty air inches below his calves.

Toby’s architectural drafting tube slips from his shoulder strap. It tumbles into the chasm.

CRACK. The wooden jaws instantly snap the thick PVC tube in half. It vanishes into the dark throat.

Toby’s eyes go wide. His breathing turns hyper-kinetic. He stares at the slick marble beneath his palms. No friction. No handholds.

TOBY
Valerie! P-p-please!

Valerie takes a half-step forward. She looks at Toby's terrified face. Then she looks at the glittering, pristine diamond resting on the nearby console table-- the house's previous payment. 

She freezes, her thumb digging brutally into her cuff.

The vacuum suction doubles in force. A hurricane of foul, coppery air rushes past Toby’s face, pulling his tie down into the dark.

His right hand slips off the marble.

He hangs by his left hand, muscles screaming. The tendons in his wrist bulge against his skin. He desperately tries to jam his thumb into a microscopic grout line.

TOBY
I c-c-can't hold it!

The wooden teeth below him begin to rotate, grinding together like a garbage disposal. Waiting for the final slip.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER / CHASM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Staring at the terrified young man she manipulated, Valerie's icy demeanor completely shatters. She rips the velvet bag of priceless diamonds from her pocket and violently hurls it into the darkness of the chasm. She lunges out of the alcove, sliding across the blood-slicked marble, and grabs both of Toby's wrists just as his grip fails, anchoring him against the house's relentless pull.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The climactic character shift; Valerie chooses a human life over her wealth, rejecting her lie. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie struggles to pull Toby up vs. the house's overwhelming gravity. Value Shift (+/-): From paralyzed guilt (-) to active, selfless heroism (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER / CHASM - CONTINUOUS

The grand foyer is gone. In its place, a massive, jagged sinkhole of splintered Brazilian walnut and grinding foundation. A throat. 

A violent, rhythmic downdraft howls from the pitch-black abyss below. The house is inhaling.

TOBY HIGGINS dangles over the edge. His legs kick at empty space. His knuckles are bone-white, fingers desperately clawing at a fractured slab of Calacatta marble. 

In a shallow, recessed alcove, VALERIE VANCE stands frozen. Safe. The pristine lines of her designer blazer starkly contrast the carnage. 

Her breath comes in jagged, shallow gasps. Her right thumb aggressively, obsessively scrubs at her left cuff. There is no stain there, but she rubs it raw anyway. 

In her other hand, she clutches a heavy black velvet bag. The muffled, unmistakable clink of raw diamonds echoes from within. Her financial shield. Her life raft.

TOBY
(stuttering)
Ms. Vance! The-- the subfloor! It’s completely compromised! The lateral load-bearing metrics, they-- they don't make any sense!

Toby’s left hand slips. He drops an inch. He frantically adjusts his grip, forcing his index fingers to align perfectly parallel to the grout lines. A desperate attempt to organize the chaos.

TOBY
Why is the HVAC system breathing?! 

Valerie stares at the velvet bag. Her knuckles ache. She looks back at Toby. His terrified, earnest eyes pierce right through her tailored armor. 

The house groans—a deep, gastric rumble that vibrates through the soles of Valerie's heels. 

VALERIE
(voice trembling)
Focus on the fixtures, Toby. Look at the... the recessed lighting. The aesthetic value. 

TOBY
I can't-- I can't hold the angle! The friction coefficient is-- Ms. Vance, please! 

Toby’s right hand slides across the marble. A fresh smear of crimson blood from a previous client coats the stone. 

He looks up at her. Not with anger, but with absolute, naive trust. 

TOBY
I don't want to be evicted from the property, Valerie!

The word hits her like a physical blow. Evicted. 

Valerie’s icy veneer shatters. Her eyes snap wide. The obsessive scrubbing at her cuff stops. 

She looks at the velvet bag of diamonds. Millions of dollars. Never being poor again. Never begging a landlord for mercy. 

She tightens her grip on the bag. 

Then, with a guttural scream, she hurls the velvet bag straight into the dark chasm. 

The house roars as the diamonds vanish into the void. 

Valerie lunges out of the alcove. 

She hits the floorboards running, diving forward as the hardwood splinters into the abyss. She hits the edge of the chasm on her stomach. 

The Calacatta marble is slick with blood. Valerie slides dangerously fast toward the drop. 

Toby’s fingers finally give out. He falls. 

Valerie shoots her arms over the precipice. 

She catches both of his wrists in mid-air. 

The sudden, violent drop yanks Valerie forward. Her ribs slam brutally against the jagged marble edge. She screams, her teeth grinding together. 

Below them, the chasm’s suction triples in force. The house realizes its meal is being stolen. A gale-force wind tears at Toby’s clothes, trying to rip him from her grasp.

TOBY
(sobbing)
You dropped the commission! 

VALERIE
We are terminating the escrow! 

Valerie’s tailored blazer tears at the shoulders. Her meticulously manicured nails dig into Toby’s cheap suit sleeves. 

The house bucks. The marble slab beneath Valerie tips forward by a few degrees. 

Toby looks down into the infinite, grinding blackness. He looks back up at Valerie’s straining, blood-rushed face. 

TOBY
Let me go. The structural integrity won't hold both our weights. It’s simple math. 

VALERIE
Shut up! I am the primary listing agent, and I do not authorize this demolition! 

Valerie shifts her center of gravity. She wedges her expensive stiletto heel into a crack in the floorboards, anchoring herself against the house's immense gravitational pull. 

She pulls. Veins bulge in her neck. 

VALERIE
You are not closing today, Toby! 

With a massive, agonizing heave, Valerie drags Toby upward. His chest clears the jagged ledge. 

She grabs him by the lapels and throws her weight backward, hauling them both away from the precipice. 

They collapse onto the solid, remaining section of the foyer floor. Panting. Shaking. Covered in dust and blood. 

The chasm behind them snaps shut with the deafening boom of a bank vault. 

Valerie lies on her back, staring up at the pristine coffered ceiling. Her hands are empty. She has absolutely nothing left. 

She lets out a sharp, breathless laugh.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER / CHASM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's muscles scream as she grips Toby's wrists, her designer shoes slipping on the blood-slicked marble. The chasm below them roars, the floorboards splintering and shifting like a jaw trying to snap shut. Using the last of her leverage, Valerie heaves backward, ripping Toby out of the gravitational pull. They scramble on all fours across the trembling floor, dodging snapping floorboards, until Valerie spots a heavy oak door. She shoves Toby toward it, the house groaning in protest as they cross the threshold.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes immediate physical survival from the previous climax. (+/-) Shifts from imminent death to temporary escape. (><) Valerie's physical desperation vs. the house's magnetic, consuming pull.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER / CHASM - CONTINUOUS

A yawning, fleshy abyss where the foyer floor used to be. Wind HOWLS from the dark center, smelling of copper and ozone. 

VALERIE lies flat on her stomach, arms trembling, fingers locked like vices around the wrists of TOBY. 

Toby dangles over the void. Below him, the darkness pulses.

Valerie’s designer heels scrape frantically against the marble. The stone is slick with a fresh, viscous coat of blood. She slips an inch forward toward the drop.

TOBY
(stuttering)
M-Ms. Vance! The structural-- the foundational load--

VALERIE
Shut up about the foundation, Toby!

The floorboards at the edge of the chasm splinter. They curl upward, snapping violently like jagged wooden teeth. 

Toby looks down. The abyss roars, a deafening vacuum trying to suck him into its throat.

TOBY
It’s pulling me! Just-- let go! You’re going to breach your own center of gravity!

Valerie’s icy veneer cracks. Her eyes are wide, terrified, but she grips tighter. 

VALERIE
I do not lose my inventory!

Valerie digs the stiletto of her shoe into a bloody grout line. She finds a millimeter of traction. 

She screams, muscles tearing, and HEAVES backward. 

Toby flies up over the jagged lip of the floorboards. They collapse onto the blood-soaked marble in a tangle of limbs.

SNAP!

The wooden teeth crash together exactly where Toby’s torso was a second ago. 

The house lets out a low, agonizing GROAN. The walls vibrate. The chandelier above swings violently, raining crystal shards down on them.

Valerie pushes herself up on bloody hands. Her pristine blazer is ruined. She ignores it, scanning the shifting geometry of the foyer. 

The chasm is expanding. The marble tiles crack, falling away into the dark.

VALERIE
Move.

Toby is frozen, staring at the impossible physics of the shifting floor. His hands twitch, trying to trace invisible right angles in the empty air.

TOBY
(stuttering)
The blueprints... the load-bearing walls shouldn't-- they can't articulate like a--

Valerie grabs him by the collar, hauling him onto all fours.

VALERIE
We are relocating to the adjacent study! Crawl!

They scramble like animals. Behind them, the chasm rips wider. Tiles explode into dust. 

Valerie’s knees slam against the hard stone. She dodges a buckling floorboard that shoots up from the ground like a spear. 

Across the violently vibrating room: a massive, heavy OAK DOOR.

VALERIE
The oak door! Get to the oak door!

The floor tilts. The house is trying to slide them backward into the maw. Gravity shifts, pulling them toward the chasm.

Toby slides backward, crying out. Valerie shoves her hand against his back, propelling him forward. 

They reach the threshold. Toby grabs the brass handle. It won't turn.

TOBY
It’s jammed! The frame is warped!

VALERIE
It’s solid core mahogany! Push!

Toby rams his shoulder into the wood. The house ROARS--a sound like a dying whale. The floor beneath Valerie drops a full inch. 

She slips backward, her bloody hands failing to find a grip on the smooth floorboards.

VALERIE
Toby!

Toby turns. He grabs her outstretched hand, planting his feet against the doorframe, and yanks her forward just as the tile beneath her gives way completely.

The oak door bursts open. 

They tumble through the threshold into the dark room. 

Toby kicks the heavy door shut.

CLICK.

Silence. Heavy, suffocating silence.

Toby hyperventilates, curled in a ball on the floor. He desperately aligns the edge of his torn tie with the parallel seam of the floorboards, over and over.

Valerie sits slumped against the door. Her chest heaves. Her flawless facade is completely shattered. 

She looks down at her hands. Dripping with blood. 

Her breathing turns ragged. She begins to scrub furiously at the cuffs of her blouse. 

Rubbing. Scratching. Trying to get the invisible stain out. 

VALERIE
(whispering)
It’s just... post-construction settling. A minor... cosmetic defect.

She keeps scrubbing until her own skin is raw.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie yanks Toby inside and throws her entire body weight against the heavy oak door. She slides the deadbolt home just as a massive, unseen force slams against the outside wood. The muffled, horrific symphony of grinding bones and shattering glass echoes through the walls. Valerie's icy veneer crumbles completely. She slides down the door to the floor, her breathing erratic. Her hands begin to frantically, obsessively scrub at her blazer cuffs, her fingernails digging deep into the expensive fabric to scratch away invisible blood.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Initiates the Dark Night of the Soul. (+/-) Shifts from adrenaline-fueled flight to paralyzing, inescapable trauma. (><) Valerie's desperate need to sanitize her guilt vs. the auditory reality of the massacre outside.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - CONTINUOUS

Valerie yanks Toby into the cramped, pitch-black space. She hurls her entire body weight against the heavy oak door. 

Her manicured fingers fumble in the dark, slamming the steel deadbolt home.

BOOM.

A massive, unseen force impacts the other side of the wood. The reinforced doorframe groans. Plaster dust rains from the ceiling.

Outside, a muffled, horrific symphony begins. 

The wet, unmistakable crunch of grinding bones. The chaotic avalanche of shattering glass. A chorus of wealthy, panicked screams abruptly cut short by a sickening, cavernous SLURP.

Valerie's icy, flawless veneer completely disintegrates.

Her knees buckle. She slides down the vibrating wood of the door, hitting the concrete floor. Her breathing is shallow, erratic.

Her hands drop to her lap. Her fingers curl into claws. She begins to frantically, obsessively scrub at the cuffs of her Tom Ford blazer. 

Scraping the expensive fabric. Digging for invisible blood.

Toby presses his back against the far wall. His eyes are wide, locked on the door. He is hyperventilating.

TOBY
(stuttering)
Th-the gala. Ms. Vance, the-- the guests out there. Mr. Sterling...

Valerie doesn't look up. She just scrubs her cuffs.

VALERIE
It’s structural settling. The foundation is simply adjusting to the... the new weight distribution. 

TOBY
I heard him screaming. I heard his... his chest cave in.

Toby’s hands tremble wildly. He turns to a metal utility shelf beside him. It’s cluttered with spare paint cans and loose ceramic tiles. 

Desperate for control, Toby begins shifting the paint cans. Aligning their labels. Pushing them into perfect, ninety-degree right angles against the wall.

TOBY
I-I should make coffee. Do we have a percolator in here? I could-- I could get us some water.

VALERIE
Look at the acoustic insulation in these walls, Toby. Top-tier. You can barely hear the ambient neighborhood noise. It’s a massive selling point for high-net-worth buyers.

Outside, a wet, agonizing TEAR echoes through the floorboards. A heavy, wet mass hits the drywall.

Toby freezes. He stares down at a perfectly aligned row of brass hinges on the shelf.

TOBY
It’s not a house. It’s an abattoir.

VALERIE
(scrubbing faster)
It is a mid-century masterpiece with a highly motivated seller.

TOBY
You knew. The tech billionaires. Gemma’s prime clients. They didn't relocate to Dubai.

Valerie’s fingernails dig so hard into her cuff that a seam pops.

VALERIE
We are facilitating a highly exclusive transaction. The market requires aggressive positioning.

TOBY
It’s eating them!

Toby sweeps his arm, violently knocking his perfectly aligned paint cans onto the floor. They clatter loudly against the concrete.

Silence falls over the closet. 

Both of them stare at the door. Waiting.

A low, guttural rumble vibrates through the foundation beneath their feet. A deep, resonant purr. The house is digesting.

Valerie’s hands shake violently. She stares down at her pristine, bloodless cuffs.

VALERIE
The return on investment... the diamonds... they're completely conflict-free.

TOBY
(tears welling)
My dad always said... a house is supposed to keep the monsters out.

Valerie closes her eyes. A single tear cuts through her meticulous makeup. 

VALERIE
This property comes with the amenities built-in.

She aggressively rips the cuff off her blazer, entirely consumed by the invisible stain.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Trapped in the suffocating darkness, illuminated only by the faint glow of Toby's cracked phone screen, Valerie chokes back a sob. She stares at her raw, scratched wrists and confesses the grim mechanics of her dark pact. She details the velvet bags of diamonds, her voice hollow as she explains the trades. She curls her knees to her chest, physically shrinking in the space, and admits that her terrifying crusade to avoid poverty turned her into the exact same ruthless monster that evicted her family years ago.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Delivers the core thematic confession and exposes the protagonist's fatal flaw. (+/-) Shifts from defensive concealment to devastating emotional nakedness. (><) Valerie's desire to hide her sins vs. the crushing weight of her own guilt.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - MOMENTS LATER

Pitch black. A wet, rhythmic THUDDING reverberates through the walls. Not pipes. A heartbeat. 

A phone screen clicks on. Spidery cracks across the glass cast a fractured, pale-blue glow over the cramped space.

TOBY HIGGINS (20s) holds the phone. His chest heaves. He kneels beside a massive commercial water heater, surrounded by boxes of hardware. 

VALERIE VANCE (40s) sits slumped against the reinforced steel door. Her tailored silk blazer is torn. Her wrists are raw, stripped of skin from hauling the heavy deadbolts shut. 

Toby sets the phone down. His trembling hands find a scattered box of heavy industrial hex-nuts on the floor. 

TOBY
Th-the structural integrity of this room is sound. It’s reinforced steel. But the foundation groans... th-there’s a sheer-force anomaly happening outside. I don't understand the load-bearing distribution.

He picks up a hex-nut. Places it on the floor. Picks up another. Lines it up perfectly parallel to the first. A precise right angle.

TOBY
(stuttering worse)
Mr. Sterling... the floorboards just... they unzipped. Like a jacket. Ms. Vance, wh-what is the architectural explanation for what we just saw?

Valerie stares at her bleeding wrists. The icy, flawless veneer is gone. Her breath shudders. 

She grabs her left sleeve. Her right thumb rubs frantically at the edge of her expensive silk cuff. Scrubbing an invisible stain. Over and over. 

VALERIE
It was a market correction. We were over-leveraged, Toby. We just need to restructure the asset.

TOBY
Restructure? People are dead. The house digested them. Wh-why did you lock the front doors from the outside? 

Toby aligns a third hex-nut. Perfectly equidistant. 

TOBY
Wh-why did you keep inviting them here? 

Valerie scrubs the cuff harder. The friction turns her own skin red. 

VALERIE
Because the ROI was unprecedented. 

Valerie chokes on the words. A wet, pathetic sob escapes her throat. She bites her lip to kill the sound, tasting blood.

VALERIE
(whispering)
Arthur Pendelton was a loud, depreciating asset. He didn't matter. None of them mattered. 

TOBY
I-I require clarification. What do you mean they didn't matter?

VALERIE
After Arthur... after the property executed its first foreclosure... I came back the next morning to assess the liability. 

Valerie stops scrubbing. Her hands fall limp into her lap. 

VALERIE
There was no blood. No tax footprint. Just a small, black velvet bag sitting on the kitchen island. 

Toby freezes. His hand hovers over a fourth hex-nut. 

TOBY
A bag?

VALERIE
Flawless. Uncut. Two carats each. The appraisal was staggering. Every time the house fed... it paid a broker's fee. 

The walls constrict slightly. A low, gastric gurgle echoes through the drywall. 

Toby stares at her. The blue light from his cracked phone illuminates the absolute horror on his face. 

TOBY
You traded them. You acted as a-a procurement agent for a slaughterhouse. 

VALERIE
I had a liquidity crisis! 

Valerie’s voice cracks. She pulls her knees tightly to her chest, physically shrinking into the corner of the closet. The sharp corporate titan dissolves into a terrified, small child.

VALERIE
You don't know the math, Toby. You don't know what the pavement feels like when it's thirty degrees and your mattress is on the curb. 

She buries her face in her bruised arms. 

VALERIE
My father cried. My mother begged. She got on her actual knees and begged the landlord for a one-week grace period. He didn't even look at her. He just checked his watch and signaled the sheriff. 

Toby abandons the hex-nuts. He looks at the reinforced door, then back to the pathetic, curled-up woman in the corner. 

TOBY
So you made sure you would never be on the pavement again. 

VALERIE
If you control the property, you control the world. No one can ever hurt you. No one can ever humiliate you. 

Valerie lifts her head. Tears cut through the dust on her cheeks. She looks at her raw, bloody wrists, then at the heavy steel door keeping the monster at bay.

VALERIE
I just wanted to be safe. 

TOBY
(softly)
But you aren't, Ms. Vance. You just became the landlord. 

The words land like a physical blow. Valerie’s breath hitches. The justification drains from her eyes, leaving nothing but the crushing, suffocating weight of her own sins. 

She looks at the perfectly aligned hex-nuts on the floor. She looks at Toby's earnest, terrified face. 

A massive, deafening IMPACT strikes the other side of the steel door. The metal buckles inward. Dust rains from the ceiling.

Valerie doesn't flinch. Her expression hardens. The fear vanishes, replaced by a cold, devastating clarity. 

VALERIE
You're right. 

She slowly uncurls her legs. She plants her feet on the concrete floor. 

VALERIE
Time to terminate the lease.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Toby listens in stunned silence. As Valerie's scrubbing intensifies, Toby's trembling hands reach out. He firmly grips her wrists, forcing her frantic movements to a dead halt. He meets her tear-filled eyes. Overcoming his severe stutter, he speaks with unprecedented clarity. He tells her the system is irreparably broken, but she has the power to smash it to pieces. The quiet, profound forgiveness in his voice acts as a physical shockwave. Valerie's posture straightens, her paralyzing despair evaporating into a cold, hardened resolve.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Acts as the emotional catalyst and epiphany of the sequence. (+/-) Shifts from paralyzing self-hatred to empowered resolve. (><) Valerie's self-destructive spiral vs. Toby's unexpected moral leadership and forgiveness.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - CONTINUOUS

The walls PULSATE. A wet, rhythmic contraction echoes from the drywall. The house is digesting.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) sits on an overturned industrial bucket. Her pristine blazer is covered in drywall dust. She is hyperventilating.

Her thumbs dig viciously into the white silk of her French cuffs. SCRUBBING. Over and over. Erasing a stain only she can see. The friction is raw, frantic.

Across the cramped space, TOBY HIGGINS (20s) stands frozen. His eyes are wide, processing the horrific ledger Valerie just confessed to.

Toby’s hands shake violently. He turns to a metal utility shelf. He grabs a scattered handful of heavy brass pipe fittings.

With rigid, jerky movements, Toby lines the fittings up on the shelf. Perfect right angles. Exactly two inches apart.

TOBY
(stuttering)
Th-th-the zoning... the zoning board... th-they don’t account for... for structural anomalies of this...

VALERIE
We just need to re-evaluate the asset. It’s a seller’s market, Toby. We leverage the exclusivity.

Valerie scrubs harder. The silk of her cuff begins to fray. A thread snaps.

VALERIE
(manic)
The buyer pool is infinite. We just... we amortize the risk. We partition the dining room. It’s just an aggressive ROI. It’s just business.

The floorboards shift. A deep, gastric MOAN reverberates through the copper pipes.

Toby drops a brass fitting. It clatters to the concrete. He doesn't pick it up. His hands hover over his perfectly aligned row.

TOBY
M-my dad... he signed the adjustable rate. Th-thought if we just... paid the escrow... played by the rules...

Toby looks at the exposed blueprints pinned to the closet door. His eyes trace the complex, impossible geometry of the mansion’s veins.

TOBY
The s-system... it’s not designed to house us, Valerie. It’s designed to eat us.

Valerie’s breathing hitches. Her thumbnails dig so hard into her wrists they draw blood.

VALERIE
I am the listing agent. I control the escrow. I control--

Toby turns. He steps away from his perfectly organized shelf.

He kneels in front of her. His trembling hands reach out.

He grips Valerie’s wrists. Hard.

The frantic scrubbing stops.

Valerie freezes. She looks up. Her icy, professional mask is entirely gone, replaced by the terrified, evicted child she’s been running from her whole life. Tears spill over her eyelashes.

Toby looks down at her bleeding wrists. He doesn't flinch. He meets her eyes.

When he speaks, the stutter is completely gone. His voice is a quiet, steady anchor.

TOBY
The foundation is rotten. You can’t renovate it. You can’t flip it.

Valerie stares at him. The silence between them is louder than the groaning house.

TOBY
It’s a teardown, Valerie. A total loss.

Toby gently releases her wrists. He doesn't pull away. He stays kneeling.

TOBY
But you hold the demolition permit.

A profound, impossible forgiveness radiates from his posture. He isn't looking at a monster. He is looking at a victim who finally has the keys to the bulldozer.

Valerie looks at her torn, bloodied cuffs. The phantom stains are gone. Only the real blood remains.

A massive centipede scurries out from a crack in the baseboard, fleeing the groaning walls. It stops near Valerie’s designer heel, twitching frantically.

Valerie slowly reaches down. She gently scoops the terrified insect into the palm of her hand, then slips it safely into the breast pocket of her blazer.

She inhales. A long, shuddering breath.

When she exhales, the terror evaporates. Her spine straightens. The evicted child vanishes, replaced by something entirely new. Cold. Hardened. Lethal.

Valerie stands up. She towers in the cramped space.

She looks at the massive, red iron wheel of the main gas line protruding from the wall.

VALERIE
What’s the maximum PSI on a commercial gas main before the structural integrity fails?

Toby looks at the pipes. A small, genuine smile breaks through his exhaustion.

TOBY
In a closed envelope? About four hundred pounds per square inch.

Valerie reaches out and grips the cold iron wheel.

VALERIE
Let’s flood the market.

With a violent heave, Valerie CRANKS the wheel open.

A high-pitched HISS fills the closet as raw natural gas floods the pipes.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Under extreme stress, Toby's hands shake as he compulsively organizes a pile of scattered screws on the closet floor into perfect right angles. Channeling his nervous energy into his savant-like obsession, he opens the municipal blueprint files on his cracked smartphone. His eyes dart rapidly as he traces the impossible, shifting geometry of the mansion against the original structural schematics. He taps a specific junction on the digital blueprint, pointing out a massive, highly pressurized gas main beneath the foundation, calculating that rupturing the primary valve will flood the lower levels with combustible vapor.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Identifies the weapon for the Break Into Three. (+/-) Shifts from helpless victims to tactical strategists. (><) Toby's analytical demolition plan vs. the house's illogical, shifting architecture.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - MOMENTS LATER

A heavy, wet THUD reverberates through the reinforced steel door. Not the sound of settling wood. The sound of digestion.

TOBY HIGGINS (20s) sits in the dark on the concrete floor. A pile of scattered, rusted drywall screws lies between his knees. 

His hands shake violently. He slides one screw across the dust. Then another. He aligns them into a flawless, ninety-degree angle. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) paces the cramped space. She aggressively rubs the cuff of her immaculate silk blouse. There is no stain, but she scrubs it like she is trying to erase a sin.

VALERIE
(tight, controlled)
The structural integrity of this property is temporarily compromised. We just need to identify a localized exit strategy. An immediate vacatur.

TOBY
I-I-I don’t think vacatur is an option, Valerie.

VALERIE
Don't be defeatist, Toby. Every property has a backdoor. A service entrance. We just have to bypass the unpermitted additions.

She scrubs the cuff harder. The expensive fabric begins to fray.

VALERIE
Where are we on the schematics?

Toby slides another screw. Ninety degrees. Perfect. 

He picks up his smartphone. The screen is spider-webbed with cracks, casting a fractured, pale blue glow across his terrified face. 

He opens a massive PDF file. Municipal blueprints.

TOBY
I'm t-t-trying to cross-reference. But the corridors don't match the filings. The-the-the square footage is actively expanding.

VALERIE
Stop talking about the square footage. Focus on the load-bearing walls. Where is the structural weakness?

TOBY
It’s mathematically impossible! The geometry is... it's shifting. It’s a-a-a biological layout now. Like a digestive tract.

A low, guttural GROAN echoes from the ceiling above them. The drywall bulges inward, just an inch, before retracting. Like a lung taking a breath.

Valerie stares at the breathing ceiling. Her icy facade cracks. She looks down at her hands, trembling, and immediately goes back to scrubbing her frayed cuff.

VALERIE
Look at the original foundation. The bedrock. Ignore the cosmetic changes. What is fixed?

Toby pinches and zooms on the cracked glass. His eyes dart rapidly across the complex grid of blue and white lines. 

The panic in his chest seems to pause. The savant takes over. He traces a finger along an invisible line in the air, mapping the digital schematic onto the physical space around them.

TOBY
The municipal tie-ins. They-they can't move. The house has to pull from the city grid.

He taps a specific junction on the screen.

TOBY
Here. Beneath the sub-basement.

Valerie drops to her knees beside him. The pristine knees of her designer slacks soak up the utility closet grime. She doesn't notice.

VALERIE
What am I looking at?

TOBY
A high-pressure gas main. Commercial grade. If the house shifted its own foundation to consume the dining room, it-it-it would have exposed the primary valve.

Valerie stops scrubbing her cuff.

VALERIE
Gas.

TOBY
Massive amounts of it. If we rupture that primary valve, the lower levels will flood with combustible vapor.

VALERIE
How long?

Toby closes his eyes, doing the math in the dark.

TOBY
Based on the cubic volume of the new layout... under four minutes. It-it-it would turn the entire footprint into a bomb.

Another wet THUD against the door. The steel hinges groan. Blood begins to seep under the doorframe, pooling near Toby's perfectly arranged screws.

VALERIE
A total teardown.

TOBY
Valerie, we-we'd be inside.

Valerie looks at the blood. She looks at the bulging door. She slowly brushes the dust off her knees. 

The icy, flawless professional veneer slides back into place, colder than ever.

VALERIE
It’s an aggressive market correction, Toby. And we are going to close the deal.

Toby looks up at her. He reaches down, breaking his perfect right angle of screws, and grips his phone.

CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie studies the screen, her eyes narrowing as she points to the shifting walls around them. She realizes the house is hyper-aware and will never let Toby dismantle its internal organs unbothered. She stands up, her icy professional veneer returning, but this time weaponized for survival. She formulates the counter-measure, stating she must offer the entity an irresistible, high-value distraction directly inside its primary digestive core in the basement. She insists that the survival of the only pure person she knows hinges entirely on her serving as live bait.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Solidifies the suicidal distraction plan and completes the B-story/A-story merge. (+/-) Shifts from fear of the entity to weaponized self-sacrifice. (><) Toby's desperate need to protect Valerie vs. Valerie's absolute determination to be the bait.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - CONTINUOUS

The heavy steel door GROANS, bowing inward. 

The concrete walls of the cramped closet visibly expand and contract. Breathing. 

VALERIE VANCE (40s) and TOBY HIGGINS (20s) crouch in the dim, claustrophobic space. The only light comes from the glowing architectural tablet in Toby’s hands. 

Toby’s fingers tremble wildly. He ignores the screen, instead compulsively lining up three stray hex nuts on a metal shelf. Perfect right angles.

TOBY
(stuttering)
The, um, the schematic is... it is entirely compromised, Ms. Vance. If I attempt to sever the main arterial conduit... the HVAC, I mean...

Valerie stares at the tablet. The blue lines of the digital floor plan are literally slithering across the glass. Re-routing. 

Valerie’s thumb aggressively rubs a phantom smudge on the cuff of her silk blouse. Scrubbing until the skin is raw red.

VALERIE
It’s a seller’s market. The property is actively renegotiating the layout.

TOBY
It knows my trajectory. If I step into the interstitial crawlspace to dismantle the pressure valves, the structural load will--

VALERIE
It will evict you. Permanently.

Valerie watches a new cluster of digital walls form a jagged cage around the HVAC icon on the screen. The house is protecting its organs. 

She looks at Toby. He is nudging a fourth hex nut into the grid. Sweating. Pure, uncorrupted panic. 

Valerie’s thumb stops scrubbing. 

She inhales. The frantic, cornered animal vanishes. Her posture straightens. She smooths the lapels of her bespoke blazer. The icy, untouchable broker returns. Weaponized.

VALERIE
(cont'd)
We are approaching this negotiation from a position of weakness. We need to present a highly motivated buyer.

TOBY
I don't... I don't fully comprehend the metaphor, ma'am. 

VALERIE
It’s not a metaphor, Toby. The entity is hyper-aware. It’s guarding the utilities because it has no other immediate prospects. We have to stage a distraction. A premium, exclusive showing.

Valerie taps the absolute bottom of the tablet screen. The sub-level.

VALERIE
(cont'd)
The basement. The primary digestive core. Exposed brick, incredible square footage for a slaughter. 

TOBY
The basement is where it... where it consumes the bulk of its...

VALERIE
Its clientele. Yes. 

TOBY
No. No, absolutely not. I can-- I can go. I will go down to the basement. I could, um, I could organize the storage bins. Take a complete inventory of the--

VALERIE
Toby. Look at me.

Toby keeps his eyes glued to the hex nuts, nudging them a millimeter to the left. Avoiding her gaze.

VALERIE
(cont'd)
Look at the crown molding in here. Polyurethane. Cheap. The house has a refined palate. It craves high-value assets. 

TOBY
(stuttering)
My... my life insurance policy is fully updated. I am a viable alternative.

VALERIE
You are a pristine, unblemished title. You are the only clean asset in this entire zip code. If you walk down there, it won’t be a distraction. It will be a light appetizer before it forecloses on both of us.

Valerie steps toward the heavy steel door. 

VALERIE
(cont'd)
I am a distressed property, Toby. I am dripping with the exact kind of toxic equity this place gorges on. I am the whale. 

TOBY
Ms. Vance, please. It is structurally unsound for you to--

VALERIE
(sharp)
When I step into that basement, the house will divert every ounce of its attention to closing the deal with me. The walls will shift. The arteries will open. You will have exactly three minutes to dismantle the central nervous system unbothered.

Toby finally looks up from his perfect geometric arrangement. His eyes are wet. 

TOBY
And what about your... your exit strategy?

Valerie places a perfectly manicured hand on the heavy iron latch of the door. She offers a flawless, terrifying smile.

VALERIE
I’m going to do what I do best. I’m going to aggressively oversell it.

She yanks the steel door open. 

The hallway beyond is pitch black. A low, guttural rumble echoes from the floorboards, vibrating up through Valerie's designer heels. 

Valerie steps out into the dark.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Working in synchronized, desperate silence, Valerie and Toby approach a heavy iron ventilation grate near the floorboards. Using her designer belt buckle as a makeshift lever, Valerie helps Toby pry the rusted screws loose. They heave the heavy iron grate aside. Toby contorts his shoulders, squeezing into the narrow, claustrophobic, shifting ventilation shaft. Valerie kneels on the floor, watching his shoes disappear into the darkness until only the faint glow of his phone remains.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Executes the physical separation of the team to initiate the final attack. (+/-) Shifts from unified strength to isolated vulnerability. (><) The physical constriction of the narrow vent vs. Toby's desperate need to reach the gas main.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - MOMENTS LATER

The floorboards vibrate with a low, wet, guttural hum. The architecture is digesting. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) and TOBY HIGGINS (20s) kneel on the raw concrete. Synchronized. Desperate. Silent.

Before them sits a heavy iron ventilation grate, bolted deep into the baseboards. 

Valerie grips a solid brass, interlocking-designer belt buckle. She jams the metal prong into the groove of a rusted, industrial screw. 

She twists with her entire body weight. The prong slips, tearing a layer of skin from her thumb. She doesn't flinch. She jams it back in.

Beside her, Toby’s hands shake violently. He isn't helping with the screw. Instead, he meticulously arranges three loose washers and a bent nail into a perfect, right-angled square on the concrete. 

TOBY
(stuttering)
T-the blueprints... the original schematics didn't map this artery. It narrows by six inches past the firewall.

Valerie grunts, throwing her shoulder into the makeshift lever. 

VALERIE
It’s an open-concept duct, Toby. Lots of natural flow. You’ll slide right through.

TOBY
It’s not settling, Valerie. The foundation is... it’s shifting. The house is actively constricting the sub-floor.

Valerie stops. The screw is halfway out. 

She looks at Toby. He won't meet her eyes. He just keeps nudging the bent nail to keep his little square perfectly aligned. 

Valerie drops the bloody belt buckle. She aggressively rubs her left cuff, scrubbing at an invisible stain on the pristine white silk. Over and over.

VALERIE
Look at me.

Toby keeps his eyes on the floor. 

VALERIE
Toby.

He finally looks up. The raw terror in his eyes is suffocating. 

VALERIE
The ROI on this is our lives. You navigate the crawlspace, you locate the main gas valve, and you open it. We terminate this listing tonight.

Toby swallows hard. 

TOBY
I just... I just wanted to close on a starter home. A nice, quiet duplex.

A beat. Valerie’s icy veneer cracks. She looks at the dark, rusted grate. She is about to send the only decent person she knows into the throat of a monster. 

VALERIE
(softly)
If the structural integrity compromises... if the layout shifts... you abort the showing. You come right back. Understood?

Toby nods. He reaches out and picks up the bloody belt buckle. 

He wedges it into the final rusted screw. They both grab the heavy leather strap. Together, they pull. 

CRACK. The rust gives way. 

Valerie spots a small, trembling silverfish insect near the edge of the grate. Without thinking, she gently scoops it up with her bleeding thumb and places it safely out of harm's way. 

She nods to Toby. 

They dig their bruised fingers beneath the heavy iron grate. They heave. 

Metal shrieks against concrete. They toss the massive iron block aside. It slams against the wall. 

A blast of hot, copper-scented air hits their faces. It smells like old pennies and rotting meat. 

Pitch black inside. 

Toby pulls out his phone. Clicks on the flashlight. 

The harsh white beam reveals a suffocatingly tight, jagged sheet-metal throat. It stretches infinitely into the dark. 

Toby takes off his cheap, off-the-rack blazer. He folds it carefully, placing it perfectly parallel to his square of washers. 

He lies flat on his stomach. 

He leads with his phone, wedging his shoulders into the narrow gap. 

The metal immediately scrapes against his ribs. It’s a terrifyingly tight fit. 

Deep within the walls, the house GROANS. A wet, muscular contraction. 

The sheet metal around Toby visually buckles, compressing by a fraction of an inch. 

Toby freezes, his rapid breath echoing loudly in the tight space. 

VALERIE
Don't look at the walls. Just focus on the square footage ahead of you. Keep moving.

Toby kicks his scuffed dress shoes against the concrete, contorting his shoulders, forcing himself deeper into the claustrophobic abyss. 

Valerie kneels alone in the utility closet. 

She watches his shoes slide further and further into the dark. 

The shadows swallow his legs. Then his torso. 

Finally, only the faint, trembling glow of his phone screen remains, casting long, jagged shadows against the dust. 

The house lets out a low, satisfied rumble.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Alone in the closet, Valerie turns her attention to a glass emergency case mounted on the far wall. She wraps her blazer sleeve around her elbow and viciously shatters the pane, glass raining down onto the concrete floor. She reaches through the jagged shards and grips the heavy wooden handle of a red fire axe. She pulls it free, testing the weight in her hands, her posture transforming from a fleeing victim into an apex predator.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Arms the protagonist for the climax, externalizing her internal transformation. (+/-) Shifts from defenseless prey to armed and dangerous hunter. (><) The physical barrier of the glass case vs. Valerie's urgent need for a weapon.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - REINFORCED UTILITY CLOSET - CONTINUOUS

A heavy steel door SLAMS shut. The deadbolt SNAPS into place.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) presses her back against the cold metal. Her chest heaves. Beyond the steel, the MANSION EMITS a low, structural groan-- a wet, digestive rumble that vibrates through the concrete floor.

She looks down at her trembling hands. Her pristine, tailored designer blazer is smeared with ash and dark, unidentifiable grime.

Her thumb drops to her right cuff. She rubs the fabric. Hard. Obsessively scrubbing an invisible stain. Her breathing is ragged, bordering on hyperventilation. 

The utility closet is stark, gray, and windowless. No crown molding. No imported Italian marble. Just the raw, ugly guts of the property.

Valerie’s eyes dart across the shadows and lock onto the far wall.

Mounted between exposed copper pipes: a red emergency case. Thick safety glass. Inside rests a massive, heavy-duty FIRE AXE.

She pushes off the door and marches toward the case. 

She stops. Near the baseboard, a large WOLF SPIDER drags a crushed leg across the concrete, struggling to escape the violent vibrations of the room.

Valerie kneels. The house rumbles again, shaking dust from the ceiling. She ignores the earthquake. Gently, she slides a manicured fingernail under the spider, lifting it to the safety of a high, insulated electrical box.

VALERIE
(whispered)
High ground. Excellent resale value.

She stands, turning her attention back to the glass. Painted white letters stare back: IN CASE OF EMERGENCY, BREAK GLASS.

She searches the room. Empty shelves. No hammer. No wrench. 

She looks at her right arm. The bespoke Italian wool. She meticulously unbuttons the cuff, rolling the expensive fabric back, then wraps the thick sleeve tightly around her elbow to create a makeshift pad.

She squares her shoulders. Breathes in.

She swings her elbow into the center of the glass.

CRACK. The pane spiders, but holds firm.

Valerie grunts. She steps back, planting her expensive heels into the concrete. She swings again, viciously, putting her entire body weight into the strike.

SMASH.

A cascade of jagged glass rains down, pinging sharply against the floor. 

A massive, jagged shard remains lodged in the frame, directly in the way of the handle. Valerie doesn't flinch. 

She reaches her bare hand right through the dangerous opening. Her pale wrist hovers millimeters from the razor-sharp edge.

Her fingers wrap around the lacquered wooden handle. 

She rips the axe free, pulling it clear of the glass teeth.

The weapon is heavy. Brutal. She adjusts her grip, testing the balance in her palms. The crimson head of the axe catches the dim overhead light.

Valerie raises her chin. The obsessive twitch in her fingers vanishes. Her shoulders drop. Her posture straightens, locking into the rigid, terrifyingly calm stance of a closer. 

Outside the door, the house lets out another deafening, hungry ROAR. 

Valerie doesn't back away. She stares dead at the steel door.

VALERIE
Let's talk closing costs.

She steps toward the exit, dragging the heavy axe head along the concrete floor. The apex predator is ready to negotiate.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - HALLWAY OUTSIDE CLOSET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie steps up to the heavy oak door. She grips the deadbolt, takes one final, steadying breath, and throws the lock. She kicks the door open with her heel, stepping out into the blood-stained, shifting hallway. The house groans around her, but she ignores the shifting floorboards. Gripping the fire axe tightly, she marches forward into the belly of the beast to hunt down her predatory boss, Richard.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Crosses the threshold into the Act III execution phase. (+/-) Shifts from safe isolation to active, deadly confrontation. (><) Valerie's forward momentum vs. the house's treacherous environment and Richard's looming threat.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - HALLWAY OUTSIDE CLOSET - CONTINUOUS

The heavy oak door shudders. From the other side, a deep, structural GROAN reverberates, vibrating the brass deadbolt.

VALERIE (30s) stands inches from the wood. Her designer blazer is splattered with crimson. Her breath comes in ragged, uneven hitches.

She looks down at her left wrist. Her thumb aggressively rubs at the edge of her silk cuff. Scrubbing an invisible stain. Faster. Harder.

She stops. Stares at the heavy FIRE AXE gripped in her right hand.

Valerie tightens her grip on the fiberglass handle. The scrubbing stops. The icy, professional mask slams back into place.

She reaches out. Grips the deadbolt.

Click.

Valerie pivots, lifting her expensive Louboutin heel, and KICKS the door dead center.

The oak swings wide, slamming against the corridor wall.

Valerie steps out of the sanctuary.

The hallway is a living slaughterhouse. The pristine minimalist aesthetic--glass, steel, white oak--is warped. The walls pulse inward, breathing. Smears of fresh, arterial blood streak across the Venetian plaster.

Beneath her feet, the floorboards undulate. They ripple like the ribs of a starving hound.

Valerie doesn't flinch. She steps onto the shifting wood.

The house lets out a deafening SHRIEK of twisting metal and splintering timber.

A floorboard snaps upward like a bear trap.

Valerie swings the axe handle down, jamming it into the gap. The wood bites into the fiberglass, stalling the trap. She yanks her leg free, stepping over the hazard.

RICHARD (O.S.)
(echoing down the hall)
You can't outbid nature, Valerie!

Valerie freezes. Richard's voice bounces off the shifting angles of the hallway. It's impossible to tell which direction it's coming from.

RICHARD (O.S.)
It's a closed market now. Zero-sum. The apex predator takes the lion's share, and the weak get liquidated. The house has to eat, and I am not on the menu!

Valerie's jaw tightens. She tracks the voice, her eyes scanning the warped shadows.

VALERIE
(low, to herself)
Highly motivated seller.

She advances. The corridor narrows. The ceiling begins to lower, the recessed lighting flickering in a syncopated, dying heartbeat.

To her left, a smear of blood drips down a custom frosted-glass sconce.

Stuck in the viscous red fluid is a large, emerald-green JUNE BUG. Its legs kick frantically, drowning in the gore.

Valerie stops. The ceiling groans, dropping another inch. The walls press in. She doesn't have time.

She looks at the bug. Looks down the dark, narrowing hall.

She extends the broad side of the axe blade. Gently, with surgical precision, she slides the steel under the struggling insect.

She lifts it free from the blood, depositing it onto a dry, intact windowsill.

VALERIE
Highest and best offer, little guy.

The floor violently lurches.

Valerie is thrown against the right wall. The drywall is soft, warm to the touch. It yields like flesh.

The hallway is constricting. The house is actively trying to digest her in the corridor.

RICHARD (O.S.)
Did you really think you could manage an asset like this? You're just the middleman, Val. A disposable stepping stone.

That phrase hits her. Stepping stone. The ghost of her childhood eviction flashes in her eyes--the powerless, pathetic feeling of being small, of begging a landlord for mercy.

Valerie pushes off the fleshy wall. Her posture straightens. The fear evaporates, replaced by absolute, homicidal clarity.

VALERIE
I'm the exclusive listing agent, Richard.

She raises the fire axe high above her head.

VALERIE
And I'm authorizing a teardown.

She swings the axe with brutal, unhinged force into the pulsing wall.

CRACK.

A spray of thick, black sap--or blood--erupts from the gash in the drywall, splattering her face.

The house SCREAMS. A high-pitched, structural wail of shattering glass and bending steel.

The walls recoil, pulling back from the pain. The hallway widens, revealing a clear, straight path into the cavernous darkness of the master wing.

At the far end, a shadow moves. A man's silhouette, slipping behind a marble pillar.

Valerie grips the slick, sap-covered axe handle. She wipes the black sludge from her eyes, leaving a terrifying war paint streak across her face.

She marches forward, her heels clicking rhythmically on the blood-soaked floorboards.

Straight into the belly of the beast.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - HALLWAY / BASEMENT STAIRS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Picking up exactly where she left off, Valerie marches down the shifting, blood-stained hallway. She deliberately drags the heavy steel blade of her fire axe against the exposed wall pipes, creating a screeching, metallic ring to announce her presence and draw Richard out. Ignoring the groaning floorboards beneath her boots, she descends the stairs into the suffocating, humid depths of the basement.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes Valerie's transition from hunted prey to active predator, carrying the momentum of her decision directly into the monster's lair.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - HALLWAY / BASEMENT STAIRS - CONTINUOUS

The pristine, minimalist corridor now pulses with a sickly, rhythmic thrum. Veins of dark, coagulated blood spiderweb across the Italian marble walls. 

Valerie marches forward. Her tailored blazer is torn at the shoulder. Her face is splattered with gore. 

In her grip: a heavy steel fire axe. 

The floorboards groan beneath her boots. The architecture itself tilts, trying to throw her off balance. Valerie plants her feet, refusing to yield. 

She steps up to the exposed copper heating pipes running along the drywall. She presses the heavy steel blade of the axe against the metal. 

She walks. The blade grinds against the copper.

SCREEEEEECH.

A deafening, metallic shriek vibrates through the floor. She drags the axe continuously, carving a deep, jagged scar into the pristine house. 

VALERIE
(calling out)
Open-concept, Richard! Nowhere to hide! The sightlines in this property are unparalleled!

She reaches the basement door. It hangs off its hinges, breathing out a wave of suffocating, humid air. It smells like copper and digested rot. 

Valerie stops. Her breathing is ragged. 

Instinctively, her free hand twitches. Her thumb rubs frantically at the cuff of her ruined silk blouse. Scrubbing an invisible stain. Scrubbing, scrubbing. 

She looks down at her trembling, blood-soaked fingers. She forces her hand to stop. She tightens her grip on the axe handle. 

She kicks the broken door aside. It clatters down into the darkness. 

Valerie descends the wooden stairs. Each step emits a wet, spongy squelch. The basement is a labyrinth of shadows, concrete pillars, and massive HVAC ducts that throb like living arteries. 

Condensation drips from the low ceiling. SIZZLE. It hits the massive iron boiler. 

Valerie reaches the bottom. She levels the axe, stepping into the humid fog. 

RICHARD (O.S.)
You're depreciating the asset, Val.

Valerie freezes. Her eyes dart through the steam. 

RICHARD (O.S.)
(voice bouncing)
Nature demands a hierarchy. The apex predator doesn't throw a tantrum. It observes. It waits. 

VALERIE
Where are you, Richard? I want to show you the foundation. It's incredibly porous.

A shadow shifts behind the boiler. Valerie circles left, keeping her back to the weeping concrete wall. 

RICHARD (O.S.)
This is just a market correction. You got emotional. You let the property manage you. Hand me the axe, and I'll restructure your severance.

VALERIE
Look at the brutalist influence on that furnace housing. Sturdy. Unforgiving. They don't build them like this anymore.

From the gloom, RICHARD steps into the dim light of a flickering bulb. His bespoke suit is plastered to his skin with sweat. 

His sophisticated veneer is cracking. His eyes are wide, darting to the shifting, groaning shadows around them. 

RICHARD
(stepping closer)
Drop the axe, Valerie.

He closes the distance rapidly, violating her personal space. He leans in uncomfortably close, the smell of sour fear and expensive cologne radiating off him. 

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
You are a mid-level closer. You do not have the stomach for a hostile takeover. Drop it, or I will blacklist you from every brokerage on the West Coast. 

The floor beneath them shudders violently. A low, guttural rumble echoes from the deep crawlspace behind Richard. The house is waking up. It is hungry. 

Valerie looks at Richard. She doesn't back away. 

VALERIE
You're right. I'm just the middleman. 

Valerie swings the axe backward, smashing the heavy steel head directly into the main gas line on the wall. 

CRACK. 

Thick, noxious gas violently hisses into the humid air, spraying directly into Richard's face. 

Richard stumbles back, coughing, his eyes watering. He looks from the spewing pipe to Valerie's dead, icy stare. 

VALERIE
But the seller is highly motivated. 

She reaches into her pocket and pulls out a silver Zippo lighter. 

Richard's face drains of all color. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - BASEMENT FURNACE ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie steps into the cavernous furnace room, where the concrete walls literally pulse with a sickening, fleshy rhythm. Richard emerges from the shadows. His tailored suit is in tatters, and his eyes are completely glazed over with manic, insatiable greed as they lock onto the bulging velvet bag in Valerie's pocket. Completely ignoring the supernatural horror surrounding them, he lunges at Valerie with a rusted steel pipe.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Triggers the final physical confrontation between Valerie and her corporate greed, personified by her predatory boss.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - BASEMENT FURNACE ROOM - CONTINUOUS

The air is thick, suffocatingly hot, and smells of copper and ozone. 

This isn't a basement. It's a stomach.

The cavernous brutalist concrete walls EXPAND and CONTRACT. A sickening, wet rhythmic pulse. Thump. Squelch. Thump. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) stumbles down the iron spiral stairs. Her immaculate designer blazer is stained with god-knows-what. Her breath comes in ragged, shallow gasps. 

She furiously SCRUBS at an invisible spot on her frayed cuff.

A heavy velvet bag BULGES in her side blazer pocket. It clinks with the unmistakable sound of raw, uncut diamonds. 

A shadow detaches from the far corner. 

RICHARD STERLING (50s) steps into the flickering fluorescent light. His bespoke Tom Ford suit hangs in shredded, useless ribbons. Dust and dried viscera cake his hairline. 

Yet, Richard doesn’t even glance at the fleshy, pulsating walls. His eyes--glassy, manic, pupils blown wide--are dead-locked on Valerie’s pocket. 

VALERIE
Richard. The structural integrity is completely compromised. We are voiding the warranty just by standing here. We need to vacate the premises.

Richard tilts his head. A rusted steel pipe dangles from his right hand. 

RICHARD
The ecosystem always balances itself, Val. The apex feeds. The parasites get flushed out. It's the natural order of the market.

VALERIE
(scrubbing her cuff faster)
There is no market down here! Look at the foundation! It’s digesting us!

RICHARD
I built my firm from the studs up. Every tiny, delicate piece. Placed perfectly. Like a dollhouse. 

He takes a slow, deliberate step forward. The concrete floor beneath his wingtips softens, oozing a clear, acidic mucus. He doesn't notice. 

RICHARD
You don't get to play in my house and then steal the best furniture when you move out. 

VALERIE
I procured the listing. I fed the clients. You’re trespassing on an exclusive property.

RICHARD
I am the broker of record! 

He DRAGS the steel pipe along the floor. Screeeech. 

The furnace room responds. A low, gastric GURGLE echoes from the massive iron boiler. The walls pulse faster. Tightening. 

Valerie backs away, her heels sinking into the softening floor. 

VALERIE
I will transfer a referral fee. Twenty percent of the gross. You can walk away with your dignity. 

RICHARD
I don't want a referral fee. I want the deed. 

Richard LUNGES. 

He swings the rusted pipe like a baseball bat. Valerie ducks. 

The pipe SMASHES into a pulsating concrete pillar. The masonry splits open--not with dust, but with a spray of thick, black SLUDGE. 

The house SHRIEKS. A metallic grinding sound that shakes the floorboards above. 

Richard ignores the bleeding pillar. He drops the pipe, lunges forward, and slams Valerie against the sweating iron of the boiler. 

The heat sears through her blazer. 

Richard presses his full body weight against her, invading every inch of her personal space. His sophisticated facade shatters completely. 

RICHARD
(whispering through clenched teeth)
You’re nothing but a cheap, eviction-trash squatter. Hand over the bag. Or I will restructure your skull.

Valerie stares into his manic, glassy eyes. She feels the velvet bag digging into her hip. Her financial shield. Her absolute armor against the world. 

The boiler hisses behind her. A thick, yellow GAS pipe runs directly above Richard’s head, vibrating with intense pressure. 

Valerie stops scrubbing her cuff. Her icy veneer snaps back into place. 

VALERIE
You know what your problem is, Richard? 

Richard blinks, momentarily confused by her sudden calm. 

VALERIE
You never read the disclosures. 

Valerie drives her knee upward into his groin. 

Richard gasps, his grip loosening. 

Valerie shoves him backward. He slips on the acidic mucus, crashing hard into the bleeding concrete wall. 

The wall immediately WRAPS a fleshy, concrete fold over his arm. 

Richard screams, thrashing wildly. 

Valerie doesn't run for the stairs. She turns her gaze up to the vibrating yellow gas line. 

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - BASEMENT FURNACE ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie ducks beneath Richard's wild, murderous swing. She pivots, gripping the handle of the fire axe tightly, and drives the blunt, heavy steel end directly into Richard's kneecap. Bone cracks. Richard screams and crashes onto the blood-slicked grated floor. As his blood hits the metal, the mansion suddenly recognizes the immense concentration of greed in the room and violently reacts.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Valerie physically overpowers her human antagonist, but in doing so, inadvertently awakens the ultimate threat of the house.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MODERNIST MANSION - BASEMENT FURNACE ROOM - MOMENTS LATER

A heavy steel pipe slices through the humid air. 

VALERIE ducks just as the pipe shatters the pressure valve behind her head. Steam hisses violently into the cramped, subterranean space. 

RICHARD over-rotates from the missed swing. He recovers instantly, eyes black with predatory intent, lunging forward to close the gap.

Valerie doesn't retreat. She pivots on her heel, her hands choking up on the lacquered handle of a heavy FIRE AXE. 

She doesn't use the blade. She swings the blunt, heavy steel block at the back of the axe head like a sledgehammer.

It connects directly with Richard’s right kneecap. 

CRACK. 

The sound of the bone splintering echoes over the hissing steam. Richard’s mouth opens in a silent, agonizing gasp. 

He collapses, crashing hard onto the blood-slicked metal grating of the furnace room floor. 

Valerie stumbles back against a concrete pillar. Her chest heaves. Her thumb immediately goes to the cuff of her expensive silk blouse, obsessively scratching at an invisible stain.

RICHARD
(through clenched teeth)
You think... a temporary market fluctuation... changes the ecosystem?

He drags his upper body forward, his bloody fingers gripping the grating. Even with a shattered leg, he pulls himself closer, physically invading her space from the floor. 

RICHARD
Nature always corrects itself, Valerie. The apex predator doesn't starve. 

VALERIE
